entries.english: This is common practice elsewhere in the UK but Scottish justice works differently and courts have viewed publication of photos as potentially prejudicial.

entries.luganda: Omukuggu Pamela Ferguson owa setendekero ye Dundee awandiika “banamawulire balabika okutambula olugendo olukambwe bwebolesa ebifananyi nebirala ebyabazi bemisango.”
entries.english: Professor Pamela Ferguson of the University of Dundee notes "journalists do seem to be walking a dangerous line if publishing photos etc of suspects."

entries.luganda: W’offisi ya Crown, ekulila mu ku kwasaganya emisango, yakilambise bulungi eri banamawulire nti teli kwogere kulala kunakolebwa mpaka nga basaze wo.
entries.english: Crown Office, which is in overall charge of prosecutions, has indicated to journalists that no further comment will be made at least until indictment.

entries.luganda: Ekiwandiiko okusinziira ku lugambo, kijja kwogera ku ntalo z'ensalo, nga kuno Palestayini baagala esinziire ku nsalo oluvanyuma lw'olutalo lw'Amassekati g’Ebuvanjuba mu 1967.
entries.english: The document, according to the leak, will refer to the borders dispute, which Palestine wants based on the borders before the 1967 Mideast War.

entries.luganda: Ebyokuyiga ebirala ebyayisidwamu birimu ebiseera bya Jerusalem ebyomumaaso nga byankizo nyo eri amawanga gombi n’ensonga za Jordan Valley.
entries.english: Other topics covered reportedly include the future state of Jerusalem which is sacred to both nations and the Jordan Valley issue.

entries.luganda: Yisirayiri ebanja okubaawo kwa magye mu kiwonvu okumala emyaka kkumi kasita bateeka omukono ku ndagaano naye ate PA ekkiriziganya na kugalekawo okumala emyaka ettaano (5)
entries.english: Israel demands an ongoing military presence in the valley for ten years once an agreement is signed while the PA agrees to leave such presence only for five years.

entries.luganda: Ab’emundu mu kugezesa kw’engeri y’okukwatamu ebiwuka ebyonona enimiro bali balabwa labwa abakugu, era ebigenda mu maso nga bilabulwa n’enkola nga yekeneyebwa.
entries.english: Shooters in the supplementary pest control trial were to be closely supervised by rangers, as the trial was monitored and its effectiveness evaluated.

entries.luganda: Mu nkolagana ya NPWS ne ekibiina kya Sporting Shooters Association ekya Australia (NSW) Inc, baawandisiza banakyeewa, mu pulogulaamu eya abayizzi eya Sporting Shooters Association.
entries.english: In a partnership of NPWS and the Sporting Shooters Association of Australia (NSW) Inc, qualified volunteers were recruited, under the Sporting Shooters Association's hunting program.

entries.luganda: Okusinziira ku Mick O'Flynn, akola nga dayirekita wemboozi nebyobuwangwa naba NPWS, abakubi byebyasi abana abalondedwa okwetaba mukukuba amasasi okusooka bafunye ebiragiro byokwekuuma wamu nokutendekebwa.
entries.english: According to Mick O'Flynn, the Acting Director Park Conservation and Heritage with the NPWS, the four shooters selected for the first shooting operation received comprehensive safety and training instruction.

entries.luganda: Martelly yalayiza abakakiiko k'ebyokulonda abalala (CEP) abawerera ddala mwenda olunaku lweggulo.
entries.english: Martelly swore in a new Provisional Electoral Council (CEP) of nine members yesterday.

entries.luganda: Eno ye CEP ya Martelly eyokutano mu myaka ena
entries.english: It is Martelly's fifth CEP in four years.

entries.luganda: Omwezi oguwedde akakiko k’obwapulezidenti kalagira CEP eyaliko okulekulira nga ekimu ku bikolwa ebyolekeza ensi ku kulonda okujja.
entries.english: Last month a presidential commission recommended the prior CEP's resignation as part of a package of measures to move the country towards new elections.

entries.luganda: Kamisoni yali ekyokuddamu kya Martelly okusasanya obwegugungo obuwakanya obufuzi mu mwezi gwekumi.
entries.english: The commission was Martelly's response to widespread anti-regime protests that started in October.

entries.luganda: Ebiseera ebisinga – abegugunzi abakambwe batankuulwa okulemerwa okuteekawo okulonda , okumu okuva nga 2011.
entries.english: The sometimes-violent protests were triggered by failure to hold elections, some due since 2011.

entries.luganda: Twakafunayo abantu nga b’emulugunya ku buuma bwa Ipod nkaaga okukwata omuliro n’ebuletela abantu bana nga bakoseddwa era nga omuliro gukutte mu bifo mukaaga.
entries.english: Around 60 cases of malfunctioning iPods overheating have been reported, causing a total of six fires and leaving four people with minor burns.

entries.luganda: Minisitule y’ebyenfuna n’amakolero eya Japan (METI) yategezezza nti ebyuma ebyo byavuddeko obubenjje 27 nga buno bwekuusa ku byuma.
entries.english: Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) said that it had been aware of 27 accidents related to the devices.

entries.luganda: Wiiki ewedde, METI yalangirira nti apo yali etegezeza nti mu bintu ebikyamuse ebiwerera ddala 34, nga kino kampuni yakiyise “butaba nabwegendeereza.”
entries.english: Last week, METI announced that Apple had informed it of 34 additional overheating incidents, which the company called "non-serious."

entries.luganda: Ministule yazzeemu nga eyita ekya Apple okwongezayo alipota “mu butuufu kya nnaku.”
entries.english: The ministry responded by calling Apple's postponement of the report "truly regrettable."

entries.luganda: Musisi yakuba Mariana Ku 07:19 ez'okumakya, esawa ezobunansi (09:19 p.m. GMT Ku lw'okutaano).
entries.english: The eathquake struck Mariana at 07:19 a.m. local time (09:19 p.m. GMT Friday).

entries.luganda: Abomumambuka ga Mariana muwofiisi yebigwabitalaze yagambye nti tewabadewo buvune bwonna bwalopedwa mu nsi yonna.
entries.english: The Northern Marianas emergency management office said that there were no damages reported in the nation.

entries.luganda: Era nekifo ekikola ku kulabula kwomuyaga gwa Pacific kyagamba nti tewali kilaga muyaga.
entries.english: Also the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center said that there was no Tsunami indication.

entries.luganda: Eyali owapolisi ya Filipino akuumye abalambuzi ba Hong Kong mu buwambe nga awamba bbaasi yaabwe mu manila ekibuga ekikulu ekya Filipayini.
entries.english: A former Filipino policeman has kept Hong Kong tourists hostage by hijacking their bus in Manila, the capital of the Philippines.

entries.luganda: Ronaldo Mendoza yakubye emundu ye eya M16 eri omulambuzi.
entries.english: Rolando Mendoza fired his M16 rifle at the tourists.

entries.luganda: Abawambe abawerako be banunuddwa ate ekitono nnyo mukaaga be bakakasiddwa nti baffu.
entries.english: Several hostages have been rescued and least six have been confirmed dead so far.

entries.luganda: Abawambe mukaaga nga mulimu abaana n’abakadde bateredwa mangu nga abakubi bebifananyi aba Filipino.
entries.english: Six hostages, including the children and elderly, were released early, as were the Filipino photographers.

entries.luganda: Abakubi b’ebifananyi oluvanyuma bediza ekifo ky’anamukadde engeri jeyali yetaaga kabuyonjo. Mendoza yakubibwa masassi.
entries.english: The photographers later took the place of an aged lady as she needed the lavatory. Mendoza was gunned down.

entries.luganda: Liggins yagoberela mu bigere bya kitaawe nayingira mu mulimu gwedagala.
entries.english: Liggins followed in his father’s footsteps and entered a career in medicine.

entries.luganda: Yeatenddekebwa ng’omusawo w’abakyala natandika okolera mu dwaliro ly’a bakyala mu Auckland mu mwaka gwa 1959
entries.english: He trained as an obstetrician and began to work at the Auckland's National Women's Hospital in 1959.

entries.luganda: Bwe yali akola ku ddwaliro, Liggins mu biseera bye ebye ddembe yatandikka okunoonyereza ku bissa ebijja nga omukazi tannatuka kuzaala
entries.english: While he was working at the hospital Liggins began to investigate premature labor during his spare time.

entries.luganda: Okunonyeleza kwe kwalaga nti okuwa omukyala eddagala kwali kwongeza obwangu bwamawugwe gw’omwana okukula.
entries.english: His research showed that if a hormone was administered it would speed up the baby's foetal lung maturation.

entries.luganda: Xinhua yagambye nti abanonyerezi b’agavumenti bazuudde ‘obusanduke bubiri obudugavu’ abakwaata eby’omubanga ku lw’okusatu.
entries.english: Xinhua reported that government investigators recovered two 'black box' flight recorders on Wednesday.

entries.luganda: Banne bwe bazanya ogw'ebigwo nabo bamukungubagidde.
entries.english: Fellow wrestlers also paid tribute to Luna.

entries.luganda: Tommy Dreamer yagamba “Luna yemumbejja eyasooka asinga. Maneja wange asooka. Luna yaffa kusaawa mukaaga. Kyamjawulo. Mukazi wamaanyi.”
entries.english: Tommy Dreamer said "Luna was the first Queen of Extreme. My first manager. Luna passed away on the night of two moons. Pretty unique just like her. Strong woman."

entries.luganda: Dustin “Goldust” Runnels yagamba nti “Luna yali mupangulukuffu nga nze …oba n’okunsingako…mwagale atte ojja musubwa…kansubire ali mukifo kirungi.”
entries.english: Dustin "Goldust" Runnels commented that "Luna was as freaky as me...maybe even more...love her and will miss her...hopefully she's in a better place."

entries.luganda: Kubantu 1400 abakebuuziibwaako ng’empaka zokulonda omukulembeze tezinabaawo, abatawagira Australia okufuna obwetwaaze beyongedde nebitundu 8 ku 100 okuva mu 2008.
entries.english: Out of 1,400 people polled prior to the 2010 federal election, those who oppose Australia becoming a republic grew by 8 per cent since 2008.

entries.luganda: Alabirila Katikiro Julia Gillard agamba nti mu biseera bya kakuyege wa 2010 ow’ebyokulondanti yali akiriza nti Autralia erina okufuuka lipabuliiki kunkomelero y’ekisanja kya Nabagereka Elizabeth II.
entries.english: Caretaker Prime Minister Julia Gillard claimed during the campaign of the 2010 federal election that she believed Australia should become a republic at the end of Queen Elizabeth II's reign.

entries.luganda: Ebitundu 34 ku buli kikumi kwabo abantu abesimbyewo bakaanya ku ndowooza nti obufuzi bwa federo obwa Nnaabakyala Elizabeti II bwebuba busembayo mu Australia.
entries.english: 34 per cent of those in the poll share this view, wanting Queen Elizabeth II to be Australia's last monarch.

entries.luganda: Kunkomelero y’akalulu, 29 ku kikumi kubbo abanonyereza Australia elina okufuuka lipabulika mubwangu dala, nga ate 31 ku kikumi bakiriza nti Autralia telina kufuuka lipaabuliika.
entries.english: At the extremes of the poll, 29 per cent of those surveyed believe Australia should become a republic as soon as possible, while 31 per cent believe Australia should never become a republic.

entries.luganda: Eyawangula omudaali gwa zawabu mu Olympics yabadde agenda kuwuga mu kuzanyamu n’akafubutuko ak’ebitundutundu ebisatu mu mizanyo gy’obulamu obwabulijjo, naye olw’okwemulugunyakwe amaanyige bagatankana.
entries.english: The Olympic gold medalist was due to swim in the 100m and 200m freestyle and in three relays at the Commonwealth Games, but due to his complaints his fitness has been in doubt.

entries.luganda: Tasobode kutwala dagala eryetagisa okuwonya obulumi nga bwebaweredwa mumizanyo.
entries.english: He has been unable to take the drugs needed to overcome his pain as they are banned from the Games.

entries.luganda: Curtis Cooper, omukenkufu mu kubala n’ebyatekinologiya ku setendekero ly’omumasekati ga Missouri, azudde omuwendo ogusinga obunene okutuusa kati mu gatoya nga 25.
entries.english: Curtis Cooper, a mathematician and computer science professor at the University of Central Missouri, has discovered the largest known prime number to date on January 25.

entries.luganda: Abantu bangi bakakasa ebyazuulibwa nga bakozesa haadiweya ne sofutiweya mu ntandikwa y’ogwokubiri era nekirangililwa kulw’okubiri.
entries.english: Several people verified the discovery using different hardware and software by the beginning of February and it was announced on Tuesday.

entries.luganda: Ziyinza okuba zezali ensibuko za mazzi ku nsi awamu ne kasasilo okuvva mu bigimusa okuwanilila obulamu
entries.english: Comets may possibly have been a source of water delivery to the earth along with organic matter that can form proteins and support life.

entries.luganda: Bannasayansi basuubira okutegeera engeri ensi bwekolebwa okuva comets bwe zatomeregana emyaka mingi edda.
entries.english: Scientists hope to understand how planets form, especially how the Earth formed, since comets collided with the Earth long ago.

entries.luganda: Cuomo, 53, yatandika obufuzi bwe omwaka guno nga gutandiika n'asayininga eteeka omwezi oguwedde nga akiriza obufumbo bwabasiyazi.
entries.english: Cuomo, 53, began his governorship earlier this year and signed a bill last month legalizing same-sex marriage.

entries.luganda: Engambo yaziyise “lugambo lwa byabufuzi nakwesiruwaza”.
entries.english: He referred to the rumors as "political chatter and silliness".

entries.luganda: Ateeberezebwa okwesimbawo kubwa pulezidenti mu 2016.
entries.english: He is speculated to make a run for president in 2016.

entries.luganda: NextGen ye nkola FAA gyegamba ej'okuganya enyonyi okubuuka engeendo enyimpi netaasa obukadde bwa gallons z'amafuta buli mwaaka nesala emission za kaboni.
entries.english: NextGen is a system the FAA claims would allow aircraft to fly shorter routes and save millions of gallons of fuel each year and cut carbon emissions.

entries.luganda: Ekozesa tekinologiya okuva ku milongooti ngolese enkola enkadde eyomuttaka okusobola okukiriza abakendeeza omujuzo okusonga enwe mu nnyonyi ngabakozesa okukatiriza era bawe ababuusa enyonyi obubaka obutuufu.
entries.english: It uses satellite-based technology as opposed to older ground-radar-based technology to allow air traffic controllers to pinpoint aircraft with greater precision and give pilots more accurate information.

entries.luganda: Teri ntambula ndala etekedwa wo atte gaali zomuka wansi mu ttaka tezijja kuyimirira ku Wembule, okuyimiriza emotoka, n’okuvuga nga bwoyimirira tebijja kuberaawo kuttaka.
entries.english: No extra transport is being put on and overground trains will not stop at Wembley, and car parking and park-and-ride facilities are unavailable at the ground.

entries.luganda: Okutya okubulwa ebyentambula kwaleesewo okulowooza nti omuzannyo gwandikakibwa okuzanyibwa emabega w'enzigi enzigale ng'abawagizi ba ttiimu tebaliwo.
entries.english: Fears of lack of transportation raised the possibility that the game would be forced to play behind closed doors without the team's supporters.

entries.luganda: Okusoma okwawandiikidwa kulwokuna mu kitabo kya saayansi kwalaze nti ngebinyonyi ebipya bitondebwa ku buzinga bwa Ecuadorean Galápagos.
entries.english: A study published on Thursday in the journal Science reported on formation of a new bird species on the Ecuadorean Galápagos Islands.

entries.luganda: Abanoonyereza okuva mu setendekero wa Princeton mu United States ne sentendekero wa Uppsalamu Buswiidi gayogedde ku bikka ebipya ebilabiseewo mu Mirembe ebiri, wadde nga enkola eno ekirizibwaamu okutwaala akabanga akanene, olw’okuzaala wakati w’ekika kya Darwin Finch, Geospiza fortes, ne cactus finch atambula, Geospiza conirostris.
entries.english: Researchers from Princeton University in the United States and Uppsala University in Sweden reported the new species evolved in just two generations, though this process had been believed to take much longer, due to breeding between an endemic Darwin finch, Geospiza fortes, and the immigrant cactus finch, Geospiza conirostris.

entries.luganda: Zaabu asobola okukolebwaamu sheepu ezenjawulo. Asobola okuzinga zingibwaamu sheepu entonotono.
entries.english: Gold may be worked into all sorts of shapes. It can be rolled into tiny shapes.

entries.luganda: Kisobola okusikibwa ne kifulibwa olukomo, kye basobola okugongobala ne kisibwa. Kisoboka okukikuba oba okukizinga mu mabaati.
entries.english: It can be pulled into thin wire, which can be twisted and plaited. It can be hammered or rolled into sheets.

entries.luganda: Kisobola okukolebwa nga kitono, era nga kigatibwa ku kyuma ekirala. Kisobola okukolebwa kitono nti olumu kyakozesebwa okutimba ebifaananyi ebikolebwa n'engalo mu butabo obuyitibwa “ebiwandiiko ebisibiddwa”.
entries.english: It can be made very thin, and stuck onto other metal. It can be made so thin that it was sometimes used to decorate the hand-painted pictures in books called "illuminated manuscripts".

entries.luganda: Kinno bakiyita chemicals Ph. Osobola okukola ekiraga nga okozesa omubisi gwemboga omumyuufu.
entries.english: This is called a chemical's pH. You can make an indicator using red cabbage juice.

entries.luganda: Omukamulo gw'emboga gukyuusa langi okusinzila Ku ngeri asidi oba (alkaline) kemiko kyeri.
entries.english: The cabbage juice changes color depending on how acidic or basic (alkaline) the chemical is.

entries.luganda: Ph eragibwa n’omuwendo ogwa Hydrogen (the H in pH) ions oguba gugezesedwa mu kemiko.
entries.english: The pH level is indicated by the amount of Hydrogen (the H in pH) ions in the tested chemical.

entries.luganda: Vatomu ayodologyeni bwe bukonta obwa zijibwako amasanyalaze gaazo (kyokka akaziba ka ayodologyeni kalimu akakonta kamu ne akasanyaleze kamu).
entries.english: Hydrogen ions are protons that had their electrons stripped off them (since Hydrogen atoms consist of one proton and one electron).

entries.luganda: Swirl pawuda enkalu ebbiri wamu era n'engalo enyonjo nga mbisi, zikamulire mu mupiira.
entries.english: Swirl the two dry powders together and then, with clean wet hands, squeeze them into a ball.

entries.luganda: Ebbugumu mu ngalo zo zijja kwegatta n’obuwuka obwokungulu obuli ebweru, ekiyinza okubeera nga kisesa era bikole n’ekisusunku.
entries.english: The moisture on your hands will react with the outer layers, which will feel funny and form a sort of shell.

entries.luganda: Ebibuga bya Harappa ne Mohenjo-daro byalina kabuyonja y’amazzi kumpi mubuli nyumba nga ekwasaganyizidwa kumikutu jasuwegi ejomulembe.
entries.english: The cities of Harappa and Mohenjo-daro had a flush toilet in almost every house, attached to a sophisticated sewage system.

entries.luganda: Ebisigalira bye mikutu gya kazambi bisangidwa mu mayumba g’omu bibuga bya Minoan ekya Crete ne Santorin mu Buyonaani.
entries.english: Remains of sewage systems have been found in the houses of the Minoan cities of Crete and Santorini in Greece.

entries.luganda: Mu Misiri ekikadde nayo waliyo kabuyonjo, Persia ne China. Mukukulakulana mu Roma, kabuyonjo oluusi zali kitundu kubinabiro byolukale abaami n’abakyaala bonna jebakozesanga mu kampuni awamu.
entries.english: There were also toilets in ancient Egypt, Persia and China. In Roman civilization, toilets were sometimes part of public bath houses where men and women were together in mixed company.

entries.luganda: Bwokubila omuntu ali mayiro nkumu ewala, obela okozzesa setilayiti.
entries.english: When you call someone who is thousands of miles away, you are using a satellite.

entries.luganda: Emilongooti jomubanga jifuna amaloboozi negimala negigaza, mubwangu dala.
entries.english: The satellite in space gets the call and then reflects it back down, almost instantly.

entries.luganda: Sigino z'empewo zasindikibwa mu bbanga n'ekizungirizi. Bannassaayansi bakozesa ebyuma bimulengera wala mu bbanga kubanga embeera y'ensi etataganya ekitangala n'okulaba.
entries.english: The satellite was sent into space by a rocket. Scientists use telescopes in space because the Earth’s atmosphere distorts some of our light and view.

entries.luganda: Kyetagisa ekikompola ekinene ekyebigere nga 100 waggulu okuteekayo omukutu oba ekizungirizi mu bbanga.
entries.english: It takes a giant rocket over a 100 feet high to put a satellite or telescope in space.

entries.luganda: Ekyesuubo kikyusiza ensi mu ngeri ezitagambika.Ekintu ekikyasinze ekyesuubo kino kyekikoze kwekuleetawo entambula enyangu ate ngewenyuka buweewo.
entries.english: The wheel has changed the world in incredible ways. The biggest thing that the wheel has done for us is given us much easier and faster transportation.

entries.luganda: Kituletedde eggaali y’omukka, emotoka, nebirala bingi ebikozesebwae mubyentambula.
entries.english: It has brought us the train, the car, and many other transportation devices.

entries.luganda: Wansi wazo waliyo kappa ezitali nene nnyo ezirya obusolo okutandikira ku bumyu ku butulege ne nkobe.
entries.english: Under them are more medium sized cats that eat medium sized prey ranging from rabbits to antelopes and deer.

entries.luganda: Oluvanyuma, waliyo bu kappa bungi obutono(okuli bu kappa obwokulunda obute) obulya obutono emirundi minji obusolo obutono nga ebiwuka , emese , eminya , n’ebinyonyi.
entries.english: Finally, there are many small cats (including loose pet cats) that eat the far more numerous small prey like insects, rodents, lizards, and birds.

entries.luganda: Ekyama ekili mu byebafuna kwe kubera nga bali wamu, balina omulimu omulunjji buli omu gwebakozessa mukifo kyokulwanagana
entries.english: The secret to their success is the concept of the niche, a special job each cat holds that keeps it from competing with others.

entries.luganda: Empologoma ze kkapa ezikyasinze okubeera awamu nga zibeera mu bibinja ebinene ebiyitibwa pulayidi.
entries.english: Lions are the most social cats, living in large groups called prides.

entries.luganda: Amasanga gakolebwa ensolo ensajja ezoluganda wakati weemu ne ssatu wamu nensolo enkazi n'ento endala makumi asatu.
entries.english: Prides are made up of one to three related adult males, along with as many as thirty females and cubs.

entries.luganda: Ebitonde ebikazi bitera okukwatagana, olw'okubeera amaka amanene ag'aboluganda n’abaana.
entries.english: The females are usually closely related to each other, being a large family of sisters and daughters.

entries.luganda: Okwemanya kw’engo kulinga ekibinja ky’ebibbe oba embwa, ensolo enzewunyisa nti zefanyanganya ne engo (naye si kappa ziri endala enene) mu neeyissa, era zabulabe nnyo eri byezilya.
entries.english: Lion prides act much like packs of wolves or dogs, animals surprisingly similar to lions (but not other big cats) in behavior, and also very deadly to their prey.

entries.luganda: Omudusi eyewumbye esobola okulinya (newankubadde si bulungi), okuwuga, okufuna distance empanvu era n’esiika emirundi mingi ge manyi g’omuntu omumalirivu.
entries.english: A well rounded athlete, the tiger can climb (though not well), swim, leap great distances and pull with five times the force of a strong human.

entries.luganda: Tayiga eri mukibinja kyekimu mu(Genus Panthera) nga empologoma, engo , ne jagwa. Zino kappa enya zokka zezisobola okuwuluguma.
entries.english: The tiger is in the same group (Genus Panthera) as lions, leopards, and jaguars. These four cats are the only ones who can roar.

entries.luganda: Oluyogaano lwengo teluwulikika ngomusinde gwempologoma naye kiba nga sentensi yebigambo ebiwoganidwa.
entries.english: The tiger's roar is not like the full-voiced roar of a lion, but more like a sentence of snarly, shouted words.

entries.luganda: Ocelots zaagala okulya obusolo obutono. Zijja kukwata enkima, emisota, ebiwuka, ebinyonyi bwe ziba zisobola. Ebisolo byonna bye ziyigga bizisinga obutono nga bwe kiri.
entries.english: Ocelots like to eat small animals. They will catch monkeys, snakes, rodents and birds if they can. Almost all of the animals that the ocelot hunts are far smaller than it is.

entries.luganda: Banasayansi balowooza nti kappa ekika kya ocelot zigoberera n’ezisanga ekyokulya nga ziwunyiriza wezayise kuttaka.
entries.english: Scientists think that ocelots follow and find animals to eat (prey) by smell, sniffing for where they've been on the ground.

entries.luganda: Biraba burungi nnyo mu nzikiza nga bikozesa ebitangaala by'ekiro, ate busooba. Kappa ekika kya Oceloti zikwaata ekyokulya nga zikyuusa langi yaazo okufaanana ne weziri awo nno, ne zibuukira ekyokulya.
entries.english: They can see very well in the dark with night vision, and move very stealthily, too. Ocelots hunt their prey by blending in with their surroundings then pouncing on their prey.

entries.luganda: Akabinja kebintu ebissa akatono(ebintu ebitono mu muwendo) bwekaawulibwa okuva ku kituundu ekirala( nga bwesuka ensozi nemigga, oba bwekagenda ku kazinga akapya nga tekasobola kudda)ebiseera ebisinga kaja kwesaanga nga kali mumbeera eyawukana kweeyo gyekaalimu.
entries.english: When a small group of living things (a small population) gets separated from the main population that they came from (like if they move over a mountain range or a river, or if they move to a new island so that they can't easily move back) they will often find themselves in a different environment than they were in before.

entries.luganda: Obutonde obupya buno bulina ebikozesebwa ebyenjawulo nebibyawula ebyenjawulo oba ebikolebwa okubeera abavuganya abamaanyi nokusinga kwabo bebali betaaze ku ntandikwa.
entries.english: This new environment has different resources and different competitors, so the new population will need different features or adaptations to be a strong competitor than what they had needed before.

entries.luganda: Abantu abasoka n’embera gyebalimu tebyakyuka nyo, bakyetaga obuyambi bungi okutelela.
entries.english: The original population hasn't changed at all, they still need the same adaptations as before.

entries.luganda: Okumala akaseera, nga abantu abapya batandika okumanyiira embeera y'ekitundu kyaabwe abapya, batandika okulabika mpola mpola nga abantu abalala.
entries.english: Over time, as the new population begins to adapt to their new environment, they start to look less and less like the other population.

entries.luganda: Oluvannyuma nga wayise ebikumi n’obukadde bw’emyaaka, amawaanga abiri gajja kwawukanira ddala mu nfaanana nti tegasobola nakuyitibwa nti gali ekitonde kyekimu.
entries.english: Eventually, after thousands or even millions of years, the two populations will look so different that they can't be called the same species.

entries.luganda: Kino ekintu tukiyita kwawulamu, nga kino kitegeeza kugunjaawo bintu bya njawulo. Okweyawuzamu kintu kitebeereka era kintu kya mugaso nnyo mu butonde.
entries.english: We call this process speciation, which just means the formation of new species. Speciation is an unavoidable consequence and a very important part of evolution.

entries.luganda: Ebimera bikola oxygen abantu gwe bassa, ate ne binuusa carbon-dioxide abantu gwe batassa (kwegamba, okuyisa omukka).
entries.english: Plants make oxygen which humans breathe, and they take in carbon-dioxide which humans exhale (that is, breathe out).

entries.luganda: Ebimera bikola emere yaabyo okuva mumusana. Biwa n‘ekisiikirize.
entries.english: Plants make their food from the sun by photosynthesis. They also provide shade.

entries.luganda: Tuzimba amayumba ngatukozesa birime ate n’engoye zaffe tuzikola mubirime. Emere gyetusiinga okulya eba yabirime. Awatali birime, ensolo tezisobola kubaawo.
entries.english: We make our houses from plants and make clothes from plants. Most foods that we eat are plants. Without plants, animals could not survive.

entries.luganda: Mosasaurus yeyali asinga okutiisa ebisolo ebilala era tewali kyeyatya okujjako bane ba mosasaurs.
entries.english: Mosasaurus was the apex predator of its time, so it feared nothing, except other mosasaurs.

entries.luganda: Emba zaakyo empaanvu zeetooloddwa amannyo amooji agasukka mu nsanvu (70), nga kwootadde n’amannyo ag’ennyongeza waggulu mukamwa, kitegeza tewaali kuwona eri kyonna ekyaasala mu kkubo lyaayo
entries.english: Its long jaws were studded with more than 70 razor-sharp teeth, along with an extra set in the roof of its mouth, meaning that there was no escape for anything that crossed its path.

entries.luganda: Tetumanyideddala, naye kyandibadde ne nimi nyingi nga wuma. Mubyekyalikirya mwalimu emere yokumazzi, ebyenyanja ebinene, zi mosasaurs endala, kyandiba nokuba nga kyali kirya n’abantu.
entries.english: We don't know for sure, but it may have had a forked tongue. Its diet included turtles, large fish, other mosasaurs, and it may even have been a cannibal.

entries.luganda: Kyaalumba ekintu kyona ekya yingira mu mazzi; n’ekikulekule nga kyebayita T.rex tekyandi kisobode.
entries.english: It also attacked anything that entered the water; even a giant dinosaur such as T. rex would be no match for it.

entries.luganda: Nga emmere yabwe esinga simpya gyetuli, Abaruumi balina omugabo gwaabwe ogwebintu byemikolo gyaabwe ebitali bya bulijjo, nga mulimu embizi y'omunsiko, ngaali, amakovu, n'ekika ky'emesse ekiyitibwa omusonso.
entries.english: While most of their food would be familiar to us, Romans did have their share of strange or unusual feast items, including wild boar, peacock, snails, and a type of rodent called a dormouse

entries.luganda: Enjawulo endala eyaliwo yali nti abantu abanaku n’abakyala baalyanga emere nga batudde mu ntebe, abasajja abagagga bayagalanga nyo okulya ebijjulo nga bagalamidde buli omu ku ludda lwe.
entries.english: Another difference was that while the poor people and the woman ate their food while sitting in chairs, the rich men liked to have banquets together where they would lounge on their sides while they ate their meals.

entries.luganda: Emmere y’Abaruumi abedda tesobola kuba nga yalimu emmere eyaleetebwa mu bulaaya okuva mu Amerika oba mu Eezia mu byaasa ebyaddirira.
entries.english: Ancient Roman meals couldn't have included foods that came to Europe from America or from Asia in later centuries.

entries.luganda: Okugeza, tebaalina kasooli, ob’ennyaanya, oba lumonde newankubadde kkooko era teri Muruumi mukadde yali alidde ku sekkokkko.
entries.english: For instance, they didn't have corn, nor tomatoes, nor potatoes, nor cocoa, and no ancient Roman ever tasted a turkey.

entries.luganda: Aba Babilooni baazimbira buli katonda waabwe essinzizo Eryatebeerezebwa okubeera amaka ga katonda oyo.
entries.english: The Babylonians built each of their gods a primary temple that was considered the home of the god.

entries.luganda: Abantu baaletanga ebiweebwayo eri bukatonda n’abakulembeze bagezangako okufa ku byetaago by’obukatonda nga bayita mu mikolo n’ebikujjuko.
entries.english: People would bring sacrifices to the gods and the priests would try to attend to the needs of the gods through ceremonies and festivals.

entries.luganda: Buli kkanisa yalina olujja lwayo oluggule era munda mu sinziizo mwalimu akafo akeekusifu abasumba mwebayingiranga.
entries.english: Each temple had an open temple courtyard and then an inner sanctuary that only the priests could enter.

entries.luganda: Olumu, ebibumbe ebyakula nga zi piramiddi nga biyitibwa ziggurats byazimbibwa nga byakugatibwa ku makanisa
entries.english: Sometimes special pyramid shaped towers, called ziggurats, were built to be a part of the temples.

entries.luganda: Entiko y'omunaala yali kifo kyanjawulo eky'abakatonda.
entries.english: The top of the tower was special sanctuary for the god.

entries.luganda: Mu budde bw’omusana eri mu buwarabu, enyumba tebera yamugaso.
entries.english: In the warm climate of the Middle East, the house was not so important.

entries.luganda: Obulamu obusinga obwa ab’enju ya bebbulaniya bwatuukawo mu mpewo.
entries.english: Most of the life of the Hebrew family happened in the open air.

entries.luganda: Abakyala bakola okufumba mu kibangirizi; ebiterekelo by’emere byali bigule nga counter ku ngudo. Amayinja gakozesebwa mu kuzimba mayumba.
entries.english: Women did the cooking in the yard; stores were just open counters looking into the street. Stone was used for building houses.

entries.luganda: Tewaliwo bibira bigazi ku ttaka lye Cana, noolwekyo embawo zali za buseere nyo.
entries.english: There were no large forests in the land of Canaan, so wood was extremely expensive.

entries.luganda: Greenland yabeeramu abantu abesudde. Mu Norse sagas bagamba nti Erik the Red yagobwa okuva mu Iceland lwa butemu, era bw'aba atambula okweyongera mu bukiika kkono, yasanga Greenland nagituuma Greenland.
entries.english: Greenland was settled sparsely. In the Norse sagas they say that Erik the Red was exiled from Iceland for murder, and when travelling further west, found Greenland and named it Greenland.

entries.luganda: Okujja ko ekoyo kye yazula, abe’kika kya Eskimo bali nga webabela ebisera ebyo.
entries.english: But regardless of his discovery, Eskimo tribes were already living there at the time.

entries.luganda: Wadde nga ensi zino zili bumu nga eza Scandinavia, waliwo enjawulo mu bantu, ba kabaka, empisa, n’ebyafayo wakati mu Denimaka, Buswidi, Norway ne Iceland.
entries.english: Though each country was 'Scandinavian', there were many differences between the people, kings, customs and history of Denmark, Sweden, Norway and Iceland.

entries.luganda: Bw'oba ng’olabyeko filimu eyitibwa ekyomuwendo ky'ensi, oyinza okulowooza nti mapu bagikubira ku lunaku lwe balangirira amefuga.
entries.english: If you have watched the movie National Treasure, you may think a treasure map was written on the back of the Declaration of Independence.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde, ekyo sikituffu. Wadde nga waliwo ebyawandikidwa emabegga, eyo si mappu yabyabugagga.
entries.english: However, that is not true. Although there is something written on the back of the document, it is not a treasure map.

entries.luganda: Ebyawandikibwa emabega g’ekiwandiiko eky’Okwatula Ameefuga, byali ebigambo “Okwatuula Ameefuga Agaasangwa wo nga 4 Kasambula 1776”. Ebigambo binno byawandikibwa kifuulannenge mu maserengeto g’ekiwandiiko.
entries.english: Written on the back of the Declaration of Independence were the words "Original Declaration of Independence dated 4th July 1776". The text appears on the bottom of the document, upside down.

entries.luganda: Wadde nga tewali n’omu amanyi yakiwandiika, kimanyikidwa nti mubiseera byaakyo ebyasooka, ekiwandiiko ekyasinga obunene (kiweza yinki 29¾ ku yinki 24½) zazingibwaako neziterekebwa.
entries.english: While no one knows for certain who wrote it, it is known that early in its life, the large parchment document (it measures 29¾ inches by 24½ inches) was rolled up for storage.

entries.luganda: Nolwekyo, kitebelezebwa nti obubonero bwayongerwako nga eky'okulamba.
entries.english: So, it is likely that the notation was added simply as a label.

entries.luganda: Olunaku lwe D-Day olwokugwa n'entalo ezaddirira zanunula amabuka okuva Bufaransa, naye mu maserengeta baali tebetaaya.
entries.english: The D-Day landings and the following battles had freed the north of France, but the south still wasn't free.

entries.luganda: Kyasalibwawo aba French ba “Vichy”. Bano baali bantu bafalansa abali bataddewo eddembe n’abagirimaani mu 1940 era ngabaakola nababalumba mukifo kyokubalwanyisa.
entries.english: It was ruled by the "Vichy" French. These were French people who had made peace with the Germans in 1940 and worked with the invaders instead of fighting them.

entries.luganda: Nga 15 Ogwomunaana 1940, abalabe balumba amaserengeta ga bufulansa, olulumba lwayitibwa “kikwekweto Dragoon”.
entries.english: On 15 August 1940, the Allies invaded southern France, the invasion was called "Operation Dragoon".

entries.luganda: Mu ssabbiiti bbiri, amagye g’Abamerika ne Bufaransa bataasizza amaserengeta ga Bufaransa nga kati gadda mu Bugirimani.
entries.english: In just two weeks the Americans and Free French forces had liberated southern France and were turning towards Germany.

entries.luganda: Enkulakulana kyabuwangwa ekigabanwa akabinja akanene abakola era ababeera awamu mu kitundu.
entries.english: A civilization is a singular culture shared by a significant large group of people who live and work co-operatively, a society.

entries.luganda: Ekigambo civilization kiva mu ba Latino civils, okitegeeza civil, kya luganda ne Latin civis, ekitegeeza abatuuze ne civitas ekitegeeza ekibuga era ekyo nakyo kiraga obunene bw’ekyalo.
entries.english: The word civilization comes from the Latin civilis, meaning civil, related to the Latin civis, meaning citizen, and civitas, meaning city or city-state, and that also somehow defines the size of the society.

entries.luganda: Ebibuga byebikulakulanya ensi. Empisa enungi zitulagira nti tugabane amagezi eri abanatudira mu bigere. Yengeri yokugabana ebyafayo byaffe oburungi.
entries.english: City-states are the precursors of nations. A civilizational culture implies the passing on of knowledge across several generations, a lingering cultural footprint and fair dissemination.

entries.luganda: Obuwangwa obutono butera okusanawo nga tebulese bujulizi bwa mikululo ne bulemwa okulabwa nga enkulakulana eyanamaddala.
entries.english: Minor cultures often vanish without leaving relevant historic evidence and fail to be recognized as proper civilizations.

entries.luganda: Mu lutalo lw'enkyukakyuka amawanga ekkumi nasatu gasooka kukola gavumenti eya wakati ennafu nga Congress yokka yerimu wansi wa Articles za confederation.
entries.english: During the Revolutionary War, the thirteen states first formed a weak central government—with the Congress being its only component—under the Articles of Confederation.

entries.luganda: Congress yabulwa amanyi gona okugereka emisolo era lwakuba tewaliwo lukiiko lwa ggwanga olufuzi oba akakiiko akalamuzi neyesigama kumanyi g’eggwanga agatakolagananga okusobola okukwasaganya ebikolwa byonna.
entries.english: Congress lacked any power to impose taxes, and, because there was no national executive or judiciary, it relied on state authorities, who were often uncooperative, to enforce all its acts.

entries.luganda: Tebalina buyinza okuvvuula amateeka gemisolo n’emiwendo wakati w’amawanga.
entries.english: It also had no authority to override tax laws and tariffs between states.

entries.luganda: Ebiwandiiko byali byetaaga okukakasibwa okuva eri omuntu atamanyidwa okuva mu gavumenti zona nga tebinaba kulongosebwa ate nga gavumenti zakitwala mungeeri y’ekisaazisaazi nga n’abakiise baazo bona tebabeerangawo.
entries.english: The Articles required unanimous consent from all the states before they could be amended and states took the central government so lightly that their representatives were often absent.

entries.luganda: Omupiira gweggwanga lwa Yitale ogwebigere, wamu nettiimu yeggwanga lya bugirimaani yettiimu eyokkubiri eyetutumu mu nsi yonna era bebatwala ekikopo kyamawanga gonna mu 2006.
entries.english: Italy's national football, along with German national football team is the second most successful team in the world and were the FIFA World Cup champions in 2006.

entries.luganda: Ebyemizanyo ebiyitimufu mulimu omupiira gwebigere, ensero, vole, omupiira gw'omu mazzi, lagibe, obugaali, ice hockey, roller hockey ne mmotoka za F1.
entries.english: Popular sports include football, basketball, volleyball, water-polo, fencing, rugby, cycling, ice hockey, roller hockey and F1 motor racing.

entries.luganda: Emizannyo gyobunyogovu gikyasinze okumanyika mu bitundu by'omu mambuka nga mulimu abayitale nga bavuganya mu mizannyo gy'ensi yonna ne mu byokudduka.
entries.english: Winter sports are most popular in the Northern regions, with Italians competing in international games and Olympic events.

entries.luganda: Japaani erina ebizinga kumpi kasanvu (ekisinga obunene nga ye Honshu), ekifuula Japaani ekizinga ekyomusanvu ekisinga obunene mu nsi!
entries.english: Japans holds nearly 7,000 islands (the biggest being Honshu), making Japan the 7th largest island in the world!

entries.luganda: Olwobubinja bwebizinga Japan bwerina, Japan eyogerwako mu ssomansi nga eyekikula kya “archipelago”
entries.english: Due to the cluster/group of islands Japan has, Japan is often referred to, on a geographical stance, as an "archipelago"

entries.luganda: Taiwan yatandiika dda nnyo mu kyasa kya 15th era nga abazungu abatambulira ku bizinga bwe bali bayita wo bakiwandiika era ne bakituuma llha Formosa, akazinga akalungi.
entries.english: Taiwan beginning start way back in 15th century where European sailors passing by record the island’s name as Ilha Formosa, or beautiful island.

entries.luganda: Mu 1624, kampuni ya budaachi mu bukiikaddyo bwa Buyindi eteekawo ekitebe mumaserengeta n'ebuva njuba bwa Tayiwani, nga etandiikawo enkyukakyuka mu kukola okufulumya kw'empeke ezasooka wo n'okuwandiika abakozi aba China okukola Ku nnimiro y'omuceere ne sukaali.
entries.english: In 1624,Dutch East India Company establishes a base in southwestern Taiwan, initiating a transformation in aboriginal grain production practices and employing Chinese laborers to work on its rice and sugar plantations.

entries.luganda: Mu 1683, amajje ga Qing dynasty(1644-1912) gawamba obuvanjuba bwa Taiwan n'amambuka ne balangirira Taiwan nga weri erimu ku bwakabaka bwa Qing mu 1885.
entries.english: In 1683, Qing dynasty (1644-1912) forces take control of Taiwan’s western and northern coastal areas and declared Taiwan as a province of the Qing Empire in 1885.

entries.luganda: Mu 1895, oluvanyuma lw'okuwangulwa mu lutalo lwa Sino- Japan I olwasooka (1894-1895), Gavumenti ya Wing eyateeka omukono ku ndagaano ya Shimonoseki, mweyawelayo obuyinza Ku Taiwan eri Japan, efuga ebizinga okutuusa 1945.
entries.english: In 1895, after defeat in the First Sino-Japanese War (1894-1895), the Qing government signs the Treaty of Shimonoseki, by which it cedes sovereignty over Taiwan to Japan, which rules the island until 1945.

entries.luganda: Machu picchu eyina ebiwagwo bisatu, ebitumiddwa Intihuatana, temple y’omusana, ekisenge kya amadiinisa asaatu.
entries.english: Machu Picchu consist of three main structures, namely Intihuatana, the Temple of the Sun, and the Room of the Three Windows.

entries.luganda: Ebizimbe ebisinga ku mabbali g’ekizimbe bidabirizidwa okusobola okuwa abalambuze ekirowoozo nga bwe byali bifaanana edda.
entries.english: Most of the buildings on the edges of the complex have been rebuilt in order to give tourists a better idea of how they originally appeared.

entries.luganda: Mu mwaka gwa 1976, ebitundu asatu ku buli kikumi ebya Machu Picchu byali bideabiliziddwa era bwekili naguno gwaka
entries.english: By 1976, thirty percent of Machu Picchu had been restored and restoration continues till today.

entries.luganda: Okugeza,ekifaananyi ekyabulijo eekimanyikidwa mu nsi yonna kirina 35mm,nga yeno eyali sayizi ya filimu mu kyaasa kya analoogo.
entries.english: For example, the most common still image photography format in the world is 35mm, which was the dominant film size at the close of the analog film era.

entries.luganda: Kikyafulumizibwa nabuli kati, naye n'ekisinga omugaso kiri nti lekyo(ratio) yakyo yasikirwa okuva mu digito kamera ekifaananyi ekisensinga fomati.
entries.english: It is still produced today, but more importantly its aspect ratio has been inherited by digital camera image sensor formats.

entries.luganda: Fomati eya mili mita asattu mu taano ebuzabuzamu n’eya mili mita asatu mu mukaga mu bugazi n’abili mu nya 24 mu buwanvu.
entries.english: The 35mm format is actually, somewhat confusingly, 36mm in width by 24mm in height.

entries.luganda: Enjawulo wakati wazzino(bwogabanya kkumi na bbiri okufuna nnamba eyetongodde) nolwekyo egambibwa okubeera 3:2.
entries.english: The aspect ratio of this format (dividing by twelve to obtain the simplest whole-number ratio) is therefore said to be 3:2.

entries.luganda: Enkola zokunonyereza nyinji (APS eyamaka, okugeza) zenkana nokutebereza kuno.
entries.english: Many common formats (APS family of formats, for example) are equal to or closely approximate this aspect ratio.

entries.luganda: Etteka elisinga okunyomebwa n’okuvumwa mu matekka assatu lyetekka lyokusigala nga tukola ebigasa era nga tukuma ekifananyi
entries.english: The much-abused and often-ridiculed rule of thirds is a simple guideline creating dynamism while keeping a measure of order in an image.

entries.luganda: Kigamba nti ekifo ekisinga okukola olwesomo ekulu lili munda munyiliri zigabanya ekifanaanyi mu busatu mu busimba ne mu bukiika (laba ekyokulabilako).
entries.english: It states that the most effective place for the main subject is at the intersection of lines dividing the image into thirds vertically and horizontally (see example).

entries.luganda: Mu kaseera kano ekebyafaayo bya bulaaya, Eklezia yabakatoliki eyala egagawadde nga yamanyi yafuuka yakwekebejebwa.
entries.english: During this period of European history, the Catholic Church, which had become rich and powerful, came under scrutiny.

entries.luganda: Okumala ebanga ly’amyaka lukumi nomusobyo, ediini yabakristu yagata amawanga gabayuropiinu wammu wadde nga waliwo enjawukana mu nimi n’obuwangwa.
entries.english: For over a thousand years the Christian religion had bound European states together despite differences in language and customs. I

entries.luganda: Amaanyi gaakyo agasasana buliwamu gaakosa buli omu okuva ku kabaka okutuuka ku muntu wabulijjo.
entries.english: Its all-pervading power affected everyone from king to commoner.

entries.luganda: Ekikuru ekimu kuba kirisitu kyebakiririzaamu kiri nti obugaga bulina okukozesebwa okujawo okubonabona n’obwaavu era nti obuyambi bwensimbi mu sinzizo bwansonga eyo.
entries.english: One of the main Christian tenets is that wealth should be used to alleviate suffering and poverty and that the monetary funds of the church are there specifically for that reason.

entries.luganda: Ekitebe ekikulu ekyomumasekati ekya kereziya kyali Roma okumala emyaka egisoba mu lukumi era kino ekyekubira ky’amanyi ne’nsimbi kyaleetera bangi okwebuuza oba emiteendera gyagobererwa.
entries.english: The central authority of the church had been in Rome for over a thousand years and this concentration of power and money led many to question whether this tenet was being met.

entries.luganda: Oluvanyuma lwokugwawo kw’obulumbaganyi, Bungereza yateekawo omusale gw’amajje ga Germany.
entries.english: Soon after the outbreak of hostilities, Britain initiated a naval blockade of Germany.

entries.luganda: Enteekateeka eyo yalazze nti ejja kola, okusalako abamajje abakulu nebiweebwa abantu babulijjo, waddenga kinno kyakyankalanyiza eteeka erifuga amawanga ermazze ebyaasa bibiri.
entries.english: The strategy proved effective, cutting off vital military and civilian supplies, although this blockade violated generally accepted international law codified by several international agreements of the past two centuries.

entries.luganda: Bungereza yasima amazzi g'ensi yonna okuziyiza emmeri yonna okuyingira mu bitundu byonna ebya semayanja, okuletera akabi eri emmeri ezolukale.
entries.english: Britain mined international waters to prevent any ships from entering entire sections of ocean, causing danger to even neutral ships.

entries.luganda: Engeri jjekiri nti waliwo okuddibwamu kutono ku kano akakoddyo, bugirimaani yabade esuubira okuddibwamu okufaanagana ku nkozesa yabwe eyobwato obutono etayimiriziddwa.
entries.english: Since there was limited response to this tactic, Germany expected a similar response to its unrestricted submarine warfare.

entries.luganda: Mu mwaka gwa 1920, endowoza y’abantu yali yesigama nyo ku kubera weka n’okweyawula.
entries.english: During the 1920s, the prevailing attitudes of most citizens and nations was that of pacifism and isolation.

entries.luganda: Nga bamazze okulaba ebikangabwa n’emisango ejaliwo mu sematalo eyasooka. Ensi zaluubirirwa nyo okugeezako okwewala embeera nga eyo mubiseera eby’omumaaso.
entries.english: After seeing the horrors and atrocities of war during World War I, nations desired to avoid such a situation again in the future.

entries.luganda: Mu 1884, Tesla yadda mu Amerika okukkiriza omulimu ne Edison Company mu New York City.
entries.english: In 1884, Tesla moved to the United States of America to accept a job with the Edison Company in New York City.

entries.luganda: Yatuuka mu Amerika n'obunusu 4 ku linnya lye, akatabo k'ebitontome, n'ebbaluwa emuwaana okuva ewa Charles Batchelor (eyali amukulira mu mulimu gwe ogusembayo) eri Thomas Edison.
entries.english: He arrived in the US with 4 cents to his name, a book of poetry, and a letter of recommendation from Charles Batchelor (his manager in his previous job) to Thomas Edison.

entries.luganda: Kyayina eyedda yalina engeri eyenjawulo elaga enkyuukakyuuka mubiseera; buli ssa lya China oba buli maka agaali mu buyinza kyaali kyangu okulaba obukulembeze bwensikirano.
entries.english: Ancient China had a unique way of showing different time periods; each stage of China or each family that was in power was a distinctive dynasty.

entries.luganda: Wakati wemirembe gy’abakulembeze, waliwo enjawukana. Eyasinga okumanyibwa kuzino yali federo yababaka bobwakabaka busatu eyaliwo okumala emyaka 60 wakati w’obukelembeze bwa Han ne Jin.
entries.english: Also between each dynasty was an unstable age of divided provinces. The best-known of these periods was the Three Kingdoms epoch taking place for 60 years between the Han and the Jin Dynasty.

entries.luganda: Mubiseera bino olutalo olwamanyi lwabaluka WO wakati wa abakungu bangi nga balwanira Namulondo.
entries.english: During these periods fierce warfare took place between many nobles fighting for the throne.

entries.luganda: Obwakabaka obusatu bwebumu ku byasa ebyayiwa omusaayi mu byayita bya China enkuminenkumi zabantu zaafa nga zigezaako okutuula mu ntebe ekyasinze okubeera waggulu mu bwakabaka bwa Xi’an.
entries.english: The Three Kingdoms was one of the bloodiest eras in Ancient China’s history thousands of people died fighting to sit in the highest seat in the grand palace at Xi’an.

entries.luganda: Waliwo ebintu bingi ebiva mu bintu ebya bulijjo oba eby’obufuzi nga emitambo egyikozesebwa okubala, okukyusa okuva mu mundowooza egoberera amateeka amakakali okudda mu nfugga eya republicanism, okuggumiza n’okuwagira ensi byeetwala nti bya nkizo n’okukkiriza nti ensi ya abantu bonna nga si ya muntu omu sekkinoomu.
entries.english: There are a lot of social and political effects such as the use of metric system, a shift from absolutism to republicanism, nationalism and the belief the country belongs to the people not to one sole ruler.

entries.luganda: Oluvanyuma lwo lutalo emiririmu gya gulawo eri bonna abasajja abali basabye okusinga okukiriza abo abali abamalirivu era bawangula.
entries.english: Also after the Revolution occupations were open to all male applicants allowing the most ambitious and successful to succeed.

entries.luganda: Kyekimu ekigenda eri ab’amajje kubanga mukiffo ky’ogugaba emitendera nga basinziira ku kibiina bali basinziira kubusobozi bwamuntu.
entries.english: Same goes for the military because instead of army rankings being based on class they were now based on cailaber.

entries.luganda: Olutalo lwabufalansa lwayigiriza abakozi bangi munsi endala okutandika okwekalakaasa kwabwe.
entries.english: The French Revolution also inspired many other repressed working class people of other country's to began their own revolutions.

entries.luganda: Muhammad yali ayagala nnyo ensonga ez’omunda mu bulamu. Yabeeranga mu mpuku eyitibwa “Hira” ku lusozi olwa “Noor” (ekitangaala) okwenenya.
entries.english: Muhammad was deeply interested in matters beyond this mundane life. He used to frequent a cave that became known as “Hira‘” on the Mountain of “Noor” (light) for contemplation.

entries.luganda: Empuku yennyini, eyasimattuka ebiseera, ewa ekifaananyi ekimatizza ku Ndowooza za Muhammad ku mwoyo
entries.english: he cave itself, which survived the times, gives a very vivid image of Muhammad’s spiritual inclinations.

entries.luganda: Ngakiwumulira waggulu ku lusozi olumu mu mambuka ga Mecca, empuku yesudde yokka okuva kunsi yonna.
entries.english: Resting on the top of one of the mountains north of Mecca, the cave is completely isolated from the rest of the world.

entries.luganda: Eky’amazima, sikyangu n’olumu newekyandibadde nti waliyo amanyi nti gyekiri. Kasita kiyingiya mu mpuku, kyebonga.
entries.english: In fact, it is not easy to find at all even if one knew it existed. Once inside the cave, it is a total isolation.

entries.luganda: Tewali kisobola kulabibwa okujjako , obwengula omubalagavu wagulu n’ensozi ezetooloodewo. Bintu bitono nyo ku nsi kunno byosobola okulaba oba okuwulira nga oli mu mpuku.
entries.english: Nothing can be seen other than the clear, beautiful sky above and the many surrounding mountains. Very little of this world can be seen or heard from inside the cave.

entries.luganda: Pilamiidi eyamaanyi eye Giza yeyokka kubyewunyiisa omusaanvu ekyayimiridde n’olwaleero.
entries.english: The Great Pyramid at Giza is the only one of the seven wonders that is still standing today.

entries.luganda: Zazimbibwa abantu abamisiri mu kyaasa ekyokusatu BCE, pilamiidi enkulu y’ezimu kwezo enyinji ezazimbibwa okutendereza falawo.
entries.english: Built by the Egyptians in the third century BCE, the Great Pyramid is one of many large pyramid structures built to honor dead Pharaoh.

entries.luganda: Obwaazi bw’e Giza oba “Giza Necropolis” mu bikko bya Misiri eby'abafu mulimu obwaazi obusongovu bungi (nga muzzo nga ako ak'amaanyi ke kakyasinze obunene), amasiro manji amatonotono, obusinzizo bungi, n’ekikulekule.
entries.english: The Giza Plateau, or "Giza Necropolis" in the Egyptian Valley of the Dead contains several pyramids (of which the great pyramid is the largest), several small tombs, several temples, and the great Sphinx.

entries.luganda: Pilamidi enene yakolebwa kusa mu Falawo Khufu ekitibwa, era pilamidi entonotono, entaana n’amasinzizo gazimbibwa kusamu bakyala be, n’abomunjju ye ekitibwa
entries.english: The great pyramid was created to honor the Pharaoh Khufu, and many of the smaller pyramids, tombs, and temples were built to honor Khufu's wives and family members.

entries.luganda: Okuvunama okwawagulu kulabikanga V ate okwawansi kulinga akagata empapula oba akabokisi akataliiko kyawansi.
entries.english: The "up bow" mark looks like a V and the "down bow mark" like a staple or a square missing its bottom side.

entries.luganda: Wagulu kitegeeza nti olina kutandikira ku kasongovu osindikke akasaale, ate wansi kitegeeza nti olina kutandikira ku kikere (nga wano omukono gwo we gukwatiridde akasaale) kati osike akasaale
entries.english: Up means you should start at the tip and push the bow, and down means you should start at the frog (which is where your hand is holding the bow) and pull the bow.

entries.luganda: Okusunira ennanga wagulu evaamu eddoboozi nga ligonvugonvu, naye okugisunira wansi eddoboozi liba lya manyi atte nga liggumiza.
entries.english: An up-bow usually generates a softer sound, while a down-bow is stronger and more assertive.

entries.luganda: Wulira eddembe okuwandiika mu bubonero bwo, naye jukira obubonero bwe ba purintinze webuli olw'ensonga z'okuyimba, nolwekyo zirina okuwebwa nga ekitiibwa.
entries.english: Feel free to pencil in your own marks, but remember the printed bowing marks are there for a musical reason, so they should usually be respected.

entries.luganda: King Louis XVI atidde, Queen Marie Antoinette n’abaana baabwe abato babiri (Marie Therese ow’emyaka 11 ne Louis-Charles ne ow’emyaka ena) ne mwannyina wa kabaka Madam Elizabeth, ku 6th October 1789 baagobwa ekibinja ky’abakyala b’akatale okudda e Paris okuva Versailles.
entries.english: The terrified King Louis XVI, Queen Marie Antoinette their two young children (11 year old Marie Therese and four year old Louis-Charles) and the King's sister, Madam Elizabeth, on the 6th October 1789 were forced back to Paris from Versailles by a mob of market women.

entries.luganda: Nga bali ku mbalasi, batambula okuddayo e Paris nga bagobererwa abantu abawogana ebitiisa eri kabaka ne n’abakyala.
entries.english: In a carriage, they traveled back to Paris surrounded by a mob of people screaming and shouting threats against the King and Queen.

entries.luganda: Okwegugunga kwabantu kwawaliriza Kabaka ne Nabakayala okugulawo amadirisa g'ekigaali kyaabwe.
entries.english: The mob of people forced the King And Queen to have their carriage windows wide open.

entries.luganda: Waliwo akaseera omuntu omu kubekalakaasi bweyawuuba omutwe gwoomu kubakuumu b’obwakabaka eyatibwa ku Versailles mumaaso ga Nabagereka eyali atidde.
entries.english: At one point a member of the mob waved the head of a royal guard killed at Versailles in front of the terrified Queen.

entries.luganda: Olutalo lwa Amerika mu kuwamba ebizinga bya Phillipines byasasulilwa bafilipino benyini.
entries.english: The war expenditures of U.S. imperialism in the conquest of the Philippines were paid for by the Filipino people themselves.

entries.luganda: Bali balina okusasula emisolo eri ekibiina ekifuzi ekya U.S. okusobola okufuna ezisasula ebisinga kunsasanya era namagoba ku migabobo gali gagudde mu linya ly’obufuzi bwa Phillippine nga bayita mu kisenge ky’enyumba zokunguudo.
entries.english: They were compelled to pay taxes to the U.S. colonial regime to defray a major part of the expenditures and the interest on bonds floated in the name of the Philippine government through the Wall Street banking houses.

entries.luganda: Mazima ddala, amagoba agamaanyi agajidwa mu kunyigiriza abantu aba Fillipino byebimu ku byenfuna ku byenfuga byabamerika.
entries.english: Of course, the superprofits derived from the protracted exploitation of the Filipino people would constitute the basic gains of U.S. imperialism.

entries.luganda: Okutegeera Templar, oba olina okutegeera ensonga lwaaki enteekateeka eyo yateekebwaawo
entries.english: To understand the Templars one must understand the context that prompted the creation of the order.

entries.luganda: Emyaka, ebyafayo bino webyabelawo gyiyitibwa egyawakati. Mu byafayo bya Bangereza mu 11th, 12tgh, ne 13th, gyali emyaka wakati wa Lukumi ne lukumi mu bisatu nga mukama waffe yesu kristu affudde(AD 1000-1300).
entries.english: The age where the events took place is commonly referred as the High Middle Ages the period of European history in the 11th, 12th, and 13th centuries (AD 1000–1300).

entries.luganda: Emyaka jawagulu ejawakati jagobererwanga Emyaka ejasooka ejawakati nejigobererwa emyaka ejayita ejawakati, mu byeda ejikoma nga mu 1500.
entries.english: The High Middle Ages were preceded by the Early Middle Ages and followed by the Late Middle Ages, which by convention ends around 1500.

entries.luganda: Okumalirira kwa tekinologiya kyekigambo ekiragirira endowooza nyingi mu nkola,okuva ku kusindika-tekinologiya oba tekinologiya akwatagana ku kigambibwa nti enkomelero y’omuntu efugibwa amagezi agakwatagana n’eamateek aga sayansi n’okuletebwa kwa tekinologiya.
entries.english: Technological determinism is a term that encompasses a wide range of ideas in practice, from technology-push or the technological imperative to a strict sense that human destiny is driven by an underlying logic associated with scientific laws and their manifestation in technology.

entries.luganda: Okunyonyola okusinga okwa tekinologiya kugabanya ebirowoozo ebyawamu bibiri: nti okukulakulanya tekinolojiya yenyina ogoberera ekkubo okusinga ebyobuwangwa nebyobufuzi, nti era tekinologiya erina ensonga ku bitundu ebyensikirano, okusinga kwebyo ebyembeera zabulijjo.
entries.english: Most interpretations of technological determinism share two general ideas: that the development of technology itself follows a path largely beyond cultural or political influence, and that technology in turn has "effects" on societies that are inherent, rather than socially conditioned.

entries.luganda: Okugeza, omu ayinza okugamba nti emotoka yempaka yeetagisa okuleeta enkula kulana y’enguudo.
entries.english: For example, one might say that the motor car necessarily leads to the development of roads.

entries.luganda: Lwakubanga, omukutu ogugatta amakubo mu nsi yonna tegfakola makulu mubyenfuna eri emotoka eziwerako, noolwekyo engeri ezokukola zikolebwa okukendeeza ku miwendo gyokufuna emotoka yobwananyini.
entries.english: However, a nationwide road network is not economically viable for just a handful of cars, so new methods of production are developed to reduce the cost of car ownership.

entries.luganda: Abantu abangi okubeera ne motoka kuleeta obubenje kunguudo okweyongera, ekireeta okutondawo obukodyo obupya mu by'okujanjaba okusobola okutereza abakosedwa.
entries.english: Mass car ownership also leads to a higher incidence of accidents on the roads, which leads to the invention of new techniques in healthcare for repairing damaged bodies.

entries.luganda: Okwegadanga kwavira ddala kubya nnono, nga biva kubawandiisi bobutabo nga Goethe, Fichte, ne Schlegel.
entries.english: Romanticism had a large element of cultural determinism, drawn from writers such as Goethe, Fichte, and Schlegel.

entries.luganda: Mu nkola erimu okusavuwaza, abantu ababumbiddwa mu ngeri y'enkula, n’oluvanyuma lw'obudde era empisa n’obutonde ebyekuusa ku kifo ekyo byamerukawo era bino mu kubeera mu kukkiriziganya byali birungi n’okusinga amateeka agatekebwawo okulungamya.
entries.english: In the context of Romanticism, the geography molded individuals, and over time customs and culture related to that geography arose, and these, being in harmony with the place of the society, were better than arbitrarily imposed laws.

entries.luganda: Mumpisa eyo ekibuga kya Paris kimanyidwa nga ekyabambazi munsi, Constantinople kyekibuga kyabambazi mu bungereza eyedda.
entries.english: In the manner that Paris is known as the fashion capital of the contemporary world, Constantinople was regarded as the fashion capital of feudal Europe.

entries.luganda: Kimanyikidwa nga ensibuko y’ebyokwejalabya weyatandikira nga mu 400 A.D era kyabeerawo paka 1100A.D.
entries.english: Its renown for being an epicenter of luxury began in about 400 A.D. and lasted up until about 1100 A.D.

entries.luganda: Embeera yaakyo yadilira mu biseera by'ekyaasa eky'ekumi nebiri okusingila ddala okusinzira Ku mazima nti abalwanyi Bali bakomyewo nga balina ebirabo nga zi siliki n'ebirungo ebyali bisibwaamu ekitiibwa okusinga akatale ka Byzantine byekagaba.
entries.english: Its status declined during the twelfth century mainly due to the fact that Crusaders had returned bearing gifts such as silks and spices that were valued more than what Byzantine markets offered.

entries.luganda: Obudde bwali bwebuti okukyuusa omutwe gwa kabuga kemisono okuva mu Constantinople okudda mu Paris gyekwakolebwa.
entries.english: It was at this time that the transfer of the title of Fashion Capital from Constantinople to Paris was made.

entries.luganda: Emisomo ja Gothic jatutumuka nyoo mubiseera wakati w’ebyasa bye 10 ne 11 wamu ne 14.
entries.english: Gothic style peaked in the period between the 10th - 11th centuries and the 14th century.

entries.luganda: Mu ntandikwa enyambala yagoberera nyo eby’obuwangwa obwa Byzantine mu buvanjuba.
entries.english: At the beginning dress was heavily influenced by the Byzantine culture in the east.

entries.luganda: Naye okusinzira Ku mikutu gye mpuliziganya egisooba, stayilo mu bulaya zisobola okusigala emabega emyaka 25- Ku 30.
entries.english: However, due to the slow communication channels, styles in the west could lag behind by 25 to 30 year.

entries.luganda: Nga emyaka gy'edda ginaatera okuggwako ensi z'abazungu zatandika omusono gwazo. Emu ku nkulaakulanya ekyasinze mu biseera ebyo, abantu baatandika okukozesa amappeesa okusiba engoye.
entries.english: towards the end of the Middle Ages western Europe began to develop their own style. one of the biggest developments of the time as a result of the crusades people began to use buttons to fasten clothing.

entries.luganda: Obulimi bwokulima ekyokulya yenima ekolebwa okufunamu emere eyo emala omulimi n’abamakaage okulyaako.
entries.english: Subsistence agriculture is agriculture carried out for the production of enough food to meet just the needs of the agriculturalist and his/her family.

entries.luganda: Okulima okw’emmere ey’ewaka kwangu, ebiseera ebisinga kwekuusa ku bijimusa eby’obutonde nga bakozesa ensigo ezasangwawo nga bakyuusa ebirime oba enkola endala ez’enjawulo okusobola okukungula ekingi.
entries.english: Subsistence agriculture is a simple, often organic, system using saved seed native to the ecoregion combined with crop rotation or other relatively simple techniques to maximize yield.

entries.luganda: Ebyafaayo biraga nti abalimi abasinga beetabanga mu kulima emmere y'awaka era bwe gukyaali mu nsi ezikyakula.
entries.english: Historically most farmers were engaged in subsistence agriculture and this is still the case in many developing nations.

entries.luganda: Obuwangwa obutonotono bugatta abantu bendowooza emu abawulira nga baboledwa omutindo gw’ebitundu byabwe n’ekibayamba okufuna amagezi gokumayibwa.
entries.english: Subcultures bring together like-minded individuals who feel neglected by societal standards and allow them to develop a sense of identity.

entries.luganda: Obuwangwa obutonotono busobola okwawulwayawulwa olwemyaka, ekikula, ebibiina, ekifo n’ekikula kyabakiise.
entries.english: Subcultures can be distinctive because of the age, ethnicity, class, location, and/or gender of the members.

entries.luganda: Ebisanyiizo ebisalawo ekitundu kyobuwangwa nga ekyenjawulo luyina kuba lulimi, eby'obulungi ediini, ebyobufuzi, ekikula, ekitundu, oba omugate gwensonga .
entries.english: The qualities that determine a subculture as distinct may be linguistic, aesthetic, religious, political, sexual, geographical, or a combination of factors.

entries.luganda: Bamemba bakatundu kebyobuwangwa bulijjo balaga obwabamemba ngabakozesa omusono gwobubonero, ngamuno mwemuli emisono, eneyisa n’embeera.
entries.english: Members of a subculture often signal their membership through a distinctive and symbolic use of style, which includes fashions, mannerisms, and argot.

entries.luganda: Engeri emu emanyikidwa ekyasinze okwolesa omugaso gwokutabagana kwekukolawo engeri eyamba abaana bakateyamba era ngatebalina mwasirizi, abasulibwawo,abateesobola, abakabasanyizibwa abatakula nabantu bakulu ngabakuzibwa.
entries.english: One of the most common methods used to illustrate the importance of socialization is to draw upon the few unfortunate cases of children who were, through neglect, misfortune, or wilful abuse, not socialized by adults while they were growing up.

entries.luganda: Abo abaana bwe batyo babayita “bakyetwala” oba bakyewaggula. Abaana abamu bakyewaggula bakwatiddwa abantu (okusinga bakadde baabwe); emirundi egisinga okusuulibwawo kw'abaana bano kiva ku bazadde baabwe kubeegana n’obutakwatagana nabo.
entries.english: Such children are called "feral" or wild. Some feral children have been confined by people (usually their own parents); in some cases this child abandonment was due to the parents' rejection of a child's severe intellectual or physical impairment.

entries.luganda: Abaana abazzi b’emisango bayinza okuba nga bayita mumbeera eyokutulugunyizibwa n’okutyoboola eddembe lyabwe nga tebana balekawo oba okudduka.
entries.english: Feral children may have experienced severe child abuse or trauma before being abandoned or running away.

entries.luganda: Ebimu bigambibwa okuba nga byaletebwa ensolo; ate ebimu bigambibwa okuba nga byali bibera mu nsiko kulwazo.
entries.english: Others are alleged to have been brought up by animals; some are said to have lived in the wild on their own.

entries.luganda: Nga amaze okukuzibwa ebisolo ebitali bantu, omwana w’okunsiko alaga eneeyisa (ebintu ebilabwaako) kyenkana nga ey’ebyo ebisolo, okugeza nga okutya kw’akyo oba obulati bumu na bantu.
entries.english: When completely brought up by non-human animals, the feral child exhibits behaviors (within physical limits) almost entirely like those of the particular care-animal, such as its fear of or indifference to humans.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde okusomesebwa kwa pulojekiti kuleetera okusoma okubeera okwangu n'okunyuma, naye ebyokuzimba bigendera ddala mu kkowe lya waggulu.
entries.english: While project based learning should make learning easier and more interesting, scaffolding goes a step beyond.

entries.luganda: Ekizimbe kyosomesezaamu ebyokuzimba nnokukanika tekiyitibwa nkola yakusomesa naye kyeyambisibwa abantu abayita mu musoma omupya nga okukozesa akyuma kikalimagezi oba okutaandika omulimu omupya.
entries.english: Scaffolding is not a method of learning but rather an aid that provides support to individuals whom are undergoing a new learning experience such as using a new computer program or beginning a new project.

entries.luganda: Emisingi gisobola okubera egyaddala oba nedda, kitegezza nti omusomesa ayinza okubera omusingi naye n’akoma akakwata empapula mu yafisi
entries.english: Scaffolds can be both virtual and real, in other words, a teacher is a form of scaffold but so is the little paperclip man in Microsoft Office.

entries.luganda: Amadaala agatali gadala gayungibwa mu software nga galubirirwa okubuuza, okukirizisa n’okunyonyola emitendera egisobola okuwa obuzibu abayizi okubikola boka.
entries.english: Virtual Scaffolds are internalized in the software and are meant to question, prompt, and explain procedures that may have been to challenging for the student to handle alone.

entries.luganda: Abaana bateekebwa mu balabirira abaana kulw'ensonga enyinji ezitandikira ku bulagajjavu, ku kutigumbula, n’okutta.
entries.english: Children are placed in Foster Care for a wide variety of reasons that range from neglect, to abuse, and even to extortion.

entries.luganda: Tewali mwaana ateekedwa kukulira mu mbeera etali ya kukuzibwa, kufibwaako,ate nga yakuyiga, Naye bakikola.
entries.english: No child should ever have to grow up in an environment that is not nurturing, caring, and educational, but they do.

entries.luganda: Tutegeela nti enkola yokulabilira abaana botazadde yesinga okuba enungi kubaana banno.
entries.english: We perceive the Foster Care System to be a safety zone for these children.

entries.luganda: Enkola yaffe endala eyokwerabirira eyambibwako okuwayo amaka amagumu, Abalabirira abalina omukwano, ebyenjigiriza ebigundiivu n’ebyobulamu ebyesigika.
entries.english: Our foster care system is supposed to provide safe homes, loving caregivers, stable education, and reliable health care.

entries.luganda: Okuyamba gwotazadde olina okuwa bu kyetaagisa ekyaali kibuzeewo awaka wobeela omujje.
entries.english: Foster care is supposed to provide all the necessities that were lacking in the home they were previously taken from.

entries.luganda: Omutimbagano gutwala ebintu bingi byonna mu bungi oba kin’omu mu mpuliriziganya.
entries.english: The Internet combines elements of both mass and interpersonal communication.

entries.luganda: Embala ez'enjawulo eza yintaneti ziletela emitendera egyeyongeramu mu nkola ez'emigaso ne nkola y'okutukilira ematiza.
entries.english: The distinct characteristics of the Internet lead to additional dimensions in terms of the uses and gratifications approach.

entries.luganda: Okugeza, “okuyiga” n’ “okukwatagana” biweredwa nga ebisikiriza okukozesa omutimbagano (James et al., 1995).
entries.english: For example, “learning” and “socialization” are suggested as important motivations for Internet use (James et al., 1995).

entries.luganda: “Okwetaba mu” ne “entegeragana” byalondebwa mu Eighmey ne McCord (1998) nga ebintu ebipya ebya sindikirizanga abantu ku mitimbagano.
entries.english: “Personal involvement” and “continuing relationships” were also identified as new motivation aspects by Eighmey and McCord (1998) when they investigated audience reactions to websites.

entries.luganda: Okwata obutaambi kuleesewo okuzuula okwenjawulo nga ba vvuunula obuntu obutonotono mu kikula enya amaaso buli lweekyukka mu bu tikti obutono.
entries.english: The use of video recording has led to important discoveries in the interpretation of micro-expressions, facial movements which last a few milliseconds.

entries.luganda: Olumu, kigambibwa nti omuntu asobola okumanya oba omuntu alimba nga avvunula okunnyonyola kw'obuntu obutono mu ngeri entuufu.
entries.english: In particular, it is claimed that one can detect whether a person is lying by interpreting micro-expressions correctly.

entries.luganda: Oliver Sacks, mulupapula lwe Okwogera kw’omukulembeze, yalagga nti abantu abatasobola kutegeera kwogera olw’obuvune kubwongo, baba atte basobola okusengeka nsonga zamazima bulungi.
entries.english: Oliver Sacks, in his paper The President's Speech, indicated how people who are unable to understand speech because of brain damage are nevertheless able to assess sincerity accurately.

entries.luganda: Yeyongela okugamba nti obusobozi mu kuvvunula eneeyisa y'abantu kusobola okwefaananyirizaako n'ebisolo nga embwa z'awaka.
entries.english: He even suggests that such abilities in interpreting human behavior may be shared by animals such as domestic dogs.

entries.luganda: Okunoonyereza kw’ekyaasa ky’abiri kulaga nti waliwo ebidiba bibiri ebyawukana mu kikula: byekweese era biragiddwa.
entries.english: Twentieth century research has shown that there are two pools of genetic variation: hidden and expressed.

entries.luganda: Okuzibira kuleetawo enjawukana empya, era okusaunsulamu kukijayo muebyo ebiragidwa mu kuzibira.
entries.english: Mutation adds new genetic variation, and selection removes it from the pool of expressed variation.

entries.luganda: Okusosolwa n’okugatika ebintu ebikyuusakyuusa wakati wenjuuyi zombie mu byaasa byombi.
entries.english: Segregation and recombination shuffle variation back and forth between the two pools with each generation.

entries.luganda: Wabweru ku savanna, kizibu ekintu ekirya ng'abantu okukussa asidi za amino ezetaagisa okuva mu birime.
entries.english: Out on the savanna, it is hard for a primate with a digestive system like that of humans to satisfy its amino-acid requirements from available plant resources.

entries.luganda: N’ekirala, okulemelerwa okukola bwotyo kilina ebivaamu eby’amaanyi: Okweekyaawa, okugongobala, n’okuffa nga tonatuuka.
entries.english: Moreover, failure to do so has serious consequences: growth depression, malnutrition, and ultimately death.

entries.luganda: Ebintu ebiva mu bimera ebyandibadde ebyangu okufunwa kyandibadde ekiriisa ekisangibwa mu bikoola n'ensigo, naye bino bizibu okuliibwa ffe abantu okuleka nga bifumbidwako.
entries.english: The most readily accessible plant resources would have been the proteins accessible in leaves and legumes, but these are hard for primates like us to digest unless they are cooked.

entries.luganda: Mu kugereageranya, emmere yebisolo (ensw,enkuyege , amagi) ngolese ekyokuba bti bimilika mangu,birina ekiriisa mu mubiri ekyamanyi ekirina aside zonna ezeetagisa mumubiri.
entries.english: In contrast, animal foods (ants, termites, eggs) not only are easily digestible, but they provide high-quantity proteins that contain all the essential amino acids.

entries.luganda: Okugattira awamu, tetulina kwewuunya nti bajjajjaffe e baagonjoola ekizibu bye “ebizimbamubiri” mungeri yeemu n'amazike agabeera ku ttale leero.
entries.english: All things considered, we should not be surprised if our own ancestors solved their "protein problem" in somewhat the same way that chimps on the savanna do today.

entries.luganda: Okutaataganyizibwa mutulo kwekuzuukuka ngokigenderede mubudde bwo bwewebakiramu bulijjo noluvanyuma noddamu okwebaka mu kabanga katono (eddakiika 10—60)
entries.english: Sleep interruption is the process of purposefully awakening during your normal sleep period and falling asleep a short time later (10–60 minutes).

entries.luganda: Kino kisobola okukolebwa nga okozesa essaawa y'emu eriko akade okuletera okutegera naye nga tegukuzukusiza.
entries.english: This can be easily done by using a relatively quiet alarm clock to bring you to consciousness without fully waking you.

entries.luganda: Bwewesaanga nga ogyeeko akasaawa akakuzuukusa mutulo, kasse mu kisenge ekidirira osobole okuva mubulili nga kawogana ogende okajjeko.
entries.english: If you find yourself resetting the clock in your sleep, it can be placed on the other side of the room, forcing you to get out of bed to turn it off.

entries.luganda: Ebizaamu ebirala mulimu okunywa ennyo (nadala amazzi oba kyai, amanyidwa okusaabulula) ngatonaba kwebaka, ekiletera omuntu okuyimuka nafuka.
entries.english: Other biorhythm-based options involve drinking lots of fluid (particularly water or tea, a known diuretic) prior to sleep, forcing one to get up to urinate.

entries.luganda: Omuwendo gwe emirembe egyomunda omuntu gyafuna bikwatagana mungeri ndala ku muwendo gwobunkeke mumubiri nomwoyo gwomuntu.
entries.english: The amount of inner peace a person possesses correlates oppositely to the amount of tension in one’s body and spirit.

entries.luganda: Obunafu bwa maanyi agasika mu sides, nokubeerawo kw’obulamu. Buli muntu alina obusobozi bw’okuzuula eddembe n’obumativu.
entries.english: The lower the tension, the more positive the life force present. Every person has the potential to find absolute peace and contentment.

entries.luganda: Buli omu asobola okutuuka Ku kutangazibwa. Ekintu kyokka ekiyimiridde mu kubo lya yaffe bwe bunkenke n'obutakiriza bwaafe.
entries.english: Everyone can achieve enlightenment. The only thing standing in the way of this goal is our own tension and negativity.

entries.luganda: Tibetan Buddhism esinzira ku nsomesa ya Buddha naye yayongezebwayo ekubo lya Mahayana ery'omukwano n’obukodyo obungi okuva mu yoga wa bayindi.
entries.english: The Tibetan Buddhism is based on the teachings of Buddha, but were extended by the mahayana path of love and by a lot of techniques from Indian Yoga.

entries.luganda: Mu mazima Tibetan Buddhism nyangu nyo. Erimu Kundalini Yoga, okufumintiriza n’ekkubo ery'okwagala okunyuvu.
entries.english: In principle the Tibetan Buddhism is very simple. It consists of Kundalini Yoga, meditation and the path of all-embracing love.

entries.luganda: Ne yoga wa Kundaliini, amanyi ga kundaaliini (amanyi agookutangaaza) gazuukusibwa okuyita mu ngeri jebatuulamu, Okussa, mantra n’ebilagibwa
entries.english: With Kundalini Yoga the Kundalini energy (enlightenment energy) is awakened through yoga postures, breathing exercises, mantras and visualizations.

entries.luganda: Wakati mu kulowoza kwe Tibetan kwe kusilikilila kwa katonda Yoga. Mu kulowooza ku bakatonda abangi, amaanyi gadda, ebirowoozo birongoka, odda engulu ate n’okutangazibwa kubeerawo.
entries.english: The center of Tibetan meditation is the Deity Yoga. Through the visualization of various deities the energy channels are cleaned, the chakras are activated and the enlightenment consciousness is created.

entries.luganda: Bugirimaani yali mulabe wabonna mu sematalo owokubiri, nekiileeta okwegatta wakati wa USSR ne USA. Okukomekkereza kw’olutalo n'obutategeragana mu nkola, emittendera n'obuwangwa byaletera ensi obutakanya.
entries.english: Germany was a common enemy in World War 2, leading to cooperation between the USSR and USA. With the end of the war the clashes of system, process and culture led to the countries falling out.

entries.luganda: Emyaka ebiri nga giyiseewo oluvannyuma nga olutalo luwedde, ab’emikwano baafuuka balabe awo Olutalo Ssematalo ne lutandika.
entries.english: With two years of the end of the war, the former allies were now enemies and the Cold War began.

entries.luganda: Kyalina kuwangaala myaka 40 atte nga kyakulwaanira ddala, namagye amakugu, kumawanga okuva mu Afrika paka Asia, mu Afghanistan, Cuba n’ebifo ebirala bingi.
entries.english: It was to last for the next 40 years and would be fought for real, by proxy armies, on battlefields from Africa to Asia, in Afghanistan, Cuba and many other places.
