entries.luganda: Nga mugwomwenda nga 17, 1939, ebyokwerinda bya ba polish byali binafuyiziddwa, ate nga essuubi lyoka lyali lya kulekulira nakwegata ku lutindo lwa Roma mu maaso.
entries.english: By September 17, 1939, the Polish defense was already broken, and the only hope was to retreat and reorganise along the Romanian bridgehead.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde, entekateeka zino zafulibwa ddala kumpi zakiro, nga abajaasi abasuka 800,000 okuva mu maje amamyufu agebyokwegatta byabasoviyeti yayingira n'ekola nabokumwanjo ba Belaruss ne Ukraine oluvanyuma lw'okulumba ebitundu by'ebuva njuba bwa Poland nga bawakanya endagaano y'emirembe eya Riga, Soviyeti - polisi endagaano y'obutalwaana, n'endagaano endala ez'amawanga, ezababiri ne sekinomu.
entries.english: However, these plans were rendered obsolete nearly overnight, when over 800,000 soldiers from the Soviet's Union Red Army entered and created the Belarussian and Ukrainian fronts after invading the eastern regions of Poland in violation of the Riga Peace Treaty, the Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, and other international treaties, both bilateral and multilateral.

entries.luganda: Okukozzesa meeli okutambuzza ebyamaguzi y’engeri ekyasinze okubera enungi okutambuzza abantu n’ebintu ku maazi.
entries.english: Using ships to transport goods is by far the most efficient way to move large amounts of people and goods across oceans.

entries.luganda: Omugaso gwa ba sirikale gwa ku kakas anti ensi yo esigaza obusobozi okutambuza abantu ne ebyamaguzi, ate nga mu kaseera kekamu, okuyingirira abalabe bo mu kutambuza abantu ne ebyamaguzi byabwe.
entries.english: The job of navies has traditionally been to ensure that your country maintains the ability to move your people and goods, while at the same time, interfering with your enemy's ability to move his people and goods.

entries.luganda: Ekimu ku bisinga ebitasaana ekyokulabilako ekyakabaawo ku binno ye kakuyege w’omumabuka ga Atlantic owa WWII.Abameriaca baali bagezaako okujayo abantu n’ebikozesebwa nga bayita mu semayanja Atlantic okuamba Britain.
entries.english: One of the most noteworthy recent examples of this was the North Atlantic campaign of WWII. The Americans were trying to move men and materials across the Atlantic Ocean to help Britain.

entries.luganda: Mu kasera ke kamu, amajje ga bugirimaani ag’okuttaka, nga bakozesa obwato okusinga, bali bagezaako okuyimiriza akalipagano.
entries.english: At the same time, the German navy, using mainly U-boats, was trying to stop this traffic.

entries.luganda: Singa amagye g'omukago galemererwa, Bugirimani yandibadde esobola okuwamba Bungereza nga bweyakola ebitundu bya Bulaya ebirala.
entries.english: Had the Allies failed, Germany probably would have been able to conquer Britain as it had the rest of Europe.

entries.luganda: Embuzi ziringa zezasooka okulundibwa kumpi emyaka 10,000 egyayita mu nsozi za Zagros eza Iran.
entries.english: Goats seem to have been first domesticated roughly 10,000 years ago in the Zagros Mountains of Iran.

entries.luganda: Ebyobuwangwa namawanga ebyedda batandika okubikuuma okusobola okufuna amangu amata, enviir, enyama namaliba.
entries.english: Ancient cultures and tribes began to keep them for easy access to milk, hair, meat, and skins.

entries.luganda: Embuzi e’zakuno zaakuumirwanga mu maggana agaatambuliranga mu nsozi ne mu bifo ebirala omulundibwa, ebiseera ebisinga nga zilabirirwa omuluuzi ate nga abasing baali baana batto oba naga batiini, n’okwefaananyiriza omuluunzi gwe tumanyi ffena. Zino engeri z’okusumba zikyakozesebwa ne leero.
entries.english: Domestic goats were generally kept in herds that wandered on hills or other grazing areas, often tended by goatherds who were frequently children or adolescents, similar to the more widely known shepherd. These methods of herding are still used today.

entries.luganda: Amakubo g'amagaali gazimbibwa mu England mangu mu kyaasa kye 16.
entries.english: Wagonways were built in England as early as the 16th Century.

entries.luganda: Waddenga enguudo za wagon zalimu enkuubo zabibaawo, zaali zisobozesa endogoyi okuzisika okusobola okufuna obwangu n’okusika emigugu eminene okusinga bwegwaali ku nguudo embi ez’olunaku.
entries.english: Although wagonways merely consisted of parallel planks of wood, they allowed horses pulling them to achieve greater speeds and pull larger loads than on the slightly more rough roads of the day.

entries.luganda: Ekkubo lye ggaali y’omukka gagunjibwawo mangu okukwata ebimotoka. Bwebatyo, nebazuula nti ebimotoka majegere bikola bulungi nga bisibwaako ekyuuma kumipiira.
entries.english: Crossties were introduced fairly early to hold the tracks in place. Gradually, however, it was realised that tracks would be more efficient if they had a stip of iron on the top.

entries.luganda: Kino kyafuuka kikolwa kya bulijjo, naye ayoni yaleeta okwononeeka okulala Ku namuziga z'ebiti ez'ebigaali.
entries.english: This became common practice, but the iron caused more wear on the wooden wheels of the wagons.

entries.luganda: Oluvanyuma emipiira gy'embaawo gyakyuusibwa n’egyebyuuma. Mu 1767, enguudo z’emitayimbwa emijjuvu egyasooka gyateebwawo.
entries.english: Eventually, wooden wheels were replaced by iron wheels. In 1767, the first full-iron rails were introduced.

entries.luganda: Entambula eyasokawo yali ya kutambulisa bigere, abantu batambula emyaka obukadde bibili emabegga nga bazzude Homo Erectus (ekitegezza omussajja ayimilidde)
entries.english: The first known transportation was walking, humans began walking upright two million years ago with the emergence of Homo Erectus (meaning upright man).

entries.luganda: Ababasokawo bali nga bizike ebitatambula nga byesimbye nga bwebili kati.
entries.english: Their predecessors, the Australopithecus did not walk upright as habitually.

entries.luganda: Bipedal specializations zisangibwa mu magumba ga Australopithecus okuva Ku bukadde bw'emyaka 4.2-3.9 egyayita, yadde Sahelanthropusyanadiba yatambulira Ku magulu abiri nga bukyaali mu myaka obukadde musanvu egyayiita.
entries.english: Bipedal specializations are found in Australopithecus fossils from 4.2-3.9 million years ago, although Sahelanthropus may have walked on two legs as early as seven million years ago.

entries.luganda: Tusobola okutandika okubeera mu mukwano n’obutonde bwensi, tusobola okwegata kumugendo gw’obutonde bwensi era tusobola okubeera abalwanirizi okusobola okukyuusa okubonabona kw’omumaaso mu diguli ezimu.
entries.english: We can start living more friendly to the environment, we can join to the environmental movement, and we can even be activists in order to reduce the future suffering in some degree.

entries.luganda: Kino kiringa nga akobonero akajjanjabwa ebiseera ebisinga. N’ewankubade, bwetuba nga tetwagala kusalawo mu bwangu, era tutekeddwa okufuna emilandila ewava ekizibu era tukimale wo
entries.english: This is just like symptomatic treatment in many cases. However, if we do not only want a temporary solution, then we should find the root of the problems, and we should deactivate them.

entries.luganda: Kyangu okulabira ddala nti ensi ekyukidde ddala olwenkyukakyuka eziri mu saayansi ne tekinologiya, nebizibu byeyongedde nyo olwomujuzo gwabantu nembeera zabantu ezokwegagasa.
entries.english: It is obvious enough that the world has changed much because of humankind's scientific and technological advancements, and problems have become greater because of overpopulation and mankind's extravagant lifestyle.

entries.luganda: Nga aba konguleesi bamaze okukikwaata nga 4 ogw’omusanvu, Ekiwaandiko ekitaali kisunsule nga kiteeredwaako omukono omukulembeeze waakyo John Hancock era omuwandiisi Kalooli Thomson kyasindikibwa ebisenge bitonotono okuva mu duuka webyayokerwa elye John Dunlap.
entries.english: After its adoption by Congress on July 4, a handwritten draft signed by the President of Congress John Hancock and the Secretary Charles Thomson was then sent a few blocks away to the printing shop of John Dunlap.

entries.luganda: Okuyita mukiro kkopi wakati wa 150 ne 200 zakolebwa, kati nga zimanyiddwa nga “Dunlap enjuyiengazi ”.
entries.english: Through the night between 150 and 200 copies were made, now known as "Dunlap broadsides".

entries.luganda: Okusoma kw’ekiwandiiko okw’omulujjudde kwakolebwa John Nixon mu kibangirizi kya Independence Hall nga Kasambula 8.
entries.english: The first public reading of the document was by John Nixon in the yard of Independence Hall on July 8.

entries.luganda: Omuntu omu yatumidwa mu George Washington mu mwezi ogw’omukaga nga 6, eyali akirina mu kusomebwa nga agenda kulugendo lwe mu New York mu mwezi gw’omukaga nga 9. naye ekiwandiiko ekilala kyatuka mu London ogw’omunana nga 10.
entries.english: One was sent to George Washington on July 6, who had it read to his troops in New York on July 9. A copy reached London on August 10.

entries.luganda: Ebyawandikibwa 25 ebya Dunlap broadsides ebiriwo byebisinga obukadde ebyawonawo ku kiwandiko. Ekiwandiko ekya sooka wo ekyawandikibwa tekyawona.
entries.english: The 25 Dunlap broadsides still known to exist are the oldest surviving copies of the document. The original handwritten copy has not survived.

entries.luganda: Abanonyereza ku byensi bangi olwaleero bakiriza nti ekimu ku kibinja kya dayinoso kyawonawo era bilamu olwaleero tubiyita binyonyi.
entries.english: Many paleontologists today believe that one group of dinosaurs survived and is alive today. We call them birds.

entries.luganda: Abantu abasing tebabirowozako nga bisolosolo kubanga birina ebyooya ate bisobola okubuuka.
entries.english: Many people don't think about them as dinosaurs because they have feathers and can fly.

entries.luganda: Naye waliwo ebintu bingi ebikwata Ku binyonyi ebifanana nga dayinoso.
entries.english: But there are a lot of things about birds that still look like a dinosaur.

entries.luganda: Balina ebigere ebiriko ebigalagamba n'enkwagulo, bibiika amagi era bitambula ku magulu gabyo abiri ag'emabega nga T-rex.
entries.english: They have feet with scales and claws, they lay eggs, and they walk on their two back legs like a T-Rex.

entries.luganda: Era kompyuta zonna ezikozesebwa leero zisinziira kukutigatiga kwa bubaka nga bino biwandikibwa mu namba ezigoberegana.
entries.english: Virtually all computers in use today are based on the manipulation of information which is coded in the form of binary numbers.

entries.luganda: Ennamba eya nakabiri ebera ne namba emu kuzino, kyentegeeza, 0 oba 1, era zino ziyitibwa nnamba zinakabiri oba biiti bwemba ngankozeseza olulimi lwa kalimagezi.
entries.english: A binary number can have only one of two values, i.e. 0 or 1, and these numbers are referred to as binary digits - or bits, to use computer jargon.

entries.luganda: Obutwa obunnweleddwa buyinza obutalagila wo, ebimu ku bisoka okweyoleka kwe kusesema, naye era n’eddwade enddala bwezityo, kale sikyangu kumanya
entries.english: Internal poisoning may not be immediately apparent. Symptoms, such as vomiting are sufficiently general that an immediate diagnosis cannot be made.

entries.luganda: Ekisinga okulaga nti omuntu anywedde obutwa ke kaccupa akobutwa okusangibwa nga kasumulule oba ebiragalagala ebyomunju ebirimu obutwa.
entries.english: The best indication of internal poisoning may be the presence of an open container of medication or toxic household chemicals.

entries.luganda: Kebela obulambe okufuna enkozesa y’obuyambi obusokerwaako ku butwa obwo.
entries.english: Check the label for specific first aid instructions for that specific poison.

entries.luganda: Ekigambo biwuka kikozesebwa abakuggu mu biwuka okutegezza ekika kye biwuka bino
entries.english: The term bug is used by entomologists in a formal sense for this group of insects.

entries.luganda: Kino ekigambo kiva mu kumanya kw’ebiku, ebiwuka ebibeera ku mibiri gy’abantu.
entries.english: This term derives from ancient familiarity with Bed-bugs, which are insects highly adapted to parasitize humans.

entries.luganda: Ebiku byombi ebya Assasin ne Bed-bugs byatabu nyoo era bisobola okubeera mubisusunku nemunyumba zababikyazizza.
entries.english: Both Assassin-bugs and Bed-bugs are nidicolous, adapted to living in nest or housing of their host.

entries.luganda: Mu Amerika yonna, waliwo abantu emitwalo ana 400000 abalina obulwadde bw’emisiwa MS, ekitegezza nti bwe bukyasinga okusanyalaza abavubuka n’abantu abato abali mu myaka gyomumasekati.
entries.english: Across the United States of America, there are approximately 400,000 known cases of Multiple Sclerosis (MS), leaving it as the leading neurological disease in younger and middle aged adults.

entries.luganda: MS kirwadde ekikwata enkola y'emisuwa eya wakati, ekolebwa obwongo, olugongogongo ne omusuwa gwa ku liiso.
entries.english: MS is a disease that affects the central nervous system, which is made up of the brain, the spinal cord and the optic nerve.

entries.luganda: Okunoonyereza kulaga nti abakyala basinga abaami emirundi ebiri okufuna MS.
entries.english: Research has found that females are two times more likely to have MS then males.

entries.luganda: Abagalana basobola okusalawo si mu kwagala kwa bwe, oba okw’omwana w’abwe,okukuza omwana.
entries.english: A couple may decide it is not in their best interest, or in the interest of their child, to raise a baby.

entries.luganda: Abafumbo bano bayinza okusalawo okola pulani y’okutwalamu omwana ono.
entries.english: These couples may choose to make an adoption plan for their baby.

entries.luganda: Mu kuzaala mu mateeka, abazadde abazaala bejjako obuvunanyizibwa okusobola abaagalana abalala okukuza omwana.
entries.english: In an adoption, the birth parents terminate their parental rights so that another couple may parent the child.

entries.luganda: Ekigendelelwa kya sayansi ekisinga obukulu kwe kutegeera engeri ensi gyekolamu mu nkola ye kinasayansi. Enkola eno mubutuufu elungamya okunonyereza kwa sayansi okusinga.
entries.english: Science’s main goal is to figure out the way the world works through the scientific method. This method in fact guides most scientific research.

entries.luganda: Tekiri kyokka yadde, okugezesa, era okugezesa kiba kigezo ekikozesebwa okujjamu ekimu oba okusingawo ebiteberezebwa okusoboka, okubuuza ebibuuzo, n'okwetegereza nakyo kirungamya okunoonyereza kwa ssaayansi.
entries.english: It isn’t alone though, experimentation, and an experiment is a test that is used to eliminate one or more of the possible hypotheses, asking questions, and making observations also guide scientific research.

entries.luganda: Ab'obutonde n'bawandiisi balubilira Ku mpandiika ey'obutabo bw'omulembe, era mu bulambike, ku Bayibuli mu Lulatini.
entries.english: Naturalists and philosophers focused on classical texts and, in particular, on the Bible in Latin.

entries.luganda: Ebiteeso by’Aristotle's bye byaali bikkirizibwa ku buli nsonga za ssaayansi, omuli n'endowooza z’abantu.
entries.english: Accepted were Aristotle's views on all matters of science, including psychology.

entries.luganda: Nga amagezi ga bagereeki bwe gakendeera, obukiika kono bwesalako okuva ku mirandira gyayo ne bugeleeki eky'amaanyi.
entries.english: As knowledge of Greek declined, the West found itself cut off from its Greek philosophical and scientific roots.

entries.luganda: Abasiinga beekanyiza mu bivuga byabasomi bwobwongo neneeyisa kukubeerawo kwebintu ebyekuusa ku ngeri ebintu gyebikolebwamu ngabayita mu ssaawa zamasomo gebyebisolo.
entries.english: Many observed rhythms in physiology and behavior often crucially depend on the presence of endogenous cycles and their production through biological clocks.

entries.luganda: Enyimba ezakaseera, ezitali kudibwamu kuva kukulabulwa kwebweru, kuwandikidwa kulwebintu ebiramu ebisinga nga obuwuka, ensiringanyi, ebimera n'ebisolo.
entries.english: Periodic rhythms, which are not simply responses to external periodic cues, have been documented for most living beings, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.

entries.luganda: Essaawa ezomubiri zebezaawo zokka ezisobola okubeerawo nga zibala nga n’obulimi newebubera nga tebuliimu.
entries.english: Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.

entries.luganda: Okugezesa kwa Hershey ne Chase byebimu ku byalaga nti endagabutonde eri mu musaayi gwa muntu
entries.english: The Hershey and Chase experiment was one of the leading suggestions that DNA was a genetic material.

entries.luganda: Hershey and Chase bakozesanga phages, oba buwuka, okuzimba endanga buttonde mu buwuka.
entries.english: Hershey and Chase used phages, or viruses, to implant their own DNA into a bacterium.

entries.luganda: Bakola okugezesa kwa mirundi ebiri okulamba oba DNA mu phage ne radioactive phosphorus oba protein za phage ne radioactive sulfur.
entries.english: They did two experiments marking either the DNA in the phage with a radioactive phosphorus or the protein of the phage with radioactive sulfur.

entries.luganda: Okukyusa omusayi gw’omuntu kilina obuzibu bungi okusinzira otya bw’oba ogukyusiza,omugaso gw’okukyusa ebintu byonna mu oba obutafali bwe mibiri okukosebwa era nga buba bukyafu bya butafali.
entries.english: Mutations can have a variety of different effects depending on the type of mutation, the significance of the piece of genetic material affected and whether the cells affected are germ-line cells.

entries.luganda: Okukyuusa mundagabutonde y'obutafaali bw’obuwuka kyoka kyekisobola okusigibwa abaana, ng’ate enkyuukakyuuka mubutafaali awalala kisobola okuleeta okuffa kw’obutafaali obwa kansa.
entries.english: Only mutations in germ-line cells can be passed on to children, while mutations elsewhere can cause cell-death or cancer.

entries.luganda: Ebyobulambuzi ebisinziira ku buttoned bisikiriza abantu abanyumirwa okukyalira ebiffo eby’obutonde olw’okunyumirwa okulengera, omuli ebimera n’ebisoro byomunsiko.
entries.english: Nature-based tourism attracts people interested in visiting natural areas for the purpose of enjoying the scenery, including plant and animal wildlife.

entries.luganda: Ebyokkola nga otuuse mu kifo ebyokulabirako nga mw’otwalidde okuyigga, okuvuba, okkuba ebifaananyi, okulaba ebinyonyi, nokulambula ebisolo (paaka) n’okusoma ebikwaata ku nsi.
entries.english: Examples of on-site activities include hunting, fishing, photography, bird watching, and visiting parks and studying information about the ecosystem.

entries.luganda: Ekyokulabirako kwe kukyala, okukuba ebifaananyi, nokuyiga ku organgatuangd mu Borneo.
entries.english: An example is visiting, photographing, and learning about organgatuangs in Borneo.

entries.luganda: Buli ku makya, abantu bava mu bibuga ebitono mu matooka g’abwe okugenda okola n’ebayita ku banabwe nga ebifo byabwe ebyokoleramu babilese mabega .
entries.english: Every morning, people leave small country towns in cars to go their workplace and are passed by others whose work destination is the place they have just left.

entries.luganda: Mu bino ebyentambula ebyakabi buli omu alina ngeri emu oba endala gyali nga abimanyiiko, ate nga awagira ebyentambula eby'emmotoka z’obuyonjo.
entries.english: In this dynamic transport shuttle everyone is somehow connected with, and supporting, a transport system based on private cars.

entries.luganda: Sayansi kati alaga nti binno ebyenfuna ebiccupulidwa ennyo bikyuusizanyo abantu webabeera okuva ku mbeera etebenkedde ekiyambye ku kujja kwabantu okumala emyaaka obukadde bubiri.
entries.english: Science now indicates that this massive carbon economy has dislodged the biosphere from one of its stable states that has supported human evolution for the past two million years.

entries.luganda: Buli muntu yenna eyenyigira mu nkola y’eitundu bya ffene tukozesa n’entambula yonna.neye buli omu yemulugunya ku ntambula ze tukozesa.
entries.english: Everyone participates in society and uses transportation systems. Almost everyone complains about transportation systems.

entries.luganda: Mu nsi e’zikulaakulana olwawo okuwulira obutali bumativu obwenkanankana ku mutindo gwa mazzi oba entindo nga zigwa wansi.
entries.english: In developed countries you seldom hear similar levels of complaints about water quality or bridges falling down.

entries.luganda: Lwaaki enkola z’ebyentambula zileeta okwemulugunya bwekutyo, lwaki zilemelerwa buli lunaku? Oba abakozi b’enguudo tebategeera kyebakola? Oba waliwo ekyamaanyi ekigenda mumaaso?
entries.english: Why do transportation systems engender such complaints, why do they fail on a daily basis? Are transportation engineers just incompetent? Or is something more fundamental going on?

entries.luganda: Entambula ya ebidduka gwe musomo ogukwata ku buli nvuga eya derreva sekinoomu n’ebidduka byaabwe wakati w’ebiffo bibiri n’engeri jebakolaganamu awamu.
entries.english: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.

entries.luganda: Eky’ennaku,okuyiga amateeka g’okunguudo kuzibu kubanga en’eyisa yaba deleeva tosobola kugitebereza n’okugikakasa kikumi kukikumi.
entries.english: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.

entries.luganda: Ekirungi nti abavuga beeyisa mu ngeri etegerekeka, n'olwekyo entambula esobola okulagirwa mu ngeri ey'okubala.
entries.english: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.

entries.luganda: Okusobola okulaba obulungi entambula ya kalipagano, enkolagana ekoleddwa wakati w’ebintu bisatu eby’omugaso(1)entambula,(2)entuuko(3)obuwenyufu.
entries.english: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.

entries.luganda: Enkolagana zinno ziyamba mu kuteekateeka, emisono, n’enkola y’ebibeera ku nguudo.
entries.english: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.

entries.luganda: Ebiwuka byekbisolo ebyasooka okubuuka mubbanga. Obusoobozi bwabyo okubuuka bwabiyamba okudduka abalabe natte nokufuna emere n’abagalwa mubwangu.
entries.english: Insects were the first animals to take to the air. Their ability to fly helped them evade enemies more easily and find food and mates more efficiently.

entries.luganda: Ebiwuka ebisinga birina olukisa oluzisobozesa okufunya ebiwaawatiro nebibizaayo mu mibiri gyabyo.
entries.english: Most insects have the advantage of being able to fold their wings back along the body.

entries.luganda: Kinno kibawa obutundu obutonotono bungi okwekweeka ku balabe.
entries.english: This gives them a wider range of small places to hide from predators.

entries.luganda: Olwaleero, ebiwuka byoka ebitasobola kuzinga mabega biwawatiro byaabyo ze ensweera za Dragon n'ensweera za may.
entries.english: Today, the only insects that cannot fold back their wings are dragon flies and mayflies.

entries.luganda: Ebikumi by’emyaka emabega, omusajja eyayitibwa nga Aristarchus yagamba mbu ebintu byonna mubwengula byetoololera ku musana.
entries.english: Thousands of years ago, a man called Aristarchus said that the Solar System moved around the Sun.

entries.luganda: Abantu abaamu balowooza nti yali mutufu naye abasinga bakiriza kirala; amanyi g’enjuba g’atambula ku nsi,okuli omusana (n’emunyenye ezimu).
entries.english: Some people thought he was right but many people believed the opposite; that the Solar System moved around the Earth, including the Sun (and even the other stars).

entries.luganda: Kino tekikola makulu kubanga ensi terabika nga etambula, sikyo?
entries.english: This seems sensible, because the Earth doesn't feel as if it's moving, does it?

entries.luganda: Omugga gwa Amazon gwa kubiri mu buwanvu ne mu bunene mu nsi. Gutwala okusinga emirundi 8 amazzi mangi nga omugga ogwokubiri mu bunene.
entries.english: The Amazon River is the second longest and the biggest river on Earth. It carries more than 8 times as much water as the second biggest river.

entries.luganda: Omugga Amazon era gwe gusinga obugazi ku nsi yonna, awamu, obugazi buwera mailo mukaaga.
entries.english: The Amazon is also the widest river on Earth, at times six miles wide.

entries.luganda: Ebitundu abili ku buli kikumi ag’amazzi mu migga egyli ku nsi gava mu Amazoni.
entries.english: A full 20 percent of the water that pours out of the planet's rivers into the oceans comes from the Amazon.

entries.luganda: Omugga gwa Amazon gwa 6,387 km (zemailo 3,980). Gusolooza amazzi okuva mu migga emitono.
entries.english: The main Amazon River is 6,387 km (3,980 miles). It collects water from thousands of smaller rivers.

entries.luganda: Wadde nga ekizimbe kya pyramid mu mayinja kyeyongerayo paka kunkomelero y’obwakabaka obukadde, pyramid ze Giza tezakendeela mu bunene ne mu nkolayazo eyomulembe.
entries.english: Although pyramid-building in stone continued until the end of the Old Kingdom, the pyramids of Giza were never surpassed in their size and the technical excellence of their construction.

entries.luganda: Ebyobuwangwa bwabamisiri ebyayita ebirimu ebijjukizo babakadde bange, ngebyo byaliwo emyaka lukumi ejayita.
entries.english: New Kingdom ancient Egyptians marvelled at their predecessors monuments, which were then well over a thousand year old.

entries.luganda: Vatican City kilimu abantu 800. Ke kasi akeefuga kokka akakyasinze obutono munsi yonna era ensi esinga abantu abatono.
entries.english: Vatican City's population is around 800. It is the smallest independent country in the world and the country with the lowest population.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga Vaticani kikozesa luyitale mu bufuzi bwayo ne mu kubunya obubaka
entries.english: Vatican City uses Italian in its legislation and official communications.

entries.luganda: Oluyitale nalwo lulimi lwabulijo olukozesebwa abasing abakolera mu America wade nga Olulatini lusinga kukozesebwa nyo mumikolo jediini.
entries.english: Italian is also the everyday language used by most of those who work in the state while Latin is often used in religious ceremonies.

entries.luganda: Abatuze be Vatican bona banzikiriza y aba Roma abakatuliki.
entries.english: All citizens of Vatican City are Roman Catholic.

entries.luganda: Abantu babadde bamanyi ku kemiko ezenjawulo okugeza zaabu, feeza, n’amanda mu bunji, nga bino byonna bisobola okubeera nga bigibwa mu butonde mu bujjuvu bwabyo ate nga byangu bya kusima n’ebikozesebwa ebya bulijjo.
entries.english: People have known about basic chemical elements such as gold, silver, and copper from antiquity, as these can all be discovered in nature in native form and are relatively simple to mine with primitive tools.

entries.luganda: Omu filosofa Aristole yatebelezza nti buli kimu mu nsi eno kiklebwa ekimu ku bintu bino wamanga; ettaka, amazzi, empewo n’omuliro
entries.english: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.

entries.luganda: Kino kyali kiringa ekintu kyonna nga ebintu bina ebikola matter (mu mitendera egyenjawulo): ekyekute, e’kyamazzi, omuka, ne plasma ne wankubadde nga yakiwumba era nti bikyuka mu ngeri nyingi okukola kye tulaba
entries.english: This was more like the four states of matter (in the same order): solid, liquid, gas, and plasma, though he also theorised that they change into new substances to form what we see.

entries.luganda: Ekyuuma kiba kilungo kya butundu bubiri n’okusingawo. Teweerabira nti waliwo ebyuma binji ku kameeza ka piliyodi.
entries.english: Alloys are basically a mixture of two or more metals. Don't forget that there are many elements on the periodic table.

entries.luganda: Ebintu nga calcium ne potassium biyitibwa byuma, naye tulinayo ebyuma ebirala nga feeza ne zaabu.
entries.english: Elements like calcium and potassium are considered metals. Of course, there are also metals like silver and gold.

entries.luganda: Osobola n’okuba n'ebyuuma ebirinamu emiwendo emitono egitalimu byuuma nga Carbon.
entries.english: You can also have alloys that include small amounts of non-metallic elements like carbon.

entries.luganda: Buli kimu mu nsi kikolebwa obutundutundu obutono. Buli kintu kikolebwa obuntu obutono obuyitibwa atoms.
entries.english: Everything in the Universe is made of matter. All matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.

entries.luganda: Akasirikitu akayitibwa atom cokewunyisa nyoo katini nyoo naye obuwumbi bwako busobola okuja mukaseera kunkomerero ya sentesi eno.
entries.english: Atoms are so incredibly tiny that trillions of them could fit into the period at the end of this sentence.

entries.luganda: Nolwekyo, ekalaamu yali mukwaano gwabanji bweyafuluma.
entries.english: Thus, the pencil was a good friend to many people when it came out.

entries.luganda: Kyanaku, nga engeri empya ez’okuwandiika bwezivudeyo, ekalaamu tekyalinanyo ky’amanyi n’amugaso.
entries.english: Sadly, as newer methods of writing have emerged, the pencil has been relegated to lesser status and uses.

entries.luganda: Abantu kati bawandiika obubaka ku ntimbe z'ebyuuma bikali magezi, nga tebasoose kusembelera kisongola.
entries.english: People now write messages on computer screens, never having to come close to a sharpener.

entries.luganda: Omunti yewuunya oba ekyuuma kikalimagezi kinaafaanana kitya nga endala etondebwaawo.
entries.english: One can only wonder what the keyboard will become when something newer comes along.

entries.luganda: Eno bbomu ekolera ku mulamwa nti kyetaagisa amaanyi okukuuma akaziizi, obukontanyo ne nampawengwa awamu.
entries.english: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons.

entries.luganda: Kiringa okuyiringisa ekigaali ekizitowa ku kasozi. Okwawula nucleus waggulu nate kati awo nesumulula agamu ku maanyi ago.
entries.english: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy.

entries.luganda: Atomu ezimu zirina obutundutundu ekitegeeza nti zisobola okumenyekamenyeka nga tezasiddwayasiddwa.
entries.english: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.

entries.luganda: Kungulu kw'omwezi kwakolebwa njazi n'enfufu. Olububi lwokungulu olw'omwezi luyitibwa kulasiti.
entries.english: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust.

entries.luganda: Olususu lwayo lubalibwaamu 70km mu bugazi nga olabidde kumpi ne 100km mu bugazi nga olabidde wala.
entries.english: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side.

entries.luganda: Kaluwewele wansi wa maria ate kikwaafu wansi w’enjaazi.
entries.english: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands.

entries.luganda: Wayinza okubeerawo maria omungi ku kitundu ekiriraanye kubanga ekikakampa kiba kitono.kiba jyangu olunyata okubumbulukuka.
entries.english: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface.

entries.luganda: Ebyaawandiikibwa ebikusike biluubililwa ku kufuna kiki ekikyankalanya abantu oba ekibanyumila.
entries.english: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them.

entries.luganda: Engambo zigamba nti abantu balina ebyeetago oba bye baagala ebibabeeramu nga bakula okufuuka abantu abakulu.
entries.english: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood.

entries.luganda: Ebyawandiikibwa bilaga kiki ekiletela abantu abamu okwagala ebintu byebaagala nebibetoloode ekibaletera okukola oba obutakola ebintu ebimu.
entries.english: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things.

entries.luganda: Endowooza bbiri ezisinga etutumu eza Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory ne Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.
entries.english: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.

entries.luganda: Okutwaliz’awamu, empisa za milundi ebiri ziyinza okuvayo ng’abameja bakulembera abantu. Esoka kwe kugezako okubera omu ku basajja oba okubera abawala.
entries.english: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals).

entries.luganda: Ekika ky’omukulu ono kirina obuzibu mu byasalawo ebitali bya bulijjo, akola ebibonerezo, okupima omukozi bwakoze, okugaba eby’okukola, n’okubeera n’obuvunanyizibwa ku kyokola.
entries.english: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable.

entries.luganda: Kunkomelero yazilangi eziri wagulu, enkyuuka kyuuka okudda okubuzaabuza alabika nga alina okukyuusa bulikimu tiimu kyebade ekola bakole ebyaabwe.
entries.english: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own.

entries.luganda: Kubanga era, omukulembeze yaavunanyizibwaako obuwanguzi n’okulemererwa kw’ekibinja
entries.english: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team.

entries.luganda: Eneeyisa eno ebiseera ebisinga evaamu enkyuukakyuuka mu bukulembeze nekibiina ekisigalidde.
entries.english: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team.

entries.luganda: Obubondo obulowoozebwa bukolebwa ku mitindo gy’okuwangula nga obubondo obwaddala, naye waliwo enjawulo ezirabika.
entries.english: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences.

entries.luganda: Abantu b’okutiimu abatatela kuberawo bebatela okubera ng’abatukilwako ng’oyagadde okumanaya ekiffa ku kiwayilo
entries.english: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group.

entries.luganda: Batera okubeera n’obwetwaaze okusinga tiimu z'abantu abali awamu engeri tiimu zaabwe bwe ziyinza okusisinkana okusinziira ku njawulo mu biseera ekitasoboka kutegerekeka ababakulira.
entries.english: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management.

entries.luganda: Okubaawo kwa “ttiimu etalabika” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) kintu kya ttiimu elowoozebwako eky’anjawulo.
entries.english: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team.

entries.luganda: “Tiimu etarabika” kwekudukanya tiimu nga buli memba awa alipooti. Tiimu etalabika etekawo omutindo eri buli memba.
entries.english: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member.

entries.luganda: Lwaki ekitongole kyandiyagadde okuyita mu mitendera egirya obudde okuteekawo ekitongole ky'okusoma? Ekigendererwa ekimu eky'okuteekawo enkola z'okusoma mu kukolebwa kwe kuyiiya.
entries.english: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation.

entries.luganda: Ebikola bwebiba nga bikozzesedwa bulungi mu kitongole, akuyiya n’amagezi ibera bijja kukola bulungi
entries.english: When all available resources are effectively used across the functional departments of an organization, creativity and ingenuity can transpire.

entries.luganda: Nekyavaamu, omutendera gw'ekitongole ekikolera awamu okuwangula ebiziyiza kisobola okutuusa Ku mutendera omupya ogw'okuyiiya okuwereza obwetaavu bw'omuguzi.
entries.english: As a result, the process of an organization working together to overcome an obstacle can lead to a new innovative process to serve the customer's need.

entries.luganda: Nga ekitongole kyonna tekinnaba kubeera nga kireeta enkyukakyuka, abakikulembera be balina okukiteekawo nga kw’ogasse n'amagezi agagabanwa awamu n’okuyiga.
entries.english: Before an organization can be innovative, leadership must create a culture of innovation as well as shared knowledge and organizational learning.

entries.luganda: Malayika (2006), anyonyola enkola yaba Continaamu nga enkola ekozesebwa okuyamba ebitongole okutuuka ku buwanguzi obw’ekika ekyawagulu.
entries.english: Angel (2006), explains the Continuum approach as a method being used to help organizations reach a higher level of performance.

entries.luganda: Data w'okusoma Ku byobwongo aleeta obukakafu obwo buliwo obuwa okutegeera eri okunonyereza Ku bwongo, nolwekyo kikendeza Ku kifo ekyoku nonyereza, nekikifuula okuba ekituufu kyenyini.
entries.english: Neurobiological data provide physical evidence for a theoretical approach to the investigation of cognition. Therefore it narrows the research area and makes it much more exact.

entries.luganda: Enkolagana eriwo wakati w’ekireeta n’ekiva mu ndwadde ku bwongo n’eneyisa kiyamba ku bana sayansi mu kunonyereza kwaabwe.
entries.english: The correlation between brain pathology and behaviour supports scientists in their research.

entries.luganda: Kimanyiddwa ebbanga ddene nti ebika by’obukokosefu bw’obwongo, entiisa, ebiwundu, ne bizimba bikosa eneeyisa ne kuleetera enkyuuka mu nkola z’obwongo ezimu
entries.english: It has been known for a long time that different types of brain damage, traumas, lesions, and tumours affect behaviour and cause changes in some mental functions.

entries.luganda: Ono tekinologiya omupya atondebwawo buli kiseera atuukiriza okunoonyereza ku kikula ky’obwongo n’enkola y’ebintu ebitazulibwa nga ko.
entries.english: The rise of new technologies allows us to see and investigate brain structures and processes never seen before.

entries.luganda: Kino kituwa amawulire mangi nebikozesebwa okuzimba enkola enungamu ezinatuyamba okutegeera enkola mundowooza zaffe.
entries.english: This provides us with a lot of information and material to build simulation models which help us to understand processes in our mind.

entries.luganda: Ne wankubadde Al ayina engeri gya wulira ku kintu kyawulira, buli kintu kyonna kyamugaso mu kusoma ebintu by’okukozesa ebuma bi kali magezi, eneyisa ya byo, okuyiga awamu n’obukwata mpolaobuli mu kukozesa ebyuma ebyo
entries.english: Although AI has a strong connotation of science fiction, AI forms a very important branch of computer science, dealing with behavior, learning and intelligent adaptation in a machine.

entries.luganda: Okunoonyereza mu Al mulimu okukola ebyuuma okwanguya emirimu egyetaaga eneyisa ey'amagezi.
entries.english: Research in AI involves making machines to automate tasks that require intelligent behavior.

entries.luganda: Ebyokulabirako mulimu okufuga, okuteekateeka n’okulambika obusobozi bwokudamu ebibuuzo byabakasitoma ne byebalabye kwosa n’abasinze okuwandiika, edoboozi ne feesi.
entries.english: Examples include control, planning and scheduling, the ability to answer customer diagnoses and questions, as well as handwriting recognition, voice and face.

entries.luganda: Ebintu ebyo bifuuse bya mpisa za njawulo, ebitunulidde okufuna ebiyamba ku bizibu bye tusisinkana mu bulamu.
entries.english: Such things have become separate disciplines, which focus on providing solutions to real life problems.

entries.luganda: Ebyuma Bi Kalimagezi bikozzesebwa mu yenfuna n’obusubuzi, ebyobulamu, ebya yinginiya ne mu magye nga bwebizimbiddwa mu byuma bya komputa n’emizanyo gya vidiyo.
entries.english: The AI ​​system is now often used in the fields of economics, medicine, engineering and the military, as has been built in several home computer and video game software applications.

entries.luganda: Okulambula kintu kya maanyi nnyo mu bibiina. Bulijjo omusomesa yandyagadde okutwala abayizi be mu bifo we batandyetagisiza bbaasi.
entries.english: Field trips are a large part of any classroom. Quite often a teacher would love to take her students places to which a bus trip is not an option.

entries.luganda: Tekinologiya akuwa ekyokola wamu n’ebyobulambuzi. Abayizi basobola okulaba ku materekero g’ebikadiye, okukyalira amaterekero gebyenyanja, oba okwegomba ebibumbe ebirungi nga batudde mu kibiina.
entries.english: Technology offers the solution with virtual field trips. Students can look at museum artifacts, visit an aquarium, or admire beautiful art while sitting with their class.

entries.luganda: Okugabana okulambula nabalala nayo ngeri nnungi okufumitiriza kukulambula nokugabana ebyaliwo n’ebibiina byomumaaso.
entries.english: Sharing a field trip virtually is also a great way to reflect a on a trip and share experiences with future classes.

entries.luganda: Okugeza,buli mwaka abayizi okuva mu Bennet School e North Carolina bateeka olugendo lwabwe olwo mu kibuga ku mutimbagano, era buli mwaka, bongela okulongosamu naye ebikadde babitereka ne bibajjukiza.
entries.english: For example, each year students from Bennet School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the State Capital, each year the website gets remodeled, but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.

entries.luganda: Emitimbagano egiliko giyamba abayizi okutereeza empandiika yabwe. Nga abayizi bwebatera okuandika obumanyirivu ku mutimbagano n’ebigambo ebikontana ne bwebyogerwa, okubeerawo kw’abantu kubikyuusa.
entries.english: Blogs can also help improve student writing. While students often begin their blog experience with sloppy grammar and spelling, the presence of an audience generally changes that.

entries.luganda: Okuva nti abayizi be bakyasinze okuwulirwa, omuwadiisi awandiika ye tegerezza okusobola okwewala esobi awamu n’okuwabulwa.
entries.english: Since students are often the most critical audience, the blog writer begins to strive to improve writing to avoid criticism.

entries.luganda: Era ebyawandiikiddwa mu mawulire biraga nti “bakaka abayizi okumanya ebikwata ku nsi okubeetolola”. Kyetaagisa okumanya nti okuliisa ebyetaago byabawuliriza kiyamba era kikubiriza abayizi okubeera abagezi era ngabawoomesa ebintu (Toto,2004)
entries.english: Also blogging "forces students to become more savvy about the world around them." The need to feed the interest of the audience inspires students to be clever and interesting (Toto, 2004).

entries.luganda: Okuwandika kyekintu ekileta enkolagana okweyongelayo wakati w’abayizi mungeri esinga ekyanditusibwako mu masomero.
entries.english: Blogging is a tool that inspires collaboration, and encourages students to extend learning well beyond the traditional school day.

entries.luganda: Enkozesa enungi eya Intaneeti “esobola okuwa amanyi abayizi okusobola okwekenenya n’okwetegerza” nga bayita mukudamu ebikozesebwa ku intaneti, abayizi basobola okunyonyola ekifo kyabwe mu mbeera y’abalala nga bwebawandiika nga okumenya endaba yabwe ey’ensonga (Oravec, 2002).
entries.english: Appropriate use of blogs "can empower students to become more analytical and critical; through actively responding to Internet materials, students can define their positions in the context of others' writings as well as outline their own perspectives on particular issues (Oravec, 2002).

entries.luganda: Ottawa kyekibuga kya Canada ekikyamuffu, omuli enimi zonna era mulimu ebibumbe n’amakadiyizo agalaga ebyayita by’aCanada n’ebiliwo.
entries.english: Ottawa is Canada's charming, bilingual capital and features an array of art galleries and museums that showcase Canada's past and present.

entries.luganda: Nga weeyongeddeyo mu maserengeta ye wali ebibiro lya Niagara ate mu mambuka ye waka ew’obulungi bw’obutonde ewa Muskoka n’okweyongerayo.
entries.english: Farther south is Niagara Falls and the north is home to the untapped natural beauty of the Muskoka and beyond.

entries.luganda: Ebintu binno byona n’ebirala okuyita mu Ontario nga ekitwalibwa nga ebyomugaso mu Canadian eri ab’ebweru.
entries.english: All these things and more highlight Ontario as what is considered quintessentially Canadian by outsiders.

entries.luganda: Ebifo ebinene nga odda mu manbuka bibeerwamu kitono ate nga ebimu kyenkana nsiko omutebeerwa muntu.
entries.english: Large areas further north are quite sparsely populated and some is nearly uninhabited wilderness.

entries.luganda: Eri okugerageranya kw'abantu okwewunyiisa abangi: Waliwo abamerika bakaddugala banji ababeera mu US okusinga abatuuze be Canada.
entries.english: For a comparison of population that surprises many: There are more African Americans living in the US than there are Canadian citizens.

entries.luganda: Obuzinga bwomubukiika kkono bwa Africa buli mu semayanja wa Buyindi okuva ku lubalama lwennyanja olwa bukiika kkono bwa Africa.
entries.english: The East African Islands are in the Indian Ocean off the eastern coast of Africa.

entries.luganda: Madagascar y’ekyaasinze obunene, era ssemazinga yaayo bwekituuka kun solo zoomunsiko
entries.english: Madagascar is by far the biggest, and a continent on its own when it comes to wildlife.

entries.luganda: Obuzinga obutono obusinga bwamawanga agalina obwetwaze, oba agalina akakwate ku Bufaransa, era ngazimanyidwa nga lizooti zabiici ewalibwa ssente.
entries.english: Most of the smaller islands are independent nations, or associated with France, and known as luxury beach resorts.

entries.luganda: Abawarabu nabo baleeta obuyisiramu mu kitundu, era kyazanya ekifo kyamanyi mu Comoros ne Mayotte.
entries.english: The Arabs also brought Islam to the lands, and it took in a big way in the Comoros and Mayotte.

entries.luganda: Amaanyi ga Bulaaya n’obufuzi bwatandika mu kyasa kya 15, nga omulambuzi wa Portugo Vasco da Gama bwa zuula ekkubo mu Cape okuva e Bulaaya okutuuka e Buyindi
entries.english: European influence and colonialism began in the 15th century, as Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama found the Cape Route from Europe to India.

entries.luganda: Mu mambuka ekitundu kyasalwako aba Sahel, naye ate mu maserengeta ne mubukiika ddyo bwa semayanja wa Atlanta.
entries.english: In the north the region is bounded by the Sahel, and in the south and west by the Atlantic Ocean.

entries.luganda: Abakyaala. Kikubirizibwa eri abakyaala bonna abatambula okwogera nti bafumbo, ngatebafudeyo ku ngeri gyebayimiridde mu bufumbo bwabwe.
entries.english: Women: It is recommended that any women travellers say that they are married, regardless of actual marital status.

entries.luganda: Kiyamba okwambala empeta (eyo etaliyabeeyi nnyo.
entries.english: It is helpful to also wear a ring (just not one that looks too expensive.

entries.luganda: Abakazi balina okukizuula nti enjawukana mu byobuwangwa ziyinza okuvaamu kyewandiyise okutulugunya era sikyabulijjo okugobelerwa, okusikabmulwa omukono, nebirala.
entries.english: Women should realize that cultural differences may result in what they would consider harassment and it is not uncommon to be followed, grabbed by the arm, etc.

entries.luganda: Beera mwesimbu nga ogoba abasajja abakwaana, era tewelarikirira kukyosazeewo (oba ebyobuwangwa byawukana oba nedda, tekikukirizisa!).
entries.english: Be firm in turning down men, and don't be afraid to stand your ground (cultural differences or not, it doesn't make it ok!).

entries.luganda: Ekibuga ekipya ekya Casablanca kyatandikibwawo omuvubi w’ebyennyanja mu kyaasa ekya 10th BCE, ate kyakozesebwa aba Phoenicians, aba Roma ne ba Merenids nga ensalo yabwe gyebaayita Anfa.
entries.english: The modern city of Casablanca was founded by Berber fishermen in the 10th century BCE, and was used by the Phoenicians, Romans, and the Merenids as a strategic port called Anfa.

entries.luganda: Abapotigo bakisanyizawo nebaddamu nebakizimba wansi werinnya Casa Branca, ate oluvanyuma nebakisuulawo oluvanyuma lwokubwatuka mu 1755.
entries.english: The Portuguese destroyed it and rebuilt it under the name Casa Branca, only to abandon it after an earthquake in 1755.

entries.luganda: Kabaka wa Morocco yaddamu nazimba ekibuga ekya Daru I-Badya era nekitumibwa erinnya lya Casablanca okuva kubasuubuzi abasipanyoolo abatondawo enfo zokusuubuliramu.
entries.english: The Moroccan sultan rebuilt the city as Daru l-Badya and it was given the name Casablanca by Spanish traders who established trading bases there.

entries.luganda: Casablanca kyekifo ekikyasinze okunyuma okugulamu ebintu mu Morocco yonna.
entries.english: Casablanca is one of the least interesting places to shop in all of Morocco.

entries.luganda: Okulirana Medina enkadde kyangu okusanga ebifo awatundibwa ebyaamaguzi by’obuwangwa bya Morocco, okugeza tagines, Ensuwa, ebyaamaguzi ebyeddiba, hookahs, neekika kya Geegaw, naye byonna byabalambuzi
entries.english: Around the old Medina it's easy to find places selling traditional Moroccan goods, such as tagines, pottery, leather goods, hookahs, and a whole spectrum of geegaws, but it's all for the tourists.

entries.luganda: Goma kibuga kyabalambuzi mu Ggwanga lya Congo ewala mu buvanjuba kumpi ne Rwanda
entries.english: Goma is a tourist city of the Democratic Republic of Congo in the extreme east near Rwanda.

entries.luganda: Mu 2002 Goma yali asaanyiziddwawo omuliro gwo munsozi okuva mu Nyiragongo Volcano ogwa bikka enguudo zomu kibuga , naddala mu kibuga mu makkati.
entries.english: In 2002 Goma was destroyed by lava from the Nyiragongo volcano which buried most of the town’s streets, particularly the town centre.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde GOMA ntebenkevu, okukyaala kwonna wabweeru wa Goma kuteekeddwa okunoonyerezaako okutegeera embeera y’obutabanguko obulemedde mu mambuka g’essazza lye Kivu
entries.english: While Goma is reasonably safe, any visits outside of Goma should be researched to understand the state of the fighting that persists in the North Kivu province.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga era wewasinzirwa okulinya olusozi Nyiragongo oluwanzi lwomuliro awamu n’okulambula ku mazike okw’emiwendo emisaamusaamu mu Afirika.
entries.english: The city is also the base to climb the Nyiragongo volcano along with some of the cheapest Mountain Gorilla tracking in Africa.

entries.luganda: Osobola okukozesa bodaboda (oba boda boda taxi) okutuuka e Goma. Emiwendo gya bulijjo giri 500 Congolese Francs ku lugendo olumpi.
entries.english: You can use boda-boda (motorcycle taxi) to get around Goma. The normal (local) price is ~500 Congolese Francs for the short ride.

entries.luganda: Okugata naboluganda abatabelawoo, “Timbuktu” amazze nakozesebwa ng’ekigezesebwa okutegezza ebifo ebiri ewala.
entries.english: Combined with its relative inaccessibility, "Timbuktu" has come to be used as a metaphor for exotic, distant lands.

entries.luganda: Leero, Timbuktu kibuga ekyayavuwala, waddenga erinnya lye kyakola likifuula ekyobulambuzi, ate kirina n’ekisaawe ky’enyonyi.
entries.english: Today, Timbuktu is an impoverished town, although its reputation makes it a tourist attraction, and it has an airport.

entries.luganda: Mu 1990, kyagatibwa ku lukalala lw’ebiffo bye nsikirano ebiri mu bizibu, ku lw’entiisa y’enfuufu y’omuddungu.
entries.english: In 1990, it was added to the list of world heritage sites in danger, due to the threat of desert sands.

entries.luganda: Yali emu Ku kifo webayimilira ekikulu mu biseera bya Henry Louis Gates PBS eby'ewunyisa eby'enjawulo eb'ensi ya Africa.
entries.english: It was one of the major stops during Henry Louis Gates' PBS special Wonders of the African World.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga kyawukana nyo n’ebitundu ebilala mu nsi kubanga kilina nyo enddabika y’obuwalabu okusinga obufirika .
entries.english: The city is in stark contrast to the rest of the country's cities, because it has more of an Arabic flair than of an African.

entries.luganda: Etterekero ly’ebisoro elya Kruger Nansono paaka esangibwa mu mambuka gobukiika kkono bwamaserengeta ga Africa nga esarikiriza ne Mozambique mu buvanjuba, Zimbabwe mu mambuka , atte mumaserengeta wosanga omugga ogwa goonya.
entries.english: The Kruger National Park (KNP) lies in the north-east of South Africa and runs along the border of Mozambique in the east, Zimbabwe in the north, and the southern border is the Crocodile River.

entries.luganda: Ppaaka emalirawo ddala ekitundu kya 19,500 km² era egabanyizidwamu zooni endala entonotono, nga zonna ziwagira ebisolo byomunsiko byanjawulo.
entries.english: The park covers 19,500 km² and is divided in 14 different ecozones, each supporting different wildlife.

entries.luganda: Kyekimu ku bintu bye basinga okuliisizako amaaso mu maserengeta ga Africa era eteberezebwa okubeera nga yekutte bendera mu makumiro g'ebisolo mu maserengeta (SANParks).
entries.english: It is one of the main attractions of South Africa and it is considered the flagship of South African National Parks (SANParks).

entries.luganda: Era ngabwekiri mu kkumiro lyebisolo mu maserengeta ga Africa, wabeerawo ebikolebwamu bulijjo era wabawo ne ssente ezisasulwa okuyingira mu ppaaka.
entries.english: As with all South African National Parks, there are daily conservation and entry fees for the park.

entries.luganda: Kyalibade kyamugaso okugula Wild Card eyingiza mu bibangirize bye eby'obutonde ebimu mu South Africa oba byonna mu South Africa.
entries.english: It may also be beneficial for one to buy a Wild Card, which provides entry to either selections of parks in South Africa or all of the South African National Parks.

entries.luganda: Ekizinga kya Hong Kong kye kiwa amatwale ga Hong Kong erinya lyakyo era ky’ekiffo abalambuzi kyebatwala nga ekyenkizo.
entries.english: Hong Kong Island gives the territory of Hong Kong its name and is the place that many tourists regard as the main focus.

entries.luganda: Endabikka y’ebizimbe ebiwanvvu mu Hong Kong bifanana nga chatti emasamasa elabikilwa ku mazzi g’omwalo gu Victoria.
entries.english: The parade of buildings that make the Hong Kong skyline has been likened to a glittering bar chart that is made apparent by the presence of the waters of Victoria Harbour.

entries.luganda: Okulaba ebifo ebirungi mu Hong Kong, lekka ekizinga emabega ogende ku mwalo gwa Kowloon oguli oluda olulala.
entries.english: To get the best views of Hong Kong, leave the island and head for the Kowloon waterfront opposite.

entries.luganda: Enkulaakulana jesinga mu Hong Kong mu kibuga ekikulaakunye kirimu omujjuzo gwa bantu ku taka lye beddiza mu maserengeta ge nyanja.
entries.english: The great majority of Hong Kong Island's urban development is densely packed on reclaimed land along the northern shore.

entries.luganda: Kino ky’ekikiffo abangeleza kyebawamba, era bwobela ononya ekifo ky’okufunilamu obujjulizi, wano walungi okutandikilako
entries.english: This is the place the British colonisers took as their own and so if you are looking for evidence of the territory's colonial past, this is a good place to start.

entries.luganda: Sundarbans bwe butonde bwamangruuvu obukyaasinze okuba obunene munsi yonna, butudde ku bugazi bwa kilomita kinaana (mailo 50) okuyingira mu Bangladeshi ne munda mu Buyindi okuva ku lubalama.
entries.english: The Sundarbans are the largest littoral mangrove belt in the world, stretching 80 km (50 mi) into the Bangladeshi and Indian hinterland from the coast.

entries.luganda: Ekifo kya Sundarbans kya salibwawo nga ekya UNESCO World Heritage Site.ekintu ekimu mu kibira mu matwale ga buyindi kiyitibwa Sundarbans National Park.
entries.english: The Sundarbans has been declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The part of the forest within Indian territory is called Sundarbans National Park.

entries.luganda: Ebibira tebili bya bitoogo byoka wadde bilimu ebimu ku ebisigalila by’ekumilo ly’ebisoro elyaali limalawo Gangetic plain.
entries.english: The forests aren't just mangrove swamps though — they include some of the last remaining stands of the mighty jungles which once covered the Gangetic plain.

entries.luganda: Ekifo kya Sandarban kitudde mu kitundu kya 3,850 km², era ekitundu kimu kubisatu kili mu mazzi oba etakka lyomunnyanja.
entries.english: The Sundarbans cover an area of 3,850 km², of which about one-third is covered in water/marsh areas.

entries.luganda: Okuva 1966 zi Sundarbans zibadde kya bulambuzi ky'ebisoro, era kiteberezebwa nti kati waliwo engo z'omulubiri 400 n'empeewo ezirabidwaako nga 30,000 mu kitundu.
entries.english: Since 1966 the Sundarbans have been a wildlife sanctuary, and it is estimated that there are now 400 Royal Bengal tigers and about 30,000 spotted deer in the area.

entries.luganda: Baasi ziva Ku sitenseni ya disitulikiti wezegattira (okuyita mumugga) okuyita mu lunaku, yadde ezisinga, naddala ezo ezidda mu buvanjuba be Jakar/ Bumthang zisimbula wakati wa 06:30 ne 07:30.
entries.english: Buses depart the inter-district bus station (across the river) throughout the day, though most, especially those heading to the east and Jakar/Bumthang leave between 06:30 and 07:30.

entries.luganda: Engeri baasi eziyita mu masaza gye zitera okubeera enzijuvu, kyetaagisa nogula tikiiti nga enaku zikyaali.
entries.english: As the inter-district buses are often full, it is advisable to purchase a ticket a few days in advance.

entries.luganda: Disitulikiti ezisinga ziwerezebwa bu bbaasi bwabaJapan obutono, ngazino ziwaa emirembe ate nnyuvu.
entries.english: Most districts are served by small Japanese Coaster Buses, which are comfortable and sturdy.

entries.luganda: Emmotoka ya lukale yemu ku ngeri enyangu atte nga yeyagaza okugenda ku bifo ebyo kumpi nga Paro (Nu 150) ne Punakha (Nu 200).
entries.english: Shared taxis are a quick and comfortable means to travel to nearby places, such as Paro (Nu 150) and Punakha (Nu 200).

entries.luganda: Olutindo olwa Oyapoc lwa waya. Luviira ddala ku mugga gwa Oyapock ne lugatta ebibuga okuli ekya Oiapoque mu Brazil ne Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock mu French Guiana.
entries.english: The Oyapock River Bridge is a cable-stayed bridge. It spans the Oyapock River to link the cities of Oiapoque in Brazil and Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock in French Guiana.

entries.luganda: Eminaala ebiri gyambukira ddala mu buwanvu bwa mita 83, zino zibeera mita 378 mu buwanvu era zirimu amakubo abiri agayina obugazi bwa 3.50 m.
entries.english: The two towers rise to a height of 83 meters, it's 378 meters long and it has two lanes of 3.50 m wide.

entries.luganda: Wansi wokuyunga waliwo mita kumi na taano.Okuzzimba kwakomenkelezzddwa mu gw’omunana mu mwaka 2011 naye olutindo telwaggulwawo paka nga March 2017
entries.english: The vertical clearance under the bridge is 15 meters. Construction was completed in August 2011, it didn't open to traffic until March 2017.

entries.luganda: Olutindo luteekatekedwa okukolera ddala mu gw’omwenda 2017, abakebela ebyemisolo e Brazil basuubirwa okuba nag bamaze.
entries.english: The bridge is scheduled to be fully operational in September 2017, when the Brazilian customs checkpoints are expected to be finished.

entries.luganda: Aba Guarani kekabinja akaleeta abantu abeesenza mu kati kyetuyita obukiika ddyo bwa Paraguay, ngababeera ngabayizzi abateeka mu nkola ebyokulima.
entries.english: The Guaraní were the most significant indigenous group inhabiting what is now Eastern Paraguay, living as semi-nomadic hunters who also practised subsistence agriculture.

entries.luganda: Ekitundu ky’e Chaco gaali maka g’ebibinja ebirala by’amawanga gakasangwaawo nga Guaycurú ne Payaguá, abawonawo olw’okuyigga, okukungaanya n’okuvuba.
entries.english: The Chaco region was home to other groups of indigenous tribes such as the Guaycurú and Payaguá, who survived by hunting, gathering and fishing.

entries.luganda: Mukyaasa ekye 16 Paraguay nga bweyitibwa mu butongole “Nantamegwa wetwale lya Indies” yazalibwa oluvanyuma lw'okulumbibwa abawanguzi ba sipanisi nekibinja jenzalwa.
entries.english: In the 16th century Paraguay, formerly called "The Giant Province of the Indies", was born as a result of the encounter of Spanish conquerors with the native indigenous groups.

entries.luganda: Aba Spain batandika okweddiza obufuzi bwamawanga okumalira ddala ebyaasa bisatu.
entries.english: The Spaniards started the colonization period which lasted for three centuries.

entries.luganda: Oluvanyuma lwomusingi gwa Asuncion mu 1537, Paraguay esobodde okukuuma ekikula kyayo nebijogerako.
entries.english: Since the foundation of Asunción in 1537, Paraguay has managed to keep a lot of its indigenous character and identity.

entries.luganda: Egwanga ly’Argentina limanyiddwa nyo okubera ne tiimu y’omuzanyo gwa polo esinga mu nsi yona
entries.english: Argentina is well known for having one of the best polo teams and players in the world.

entries.luganda: Empaka ezisinga obunene ez'omwaka ziyinda mu Ntenvu (Decemba) mu kisawe Las Cañitas.
entries.english: The largest tournament of the year takes place in December at the polo fields in Las Cañitas.

entries.luganda: Okuwakanya okutonotono n’enzanya bilabibwaako wanno ebiseera ebimu mumwaaka.
entries.english: Smaller tournaments and matches can also be seen here at other times of the year.

entries.luganda: Ebikwatagana namawulire ku mpaka n’awokugula tikiti zemipiira gya Polo, kebera ku Asociacion Argentina de Polo.
entries.english: For news on tournaments and where to buy tickets for polo matches, check Asociacion Argentina de Polo.

entries.luganda: Ensimbi za Fakland ziyitibwa pawunda(FKP) era ngomuwendo gwazo gwenkanira ddala nepawunda zebungereza (GBP).
entries.english: The official Falklands currency is the Falkland pound (FKP) whose value is set equivalent to that of one British pound (GBP).

entries.luganda: Sente zisobola kuwanyisibwa ku tterekero lya sente mu bizinga ebisangibwa mu Stanley nga osaze okuva mu bukiika kono w’etterekero lya FIC.
entries.english: Money can be exchanged at the only bank in the islands which is located in Stanley across from the FIC West store.

entries.luganda: Sente za Bungereza eza Pound zikirizibwa buli wamu ku bizinga ne kalata za Credit eza Stanley ne Sente za Amerika nazzo zikirizibwa.
entries.english: British pounds will generally be accepted anywhere in the islands and within Stanley credit cards and United States dollars are also often accepted.

entries.luganda: Ku bizinga ebitwetoolodde, kalata za credit ziyinza okugaanibwa, newankubade ssente z’abamgereza ne eza Amerika ziyinza okukirizibwa. Sooka webuuze ku babnnannyini ku nsasula ekirizibwa.
entries.english: On the outlying islands credit cards will probably not be accepted, although British and United States currency may be taken; check with the owners in advance to determine what is an acceptable payment method.

entries.luganda: Kumpi tekisoboka okuvunnisa sente za Falklands wabweeru weekizinga, ku lw’ekyo vunnisa sente nga tonnava ku kizinga
entries.english: It is nearly impossible to exchange Falklands currency outside of the islands, so exchange money prior to leaving the islands.

entries.luganda: Okusinzira nti Montevideo eri mu maserengeta ga Equator, embeera y’obudde ebera ya musana nga ekitundutundu ekyo’mumambuka embeera y’obudde ya butiti. Kyekimu nga okikyusiza.
entries.english: Since Montevideo is south of the Equator, it is summer there when it's winter in the Northern Hemisphere and vice versa.

entries.luganda: Montevideo eri mu nsi ezokerera; mu myezi gy’omusana, embeera zobudde ezisukira ddala ku +30°C zibeerayo nyo.
entries.english: Montevideo is in the subtropics; in the summer months, temperatures above +30°C are common.

entries.luganda: Ebisera by’omuzira bitela okubuzabuza, embera y’obudde tetela kukwata muzira, naye empewo enyogoga nyo n’okusinga bwekyandibadde
entries.english: The winter can be deceptively chilly: temperatures rarely go below freezing, but the wind and humidity combine to make it feel colder than what the thermometer says.

entries.luganda: Tewali wo biro bya “nkuba” “n'ebikalu” eby'enkalakalira: obungi bw'enkuba busigala nga kumpi bwebumu okuyita mu mwaka.
entries.english: There are no particular "rainy" and "dry" seasons: the amount of rain stays roughly the same throughout the year.

entries.luganda: Yadde nga ensolo nyingi zamanyiilwa okulaba abantu mukuumiro lino, ebyomunsiko bisigala byamunsiiko era tolina biriisa oba okubisumbuwa.
entries.english: Though many of the animals in the park are used to seeing humans, the wildlife is nonetheless wild and should not be fed or disturbed.

entries.luganda: Okusinziira ku bakwasaganya paaka, sigala wakiri yaadi oba mita100 okuva ku zi bbeeya n'emisege era ne mita oba ebigere 25 okuva ku nsolo endala ez'omunsiko.
entries.english: According to park authorities, stay at least 100 yards/meters away from bears and wolves and 25 yards/meters from all other wild animals!

entries.luganda: Newankubadde zitunula nga empombefu emisu, emmese, enjaba, embogo, engabi, bbeya kyenkana buli bisolo ebigazi bilumba.
entries.english: No matter how docile they may look, bison, elk, moose, bears, and nearly all large animals can attack.

entries.luganda: Buli mwaka, ebikumi n’ebikumi by’abagenyi bifuna ebisago kubanga tebamanyi kwesuula akabanga. Ebisolo bino binene ate bya munsiko, ate bya mutawaana, kati mubiwe akabanga.
entries.english: Each year, dozens of visitors are injured because they didn't keep a proper distance. These animals are large, wild, and potentially dangerous, so give them their space.

entries.luganda: Mpozi nekilala, mwegendeleza obuwoowo busikiriza n’ebisoro byomunsiko ebirala, n’olweekyo wewale okwefuuyira oba okufumba emere eyakawoowo era ekifo kikuume nga kiyonjo.
entries.english: In addition, be aware that odors attract bears and other wildlife, so avoid carrying or cooking odorous foods and keep a clean camp.

entries.luganda: Apia kyekibuga ekikulu ekya Samoa. Ekibuga kino kili ku kazinga ka Upolu era kiliko abantu abakka wansi wa 40,000.
entries.english: Apia is the capital of Samoa. The town is on the island of Upolu and has a population of just under 40,000.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga Apia kyatandikibwa mu mwaka 1850 naye kyafuka ekibuga ekikulu mu Sanoa mu mwaka gwa 1959.
entries.english: Apia was founded in the 1850s and has been the official capital of Samoa since 1959.

entries.luganda: Amaato wegawumulila kekafo akatamanyidwa naval weyatuuka nga amaato omusanvu okava e Germany, US, ne Britain gagaanyi okuvaayo.
entries.english: The harbor was the site of an infamous naval standoff in 1889 when seven ships from Germany, the US, and Britain refused to leave the harbor.

entries.luganda: Amaato gonna gabbila, okujako erimu ery’omuvuzi w’eBritain. Kumpi obulamu bwabantu 200 bwagenda.
entries.english: All the ships were sunk, except for one British cruiser. Nearly 200 American and German lives were lost.

entries.luganda: Mubiseera byokulwanilira amefuga ebyategekebwa ekibiina kya May, olukungaana Lwe ddembe mu kibuga lwavaamu okutta kwa chief omukulu enyo Tupua Tamasese Lealcfi III.
entries.english: During the struggle for independence organised by the Mau movement, a peaceful gathering in the town resulted in the killing of the paramount chief Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.

entries.luganda: Waliyo beaches nyingi, okuva Auckland lwe ya yawula emyalo ebbiri. Ezikyasinze okumanyika ziri mubifo bisatu.
entries.english: There are many beaches, due to Auckland's straddling of two harbours. The most popular ones are in three areas.

entries.luganda: Embalama zenyanja ezomumambuka (Mumambuka ga Disitulikiti za Harbour) ziri ku semayanja Pacific era nga yegolola okuva ku long bay mumambuka ne Devonport mumaserengeta.
entries.english: North Shore beaches (in North Harbour district) are on the Pacific Ocean and stretch from Long Bay in the north to Devonport in the south.

entries.luganda: Embalama zonna ezisinga za musenyu naye nga nnungi okuwugira mu atte nga ezisinga zirina ebisiikirize ebivva ku miti jja pohutukawa.
entries.english: They are almost all sandy beaches with safe swimming, and most have shade provided by pohutukawa trees.

entries.luganda: Biici za Tamaki Drive zili ku lubalama lwa Waitemata mu bifo bya Missoni Bay ne Heliers wakati mu Auckland ebikulakulanye.
entries.english: Tamaki Drive beaches are on the Waitemata Harbour, in the upmarket suburbs of Mission Bay and St Heliers in Central Auckland.

entries.luganda: Zino embalama zenyanja olumu ziba zijudde abamaka n’oluseregende lwamaduuka amalungi nga okuwuga kuliko obukkumi obwa gulugulu
entries.english: These are sometimes-crowded family beaches with a good range of shops lining the shore. Swimming is safe.

entries.luganda: Bbiya owokukyalo akyasinze ye ‘namba emu’, ssi bbiiya ali awo omuzibu, naye musuffu era wadekende. Bbiya omulala owokukaalo ayitibwa “Manta”.
entries.english: The main local beer is 'Number One', it is not a complex beer, but pleasant and refreshing. The other local beer is called "Manta".

entries.luganda: Waliyo emyenge gye Bufalansa mingi, naye egya New Zealand n'egya Australia gyanditambula okusinga.
entries.english: There are many French wines to be had, but the New Zealand and Australian wines might travel better.

entries.luganda: Amazzi agava mu butaapu obwabulijjo malungi okunywa, naye amazzi agali mu bukyupa mangu okufuna bwoba olimu okutya.
entries.english: The local tap water is perfectly safe to drink, but bottled water is easy to find if you are fearful.

entries.luganda: Eri aba Australia, ekilowoozo kya, kkaawa “wa mata n'ejovu” ava bweru w'egwanga. Omuddugavu omutono ayitibwa “espresso”, cappuccino ye bamujuza amata amakwafu (ssi jovu), ate chai bagaba ataliimu mata.
entries.english: For Australians, the idea of 'flat white' coffee is foreign. A short black is 'espresso', cappuccino comes heaped high with cream (not froth), and tea is served without milk.

entries.luganda: Kyokuleeti ayokya ali kumutindo gw’eBelgium. Ebyookunywa byebibala bya beeyi naye bisukulumu.
entries.english: The hot chocolate is up to Belgian standards. Fruit juices are pricey but excellent.

entries.luganda: Okulambula kungi kukolebwa omwaka gwona nga balambuzi bazze okulaba enjazzi za munyanja, atte ebisago okuva mu kulambula kunno tebisangibwa nyo kiri awo.
entries.english: Many trips to the reef are made all year around, and injuries due to any of these causes on the reef are rare.

entries.luganda: Neera, twala okuwabulwa okuva mubobuyinza, gondera obubonero bwona era oseeyo omutima ku kulabulwa okw'okwekuuma.
entries.english: Still, take advice from authorities, obey all signs, and pay close attention to safety warnings.

entries.luganda: Ebyenyanja ekikula kya bokisi bisangibwa kumbalama zenyanja n’okumpi n’emigga okuva mu mwezi gwe 10 okutuuka mu gwokuna mumambuka mu mwaka gwa 1770. Tezitera kusangibwa wabweru mubudde obulala.
entries.english: Box jellyfish occur near beaches and near river estuaries from October to April north of 1770. They can occasionally be found outside these times.

entries.luganda: Ebyenyanja ebinene gyebili naye tebitela kulumba bantu, ebisinga bitela okutya abantu era bwebibalaba nga byekweka
entries.english: Sharks do exist, however they rarely attack humans. Most sharks are scared of humans and would swim away.

entries.luganda: Goonya zo mumazzi agomunnyo tezibeera nnyo mu ssemayanja, sisinga kubeera mu mugga mu mambuka okuva mu Rockhampton.
entries.english: Saltwater Crocodiles do not actively live in the ocean, their primary habitat is in river estuaries north from Rockhampton.

entries.luganda: Okwewandisa amangu kiwa omutambuze eddembe mu birowozo nti bajja kufuna webasobola okwebaka bwebanatuuka gyebagenda.
entries.english: Booking in advance gives the traveller peace of mind that they will have somewhere to sleep once they arrive at their destination.

entries.luganda: Abalambuuzi bulijjo babeera ne diilu n’amawooteri agamu, wadde nga osobola okwelondera awokusula nga kampu, ng’oyise mu bitongole byentambula.
entries.english: Travel agents often have deals with specific hotels, although you may find it possible to book other forms of accommodation, like camping grounds, through a travel agent.

entries.luganda: Ab’ebyentabula batela okuwa ettu elilimu ekyenkya, enteekateeka z’entabula amagenda n’amaddaku kisaawe oba n’enyonyi ezigatta engendo ne’amattu ga woteeri.
entries.english: Travel agents usually offer packages that include breakfast, transportation arrangements to/from the airport or even combined flight and hotel packages.

entries.luganda: Basobola okukukwatira ekifo bwoba nga weetaaga akadde okulowooza ku mukisa oba okwekkaanya ebiwandiiko ebirala ku lugendo lwo (okugeza . visa).
entries.english: They can also hold the reservation for you if you need time to think about the offer or procure other documents for your destination (e.g. visa).

entries.luganda: Enongosereza yonna oba okusaba kulina okuyita mu bakola kubyentabula okusooka so si kukolagana na wooteri.
entries.english: Any amendments or requests though should be coursed through the travel agent first and not directly with the hotel.

entries.luganda: Kulw’ebikujjuko ebimu, abasinga okubyetanila ku bivulu bye ndongo basalawo kusulawo, abamu bakitwala nga kitundu kinene mu kuyiga.
entries.english: For some festivals, the vast majority of the attendants to music festivals decide to camp on site, and most attendants consider it a vital part of the experience.

entries.luganda: Bw'oba oyagala ku sembera awali ekikolwa olina okuyingira nga bukyaali okufuna ekifo ewokuwumulira ekirilanye enyimba.
entries.english: If you want to be close to the action you're going to have to get in early to get a camping site close to the music.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde nti omuziki ku siteegi enkulu gwandiba ngagwabadde guwedde,, wayinza okubaawo ebitundu by'ebikujuko ebinyiza okusigala nga bizanya enyimba okutuusa ekiro muttumbi.
entries.english: Remember that even though music on the main stages may have finished, there may be sections of the festival that will keep playing music until late into the night.

entries.luganda: Ebikujuko ebisinga birina ebifo ewokukunganirwa eri abenju wamu nabaana abato.
entries.english: Some festivals have special camping areas for families with young children.

entries.luganda: Bwoba Osala amambuka ga Baltic mu bunyogovu, kebera awali akasanduuko, kubanga okuyita mu balaafu kiletera ddala amalobozi agentiisa eri abo abasinga okukosebwa.
entries.english: If crossing the Northern Baltic in winter, check the cabin location, as going through ice causes quite horrible noise for those most affected.

entries.luganda: Oluwumula lwomutuukirivu Petersburg lubelamu n’okutambula mu kibuga, abalambuzi tebetaggaa kufuna zi Visa (kebera amateeka).
entries.english: Saint Petersburg cruises include time in town. Cruise passengers are exempted from visa requirements (check the terms).

entries.luganda: Ewazanyirwa zaala bayiiya byonna okufunira ddala ku ssente nobudde obukozesebwa abantu. Amadiriisa namassaawa tegatera kubeeramu, era ewafulumirwa watera kubeera wazibu okuzuulwa.
entries.english: Casinos typically make many efforts to maximize time and money spent by guests. Windows and clocks are usually absent, and exits can be hard to find.

entries.luganda: Bulijjo balina emmere eyenjawulo, ebyokunywa nebisanyusa okusobola okukumira abagenyi mu mbeera ennungi, era wamu nokubakumira mu kifo.
entries.english: They usually have special food, drink and entertainment offers, to keep guests in a good mood, and keep them at the premise.

entries.luganda: Ebifo ebimu bigaba omwenge gwo obwerere. Newankubadde, obutamiivu buleeta obutalowoza bulungi, bonna abazanyi ba zzaala bamanyi omugaso gw'okwewala okunywa omwenge.
entries.english: Some venues offer alcoholic beverages on the house. However, drunkenness impairs judgement, and all good gamblers know the importance of staying sober.

entries.luganda: Omuntu yenna agenda okuvugira mu banga oba wagulu wensozi balina okulowooza ku muzila, balafu, oba obutiti.
entries.english: Anyone who's going to drive at high latitudes or over mountain passes should consider the possibility of snow, ice, or freezing temperatures.

entries.luganda: Ku makubo ag'omuzira ne balaafu, okukuubagana kutono era tosobola kuvuga nga eyali ku kolaasi omwerere.
entries.english: On icy and snowy roadways, friction is low and you cannot drive as if you were on bare asphalt.

entries.luganda: Ekiseera kya blizzards, omuzira ogumala okwigwisa mu kasera akatono ddala.
entries.english: During blizzards, enough snow to get you stuck can fall in very little time.

entries.luganda: Okulaba kuyinza okusiikirizibwa n'omuzira ogukunta oba ogugwa oba olufu oba bbalaafu nga ali ku ddirisa ly'emmotoka.
entries.english: Visibility may also be restricted by falling or blowing snow or by condensation or ice on vehicle windows.

entries.luganda: Mu ngeri endala, bbalaafu n'embeera z'omuzira bya bulijjo munsi ezisinga obungi, n'ekkubo ly'eyongerayo nga telitawanyiziddwa omwaka gwonna.
entries.english: On the other hand, icy and snowy conditions are normal in many countries, and traffic goes on mostly uninterrupted all year round.

entries.luganda: Safari oba osanga kye kyobulambuzi ekikyasinze mu Africa era kye kinyuvu eri abalambuzi abasinga.
entries.english: Safaris are perhaps the greatest tourism draw in Africa and the highlight for many visitors.

entries.luganda: Ekigambo safari mululimi olwa bulijjo kwe kutambula egendo empanvu okulaba ebisolo ebyenjawulo mu afirika,naddala mu bintu eya kilagala.
entries.english: The term safari in popular use refers to overland travel to view the stunning African wildlife, particularly on savanna.

entries.luganda: Ebisolo ebimu nga enjovu n’entulege zitela okusemberela emotoka, era ebikozesebwa okukwata ebifananyi bisobola okukuwa ekifananyi ekirungi.
entries.english: Some animals, such as elephants and giraffes, tend to approach closely to cars and standard equipment will allow good viewing.

entries.luganda: Empologoma, engabi n'engo ebiseera ebisinga zibeera n'ensonyi era kyetaagisa ebitangaaza okusobola okuzeetegereza obulungi.
entries.english: Lions, cheetahs and leopards are sometimes shy and you will see them better with binoculars.

entries.luganda: Okulambuula ebisolo n’ensiko(okumanyidwa nga “Bushwalk”, “okulinya ensozi”, oba “okutambuza ebigere”) kulimu okulinnya ebiwonvu okumala essaawa oba ennaku.
entries.english: A walking safari (also called a "bush walk", "hiking safari", or going "footing") consists of hiking, either for a few hours or several days.

entries.luganda: Empaka zabalema zijja kubeerawo okuva nga 24 ogw’omunaana paka nga 5 ogw’omwenda 2021. Emikolo ejimu jijja kubeerawonna mubitundu by’aJapan.
entries.english: The Paralympics will take place from 24 August to 5 September 2021. Some events will be held in other locations throughout Japan.

entries.luganda: Tokyo kijja kubeera ekibuga kyoka mu Eezia ekyatuuza Olympics ebbiri, mu 1964.
entries.english: Tokyo will be the only Asian city to have hosted two summer Olympics, having hosted the games in 1964.

entries.luganda: Singa weewandisa mangu ekifo kyo mu nyonyi n’okusula mu 2020 nga okwongezaayo tekunnalangibwa, oyinza okwesanga mu mbeera enzibu.
entries.english: If you booked your flights and accommodation for 2020 before the postponement was announced, you may have a tricky situation.

entries.luganda: Amateeka agasazaamu ga njawulo, naye mugwokusatu ngagugwako okusazaamu okusinga okwekuusa ku kirwadde kya senyiga omukambwe tekwasuuka omwezi ogwomusanvu 2020, ebyemisinde wegyali gyiteredwayo.
