entries.english: It can be customized to make it easy to read and also with as much or little color as desired.

entries.luganda: Obulwadde bw'okuwugulala “bulwadde bwa mu bwongo. Obubonero bwabwo omuli okumala gakola ebintu, okutaataganyizibwa amangu n'okubeera ow'eddalu n'okuba n'manyi amangi.
entries.english: Attention Deficit Disorder "is a neurological syndrome whose classic defining triad of symptoms including impulsivity, distractibility, and hyperactivity or excess energy".

entries.luganda: Tekiri nti mulema ku bwongo, naye kizibu mu kuyiga, kibeerawo mu baana basatu oba batano ku buli kikumi. Mu Bamerika, kisubirwa okubeera mu baana obukadde bubiri.
entries.english: It is not a learning disability, it is a learning disorder; it "affects 3 to 5 percent of all children, perhaps as many as 2 million American children".

entries.luganda: Abaana abalina obuzibu bwa ADD, basaanga obuzibu bungi okuwulira n’okussaayo omutima mu ssomero, naye basobola okussaayo omutima eri ebintu byebagala nga okuzanya emizanyo, okulaba katuni oba okuwandika enyiriri nga temuli kutonnyeza.
entries.english: Children with ADD have a hard time focusing on things like school work, but they can concentrate on things they enjoy doing like playing games or watching their favorite cartoons or writing sentences without punctuation.

entries.luganda: Bano abaana balinga abeteeka mu buzibu kubanga betaba mu mpisa ezakatyabaga, balwana ne bawakanya ab’obuyinza mu kusobola okukozesa obwongo bwabwe olwokuba obwongo bwabwe tebusobola kukozesebwa mu nkola eya bulijjo.
entries.english: These children tend to get into a lot of trouble, because they "engage in risky behaviors, get into fights, and challenge authority" in order to stimulate their brain, since their brain can not be stimulated by normal methods.

entries.luganda: ADD, oba obutateleera wamu buleeta wo enjawukana mu nkwatagana n’abemyaaka egimu kubanga abaana abalala tebasobola kutegeela lwaaki bakola byebakola oba lwaaki bogera ebigambo engeri jebabyogera oba nti enkula jebakulamu eyawukana.
entries.english: ADD affects relationships with other peers because other children can not understand why they act the way that they do or why they spell they way they do or that their maturity level is different.

entries.luganda: Nga obusobozi okufuna okumanya n'okuyiga byakyuuka mungeri nga bwe kyayogerwa waggulu womuwendo ewasinzilirirwa okumanya wekwafunirwa byakyuka.
entries.english: As the ability to obtain knowledge and to learn changed in such a way as mentioned above the base rate at which knowledge was obtained changed.

entries.luganda: Engeri gyebafuna mu amawulire yayawukana. Tebakozesa manyi mangi naye esira balisa kubyawandiikibwa.
entries.english: The approach to obtaining information was different. No longer did pressure reside within individual recall, but the ability to recall text became more of a focus.

entries.luganda: Ekyamazima obwetwaaze bwakola enkyukakyuka ey’omugundu ku nkwata y’ebyenjigiriza n'okufuna amagezi.
entries.english: In essence, the Renaissance made a significant change in the approach to learning and the dissemination of knowledge.

entries.luganda: Nga ogyeko ebisolo ebirala, amazike tezikyakozesa mikono nga zijitambuza oba nga jirimu ekintu kyonna oba nga jewuuba mu miti.
entries.english: Unlike other primates, hominids no longer use their hands in locomotion or bearing weight or swinging through the trees.

entries.luganda: Ebigere n’emikono gya zikimpanze zifanagana mu kigero, n’obuwanvu, okujjukira omugaso gw’engalo okuwanirira obuzito mu binywa okutambula.
entries.english: The chimpanzee's hand and foot are similar in size and length, reflecting the hand's use for bearing weight in knuckle walking.

entries.luganda: Omukono gwomuntu mumpi kukigere, era guyina amagumba agatewetamu.
entries.english: The human hand is shorter than the foot, with straighter phalanges.

entries.luganda: Amagumba g’omumikono agakulidde mu njazi emyaaka obukadde bubiri ku busatu egiyise gilaga enkyuukakyuuka mu bukugu bwomukono okuva mukutambula nebudda mukwefuula.
entries.english: Fossil hand bones two million to three million years old reveal this shift in specialization of the hand from locomotion to manipulation.

entries.luganda: Abantu abamu bakkiriza nti okubeera mu birooto ebijingirire ebinji ebiseera ebimu kikooya
entries.english: Some people believe that experiencing many artificially induced lucid dreams often enough can be very exhausting.

entries.luganda: Ensonga y’ekyebyewunyo kino bye birooto ebibeera nga tebyerabirwa ebiwanvuya obudde wakati wa ebiseera bya REM.
entries.english: The main reason for this phenomenon is the result of the lucid dreams expanding the length of time between REM states.

entries.luganda: Nga byewekaanga ekiro bikeendera, embeera eno eyokweebaka n’osimatuka omubiri gwo negudawo efuuka yalugero era n’etabalirwa ng’ekizibu.
entries.english: With fewer REMs per night, this state in which you experience actual sleep and your body recovers becomes infrequent enough to become a problem.

entries.luganda: Kino kikoya nyo ng’omuntu okuzzukuka buli ddakika abili oba assatu okulaba telefayina
entries.english: This is just as exhausting as if you were to wake up every twenty or thirty minutes and watch TV.

entries.luganda: Obukose businziira ku mirundi emeka jooloota ekiro.
entries.english: The effect is dependent on how often your brain attempts to lucidly dream per night.

entries.luganda: Ebintu tebyabagendera bulungi, abayitale mu mambuka ga Afrika okuva kuntandikwa. Mubbanga lya sabiiti emu yokka okuva nga yitale etaddewo olutalo, nga 10 ogwomukaaga, 1940, abangereza ba Hussars 11th baali bawambye ekijukizo kyomwalo Capuzzo mu Libya.
entries.english: Things did not go well for the Italians in North Africa almost from the start. Within a week of Italy's declaration of war on June 10, 1940, the British 11th Hussars had seized Fort Capuzzo in Libya.

entries.luganda: Mululumba lwamaserengeta ga Bardia, abangereza bakutte yinginiya mumatwale ga yitale agekkumi, Generaali Lastucci.
entries.english: In an ambush east of Bardia, the British captured the Italian Tenth Army's Engineer-in-Chief, General Lastucci.

entries.luganda: Nga 28 Ssebaseka, Marshal Italo Balbo, Governor-Generalwa Libya era omusika wa Mussoliniyatibwa omuliro nga atuuka mu Tobruk.
entries.english: On June 28, Marshal Italo Balbo, the Governor-General of Libya and apparent heir to Mussolini, was killed by friendly fire while landing in Tobruk.

entries.luganda: Omuzzanyo gw’okukola fensi guzanyibwa ku mitendera mingi okuvva ku bayizzi mu matendekero agawaggulu okutukka ku bakaffulu mu mpaka z’olympics
entries.english: The modern sport of fencing is played at many levels, from students learning at a university to professional and Olympic competition.

entries.luganda: Omuzzanyo gusinga kuzanyibwa bantu babiri mu'ngeli yokulwaana, fensa omu nga alwaanisa omulala.
entries.english: The sport is primarily played in a duel format, one fencer dueling another.

entries.luganda: Goofa kekazanyo abazanyi mwebakozesa emiti okukuba emipiira nejigwa mu binnya.
entries.english: Golf is a game in which players use clubs to hit balls into holes.

entries.luganda: Ounya ekumi n'omunaana buzanyiba mu luzannya lumu, abazanyi batela okutandikila ku kannya akasooka nebamaliliza ku kanya akekumi n'omunaana.
entries.english: Eighteen holes are played during a regular round, with players usually starting on the first hole on the course and finishing on the eighteenth.

entries.luganda: Omuzanyi asinga okukola enumba nensaza entono eza tiimu okumaliriza olusoma yawangula.
entries.english: The player who takes the fewest strokes, or swings of the club, to complete the course wins.

entries.luganda: Akazanyo kazanyibwa ku muddo, n’omuddo ogwetolodde ekituli gusayibwa negubeera mumpi neguyitibwa gwakilagala.
entries.english: The game is played on grass, and the grass around the hole is mown shorter and called the green.

entries.luganda: Oba osanga engeri eyokulambula ekyasinze okumanyika yeyo abantu abasing gyebakwasaganya nokutambula: okulambula kwokutonda.
entries.english: Perhaps the most common type of tourism is what most people associate with traveling: Recreation tourism.

entries.luganda: Wano abantu webagendera mu kifo ekyenjawulo enyo okuva ku bulamu bwaabwe obwa bulijo okusobola okuwumula n’okusanyukako.
entries.english: This is when people go to a place that is very different from their regular day-to-day life to relax and have fun.

entries.luganda: Biici, paaka nekifo ewakunganirwa bulijjo byebifo ebikyasinze okwettanirwa abalambuzi.
entries.english: Beaches, theme parks and camp grounds are often the most common places frequented by recreational tourists.

entries.luganda: Bwekiba nga ekigendererwa kyomulambuuzi mukifo kyakuyiga byannono, awo obulambuuzi buno tubuyita bulambuzi bwannono.
entries.english: If the objective of one’s visit to a particular place is to get to know its history and culture then this type of tourism is known as cultural tourism.

entries.luganda: Abalambuzi basobola okukyalira ebintu eby'enjawulo mu nsi emu oba bayinza okusalawo okusigala ku kitundu kimu.
entries.english: Tourists may visit different landmarks of a particular country or they may simply opt to focus on just one area.

entries.luganda: Abakoloniyo, oluvanyuma lw’okulaba ebyari bigenda musmaso, nebasabayo amajje amalala.
entries.english: The Colonists, seeing this activity, had also called for reinforcements.

entries.luganda: Emajje agazaamu amaanyi okugenda mumaaso mubiffo mulimu ekisooka n’ekyokusatu Hampshie ekipyaekyabasajja 200, mubufuzi bwa Koloneli John Stark ne James Reed (bombi oluvanyuma bafuuka ba genelaali).
entries.english: Troops reinforcing the forward positions included the 1st and 3rd New Hampshire regiments of 200 men, under Colonels John Stark and James Reed (both later became generals).

entries.luganda: Abasajja ba Stark bekutte ebifo ku lukomera ku mabuka g'ekifo ky'obufuzi.
entries.english: Stark's men took positions along the fence on the north end of the Colonist's position.

entries.luganda: Amazzi bwegakendera gagulawo omwaganya Ku Mugga gwa Mystic Ku peninsula y'amambuka g'ebuva njuba, bayanguwa okwongerayo olukomera n'ekisenge ky'ejinja elimpi Ku nkomerero yamambuuka eri amazzi gyegakoma Ku ka beach akatono.
entries.english: When low tide opened a gap along the Mystic River along the northeast of the peninsula, they quickly extended the fence with a short stone wall to the north ending at the water's edge on a small beach.

entries.luganda: Gridley oba Sitaaka bateekawo okuwkanya ku bigere 100 (30 m) mumaaso gekisaakate nebalagira nti tewabawo nomu akuba okutuusa ngababulijjo bakiyiseeko.
entries.english: Gridley or Stark placed a stake about 100 feet (30 m) in front of the fence and ordered that no one fire until the regulars passed it.

entries.english: The American plan relied on launching coordinated attacks from three different directions.

entries.english: General John Cadwalder would launch a diversionary attack against the British garrison at Bordentown, in order to block off any reinforcements.

entries.luganda: Generali James Ewing yandi tutte abajaasi lusanvu (700) okusomoka omugga Trenton Ferry, wamba olutindo waggulu wa Assunpink Creek n’okutangira amajje g’omulabe okutoloka
entries.english: General James Ewing would take 700 militia across the river at Trenton Ferry, seize the bridge over the Assunpink Creek and prevent any enemy troops from escaping.

entries.luganda: Amaanyi amakulu agasinga okuyikiriza abasajja 2,400 bandisazze omugga mailo Mwenda mumambuka ga Trenton, ate nebeyawulamu ebibinja bibiri, ekimu nga kikulemberwa Greene nekirala ngakikulemberwa Sullivan, okusobola okutandikawo obulumbaganyi nga amakya teganatuuka.
entries.english: The main assault force of 2,400 men would cross the river nine miles north of Trenton, and then split into two groups, one under Greene and one under Sullivan, in order to launch a pre-dawn attack.

entries.luganda: N'enkyukakyuka okuva mu kimu kya kuna okutuuka ku kitundu kya mayiro, sipiidi efuuka etali ya makulu nnyo era okuguma ne kufuuka ekyetaago ekya ddala.
entries.english: With the change from the quarter to the half mile run, speed becomes of much less importance and endurance becomes an absolute necessity.

entries.english: Of course a first-class half-miler, a man who can beat two minutes, must be possessed of a fair amount of speed, but endurance must be cultivated at all hazards.

entries.english: Some cross country running during the winter, combined with gymnasium work for the upper part of the body, is the best preparation for the running season.

entries.english: Maintaining a healthy energy balance, practicing effective hydration habits, and understanding the various aspects of supplementation practices can help athletes improve their performance and increase their enjoyment of the sport.

entries.english: Middle distance running is a relatively inexpensive sport; however, there are many misconceptions regarding the few pieces of equipment required to participate.

entries.english: The different constructions have different capabilities, are sensitive to different wave-lengths and have differing degrees of acuity, also they require different processing to make sense of the input and different numbers to work optimally.

entries.luganda: Akabinja wewakunganirwa obutofaali bwekintu ekimu mu kifo ekimu.
entries.english: A population is the collection of organisms of a particular species within a given geographic area.

entries.luganda: Abantu bonna omugatte bwe babeera bafanagana mu nneeyisa, n'ekikula baba bamanyiddwa nga ab'engeri emu.
entries.english: When all individuals in a population are identical with regard to a particular phenotypic trait they are known as monomorphic.

entries.luganda: Abantu bwe balaga ebintu ebyenjawulo ku mpiisa eyenjawulo baaba bali polymorphic.
entries.english: When the individuals show several variants of a particular trait they are polymorphic.

entries.luganda: Amagye g’enswa gatambula ne gawumula mu bifo byenjawulo .
entries.english: Army ant colonies march and nest in different phases as well.

entries.luganda: Mu kitundu ekyobulunzi, enkuyege zitambula ekiro nezibaako weziwumulira emisana.
entries.english: In the nomadic phase, army ants march at night and stop to camp during the day.

entries.luganda: Abasenze batandika ekitundu kyo kusenguka emmere eriwo nga ekendede. Mukiseera kino, abantu bakola ebisulo eby'akaseera ebikyusibwa buli lunaku.
entries.english: The colony begins a nomadic phase when available food has decreased. During this phase, the colony makes temporary nests that are changed everyday.

entries.luganda: Zino entalo z’abalunzi b’ente zimala ennaku kumi namusanvu.
entries.english: Each of these nomadic rampages or marches lasts for approximately 17 days.

entries.luganda: Akataffali kyeki? Ekigambo akatafaali kiva mu kigambo kyolulatini “cella”, ekitegeeza “Akasenge Akatono” era kyasoka kuzulibwa omukugu nga yetegereza enkula yenjuba.
entries.english: What is a cell? The word cell comes from the Latin word "cella", meaning "small room", and it was first coined by a microscopist observing the structure of cork.

entries.luganda: Akataffaali kekatundutundu k’ebintu byonna ebissa,era obuntu obutono obumu bukolebwa akataffali kamu oba obusingawo.
entries.english: The cell is the basic unit of all living things, and all organisms are composed of one or more cells.

entries.luganda: Obutaffaali bwetaagibwa era bwa mugaso okusomwako mu bulamu, mu mazima, nti era bwogerwako “emitendera egizimba obulamu”.
entries.english: Cells are so basic and critical to the study of life, in fact, that they are often referred to as "the building blocks of life".

entries.luganda: Obusimu bwomubiri buyimirizaawo obusuwa ngabusindika okukuba kwemisuwa okuyita mu mubiri gwonna okusobola okukuuma omusaayi ngagutambula burungi ngategutataganyizidwa.
entries.english: The Nervous System maintains homeostasis by sending nerve impulses through out the body to keep the flow of blood going as well as undisturbed.

entries.luganda: Bunno obusimu bwomubiri busobolwa okusindikibwa mangu mumubiri ekiguyamba okuwona obuzibu bwonna.
entries.english: These nerve impulses can be sent so quickly throughout the body which helps keep the body safe from any potential threat.

entries.luganda: Ensoke (emiyaga) zikuba ekifo ekitonotono okugerageranya n’emiyaga emirala ejj’amaanyi, naye zisobola okwoonoona buli kimu ekiri mu kkubo ly’azo
entries.english: Tornadoes strike a small area compared to other violent storms, but they can destroy everything in their path.

entries.luganda: Tornadoes zisigula miti, zijja embaawo okuva ku bizimbe, n’okusitula emmotoka mu bbanga. Tornadoes ebitundu bibiri ku buli kikumi ezisinga okubeera ebyatabbu bimala ebbanga lya ssaawa ssatu n’omusobyo.
entries.english: Tornadoes uproot trees, rip boards from buildings, and fling cars up into the sky. The most violent two percent of tornadoes last more than three hours.

entries.luganda: Zzino embuyaga ezaamanyi zilina empewo epimibwa okutuuka ku 480 km/h (133 m/s; 300 mph).
entries.english: These monster storms have winds up to 480 km/h (133 m/s; 300 mph).

entries.luganda: Abantu babadde bakozesa lenzi okuzimbulukusa ebintu okumala emyaka mingi.
entries.english: Humans have been making and using lenses for magnification for thousands and thousands of years.

entries.luganda: Wabula naye, gaalubindi entuffu ezasookera ddala zakolebwa Bulaaya mu kyaasa kya 16.
entries.english: However, the first true telescopes were made in Europe in the late 16th century.

entries.luganda: Bino ebyuuma ebirengerera ewala bikozesa ebirawuli ebigatte okusobozesa ebifaananyi ebiri ewala okulabikira okumpi era nga binene.
entries.english: These telescopes used a combination of two lenses to make distant objects appear both nearer and larger.

entries.luganda: Omululu n’obutayagaliza bujja kubeera wamu naffe era enkwatagana bwetyo bwebeera nti abangi bwe baganirwa mu kintu, wajja kubeerawo bangi abaganyulwamu nga beeyagaliza
entries.english: Greed and selfishness will always be with us and it is the nature of cooperation that when the majority benefit there will always be more to gain in the short term by acting selfishly

entries.luganda: Essubi, abantu abasinga bajja kuzula nti okuwangala kwabwe nti kujja kubba mu kukolagana n’abantu abalala.
entries.english: Hopefully, most people will realise that their long term best option is to work together with others.

entries.luganda: Abantu banji baloota ku lunaku abantu lwe banaasobola okugenda ku mmunyeenye endala nookulambula ku nsi endala. Abantu abamu beewunya wabweeru eyo bwewafaanana abamu bakkiriza aliyeeni (ebitategeerekeka) oba engeri zoobulamu endala ziyinza okubaayo.
entries.english: Many people dream of the day when humans can travel to another star and explore other worlds, some people wonder what's out there some belive that aliens or other life may live on another plant.

entries.luganda: Naye, kino singa kimala nekibaawo oba oli awo tekijja kubeerawo okumala akaseera akawerako.emunyenye zisasanye wabweru nti waliwo obuse bwa mayiro wakati wa mayiro eziliranaganye.
entries.english: But, if this ever does happen probably won't happen for a very long time. The stars are so spread out that there are trillions of miles between stars that are "neighbors".

entries.luganda: Oba osanga lunaku lumu,bazzukulu bo bajja kuba bayimiridde kukasolya kensi yobumenke ngabewunaganya kuba jjajabwe abayita?
entries.english: Maybe one day, your great grandchildren will be standing atop an alien world wondering about their ancient ancestors?

entries.luganda: Ensolo zibeera nobusimu. Zirya ebintu nezibimulungusa. Ensolo ezisinga zisobola okutambula.
entries.english: Animals are made of many cells. They eat things and digest them inside. Most animals can move.

entries.luganda: Ensolo zokka zezilina obwongo (naye ate sinsolo zonna; Ebyenyanja ebimanyidwa nga Jelly fish tebilina bwongo).
entries.english: Only animals have brains (though not even all animals do; jellyfish, for example, do not have brains).

entries.luganda: Ebisolo bisangibwa mu nsi yonna. Bisima mu taka. Biwuga mu Nyanja, ate bibuuka bire.
entries.english: Animals are found all over the earth. They dig in the ground, swim in the oceans, and fly in the sky.

entries.luganda: Akataffaali ke kakyasinze obutono mu butonde bw'ebiramu.
entries.english: A cell is the smallest structural and functional unit of a living (things) organism.

entries.luganda: Ekigambo cell kiva mu kigambo ekyo lulatini cella ekitegeeza akadduukulu.
entries.english: Cell comes from the Latin word cella which means small room.

entries.luganda: Wotunulira ebintu ng’okozzesa ekyuma ekibigaziya, ojja kulaba nga buli kimu kikolebwa mu buntu butono obwetolodde.
entries.english: If you look at living things under a microscope, you will see that they are made of small squares or balls.

entries.luganda: Robert Hooke, munnasayansi owa biology okuva mu England, yalaba obubookisi obutono mu kasaanikira nga akozesa microscope.
entries.english: Robert Hooke, a biologist from England, saw small squares in cork with a microscope.

entries.luganda: Bwafanana nga obusenge. Yeyasooka okulaba obutaffaali obufu.
entries.english: They looked like rooms. He was the first person to observe dead cells

entries.luganda: Endagakintue z'enkyusabuziba n'ebipooli bisobola okuva mu mbeera emu okudda mu ndala ne zitakyuka.
entries.english: Elements and compounds can move from one state to another and not change.

entries.luganda: Nitrogen nga omukka akyalina ebintu byebimu nga nitrogen ow'amazzi. Embeera y'amazzi nkwafu nate molecules zisigala ze zimu.
entries.english: Nitrogen as a gas still has the same properties as liquid nitrogen. The liquid state is denser but the molecules are still the same.

entries.luganda: Amazzi kyakulabirako kirala. Amazzi gakolebwa obutofaali bwa hydrogen bubiri naka oxygen kamu.
entries.english: Water is another example. The compound water is made up of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

entries.luganda: Kilina obutundu tundu bwebumu oba mukka, oba mazzi oba kyekute.
entries.english: It has the same molecular structure whether it is a gas, liquid, or solid.

entries.luganda: Wadde nga endabika yakyo eyinza okukyuuka, embeera yaakyo eya sayansi ejja kusigala yeemu.
entries.english: Although its physical state may change, its chemical state remains the same.

entries.luganda: Obudde kyekintu ekitwetolodde, era bukosa buli kimu kyetukola, naye ate nga buzibu okutegeera.
entries.english: Time is something that is all around us, and affects everything we do, yet is difficult to understand.

entries.luganda: Obudde okunoonyerezebwa ko bannaddiini, bannassomabibuuzo, ne bannasayansi okumala emyaka lukumi.
entries.english: Time has been studied by religious, philosophical, and scientific scholars for thousands of years.

entries.luganda: Ofuna obumanyirivu mu bintu bingi okuli okuyita mu by’omumaso nga oyise mu biri wo,n’emubikadde.
entries.english: We experience time as a series of events passing from the future through the present to the past.

entries.luganda: Obudde era yengeri gyetubalamu essaawa(obuwanvu) bwebintu ebibadewo.
entries.english: Time is also how we compare the duration (length) of events.

entries.luganda: Osobola okumanya obudde obuyise ngawetegereza ekiyitibwa “cyclical event”. Cyclical event kyekintu ekiddamu okubeerawo omudiringanwa buli kaseera.
entries.english: You can mark the passing of time yourself by observing the repetition of a cyclical event. A cyclical event is something that happens again and again regularly.

entries.luganda: Ennaku zino ebyuma bi kalimagezi bikozesebwa okukyusamu ebifananyi ne vidiyo
entries.english: Computers today are used to manipulate pictures and videos.

entries.luganda: Bukatyuuni obwenjawulo busobola okuzimbibwa ku kompyuta era bukatyuni buno bweyongede ddala okukozesebwa mu televizooni ne filimu.
entries.english: Sophisticated animations can be constructed on computers, and this kind of animation is increasingly being used in television and films.

entries.luganda: Omuziki bulijjo bagukwata bakozesa ebyuma bi kalimagezi ebiyoyotedwa okusobola okutobeka amaloboozi
entries.english: Music is often recorded using sophisticated computers to process and mix sounds together.

entries.luganda: Okumala ekisera kinene mu kyasa ky’ekumi n’omwenda n’ekyabili, kyalowozebwa nti abantu abasoka mu bali a Maori abaayiga enyo aganyonyi aganene agayitibwa Moas
entries.english: For a long time during the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, it was believed the first inhabitants of New Zealand were the Maori people, who hunted giant birds called moas.

entries.luganda: Enjogela ne etekawo endowooza nti aba Maori baava Polynesia mu kibinja ekinene ne bawaamba New Zealand okuva e Moriori, nebatandikawo ebika ebyobulimi.
entries.english: The theory then established the idea that the Maori people migrated from Polynesia in a Great Fleet and took New Zealand from the Moriori, establishing an agricultural society.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde obujulizi obujja bulaga nti ba Moriori bali kabinja ka ba Maori abafubutuka mu New Zealand okutuuka ku kazinga Chatham nga batongoza ebyobuwangwa byabwe obwetongodde ku bwabyo.
entries.english: However, new evidence suggests that the Moriori were a group of mainland Maori who migrated from New Zealand to the Chatham Islands, developing their own distinctive, peaceful culture.

entries.luganda: Waliwo egwanga eddala ku bizinga bya Chatham, byalimu abaava e Maori nga bavila New Zealand
entries.english: There was also another tribe on the Chatham islands these were Maori who migrated away from New Zealand.

entries.luganda: Beyitanga ba Moriori waliyo aba skirmishe abatonotono era munkomerero, aba Moriori bagwerawodala.
entries.english: They called themselves the Moriori there were a few skirmishes and in the end, the Moriori were wiped out

entries.luganda: Abantu abaalimu okumala ebyasa batuyamba okusiima amanyi gaffe n’ebyetwagala okola nga awamu batulaga wetusobezza n’obuzibu bwetusanze.
entries.english: Individuals who had been involved for several decades helped us appreciate our strengths and passions while candidly assessing difficulties and even failures.

entries.luganda: Bwe tuwuliriza abantu ssekinoomu nga boogera ku byafaayo byabwe, ne mu maka ge bavaamu, n’ebitongole bye babaddemu, ofuna okubikulirwa kw’abantu abayamba mu kulwanirira oba okusuula ekitongole.
entries.english: While listening to individuals share their individual, family, and organizational stories, we gained valuable insight into the past and some of the personalities who influenced for good or ill the culture of the organization.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde okutegeera ebyafaayo tekitegeeza nti otegeera ebyobuwangwa ne ennono, naye kiyamba ko abantu okutegeera wa webagwa mu byafaayo by'ekitongole.
entries.english: While understanding one’s history does not assume understanding of culture, it does at least help people gain a sense of where they fall within the history of the organization.

entries.luganda: Nga tukyekanya obuwanguzi nne okufuka abamanyi mu byetukola nga tuyigila ku byetuttakola bulungi, basekinomu nabuli’ omu eyetabye bazula ebilala ebyamakulu, ebilubililwa nne amanyi agavuga ekitongole.
entries.english: While assessing the successes and becoming aware of failures, individuals and the whole of the participating persons discover more deeply the values, mission, and driving forces of the organization.

entries.luganda: Mumbeera eno, okujukira ebyaliwo mu neyisa yabasububuzi n’ekyabaviramu obuwanguzi kyayamba abantu okubeera aberufu eri enkyuukakyuuka empya n’omugendo omupya esinzizo lyelikutte.
entries.english: In this instance, recalling previous instances of entrepreneurial behavior and resulting successes helped people to be open to new changes and new direction for the local church.

entries.luganda: Engero ez’obuwanguzi zikendeeza okutya kw’enkyukakyuka, ate nga zikola obusobozi bw'okukkiriza enkyukakyuka mu biseera by'omu maaso.
entries.english: Such success stories lessened the fears of change, while creating positive inclinations toward change in the future.

entries.luganda: Okugatiliza endowooza nebilala, bwe bukodyo obugunjula ebizibu, obugaata awamu endowooza eyenjawulo okufuuna ekimu.
entries.english: Convergent thinking patterns are problem solving techniques that unite different ideas or fields to find a solution.

entries.luganda: Okwekaliriza mu kulowoza bwe bwangu,amagezi ne obutufu bwawulira ddala amazima, okuda mu, n’okufuna amawulire agamala.
entries.english: The focus of this mindset is speed, logic and accuracy, also identification of facts, reapplying existing techniques, gathering information.

entries.luganda: Ekisinga okubela ekyamanyi bye bilowozo byolina mu bwongo. Okuddamu okutufu kuli kumu, kale kiyinza okubela ekitufu oba ekikyamu.
entries.english: The most important factor of this mindset is: there is only one correct answer. You only think of two answers, namely right or wrong.

entries.luganda: Eno engeri y’okuloowoza ekwataganya ne ssayansi oba emitendera egiri ku mutindo.
entries.english: This type of thinking is associated with certain science or standard procedures.

entries.luganda: Abantu abalowooza ennyo,basobola okukwata ennyukuta ezimu,okujja wo ekizibu n’okukola mu bya sayansi.
entries.english: People with this type of thinking have logical thinking, are able to memorize patterns, solve problems and work on scientific tests.

entries.luganda: Abantu be bakyasize okubeera bannakinku mu kusoma obwongo bw'abalala.
entries.english: Humans are by far the most talented species in reading the minds of others.

entries.luganda: Kino kitegeeza nti tusobola okulagula abantu kye balowooza, kye bakkiririzamu, kye bamanyi oba kye baagala.
entries.english: That means we are able to successfully predict what other humans perceive, intend, believe, know or desire.

entries.luganda: Mu busobozi muno, okutegeera ekiruubilirwa kyabalala kintu kikulu. Kitukkiriza okugonjola obuttakkanya bwebikolwa byennyini
entries.english: Among these abilities, understanding the intention of others is crucial. It allows us to resolve possible ambiguities of physical actions.

entries.luganda: Okugeza, bwolaba omuntu ng’amenya edirisa lyemotoka, otebereza nti abadde agezaako kubba motoka yamugwiira.
entries.english: For example, if you were to see someone breaking a car window, you would probably assume he was trying to steal a stranger’s car.

entries.luganda: Ajakwetaaga okulamulwa mu ngeri eyenjawulo senga yali abuziza ebisumuluzo bye eby'emotoka ate nga yali motoka ye gyeyali agezaako okumenya.
entries.english: He would need to be judged differently if he had lost his car keys and it was his own car that he was trying to break into.

entries.luganda: MRI esinzira kunambika ya Physics eyitibwa nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), eyazulibwa mu ja 1930 ne Felix Bloch (Ng’akola ku ssetendekero lye Stanford) ne Edward Purcell (okuva mu Ssetendekero lya Harvard).
entries.english: MRI is based on a physics phenomenon called nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), which was discovered in the 1930s by Felix Bloch (working at Stanford University) and Edward Purcell (from Harvard University).

entries.luganda: Mu kuwuuma kuno, ebyebulungulo n'amayengo ga lediyo galetera bu atomu okufulumya obuyengo obutono obwa lediyo.
entries.english: In this resonance, magnetic field and radio waves cause atoms to give off tiny radio signals.

entries.luganda: Mu mwaka gwa 1970, Raymond Damadian, omusawo omukugu era anoonyereza mu bya ssaayansi, yavvumbula ensibuko y'okukozesa ebifaananyi ebifaanagana nga ekyuuma ky'okukebera eby’obulamu.
entries.english: In the year 1970, Raymond Damadian, a medical doctor and research scientist, discovered the basis for using magnetic resonance imaging as a tool for medical diagnosis.

entries.luganda: Nga wayise emyaka ena, afuna obuyinza obwenkalakalila. Bwebuyinza obwasoka mu byuma ebitangaza omubiri gwona.
entries.english: Four years later a patent was granted, which was the world's first patent issued in the field of MRI.

entries.luganda: Mu mwaka gwa lukumi mu lwenda nsavu mu musanvu, Omusawo Damadian yakola ekyuma ekikola sikani y’omubiri gwona nakituma Erinya lya “Indomitable”.
entries.english: In 1977, Dr. Damadian completed the construction of the first “whole-body” MRI scanner, which he called the ”Indomitable”.

entries.luganda: Asynchronous Empuliziganya ezamu amaanyi obudde obw'okutunulamu n'engeri gyetuddamu abalala.
entries.english: Asynchronous communication encourages time for reflection and reaction to others.

entries.luganda: Kiwa abayizi obusobozi okukolela Ku musinde gwaabwe ne bafuga omusinde gw'amawulire g'ebilagiro.
entries.english: It allows students the ability to work at their own pace and control the pace of instructional information.

entries.luganda: Okugata kwekyo, waliwo ebikugira esawa bitono nokutuebereza obw'eddembe ly'esawa zokukola.(Bremer, 1998)
entries.english: In addition, there are fewer time restrictions with the possibility of flexible working hours. (Bremer, 1998)

entries.luganda: Okukozesa yintaneeti n’omutimbagano ogusaasaanidde munsi yonna gusobozesa abayizi okufuna bye baagala okumanya ekiseera kyonna.
entries.english: The use of the Internet and the World Wide Web allows learners to have access to information at all times.

entries.luganda: Abasomi basobola okuweereza ebibuuzo byabwe eri abasomesa obudde bwonna ne bafuna okudibwaamu kwabwe mangu ddala, nga tebalinze kusisiinkana basomesa.
entries.english: Students can also submit questions to instructors at any time of day and expect reasonably quick responses, rather than waiting until the next face-to-face meeting.

entries.luganda: Engeri enongooseemu eyookuyiga ewa eddembe. Teri ngeri emu ennungi eyokuyiga.
entries.english: The postmodern approach to learning offers the freedom from absolutes. There is no one good way to learn.

entries.luganda: Ekituufu, temuli wadde kyakuyiga nakimu. Okuyiga kubeerawo mu bumanyirivu wakati w’omuyizi ne byokuyiga byawereddwa.
entries.english: In fact, there is not one good thing to learn.Learning takes place in the experience between the learner and the knowledge presented.

entries.luganda: Obukenkenfu bwafe mu Kyekolere n’okwoolesa amawulire, n’okusomera ku ntegeka eziri ku television kinnyonnyola bulungi ensonga.
entries.english: Our current experience with all the do-it-yourself and information presenting, learning-based television shows illustrates this point.

entries.luganda: Banji kuffe tweesanga nga tulaba Ttivvi etutegeeza ku mbeera jetutali enyigiramu oba weetutali teeka magezi ago
entries.english: So many of us find ourselves watching a television show that informs us of a process or experience in which we will never participate or apply that knowledge.

entries.luganda: Tetulikola nkyukakyuka zamanyi ku motoka, tetulizimba kifuyirira mazzi mulujja lwaffe, tetuligenda ko Peru kugezesa byayononebwa edda, oba okukola enkyukakyuka ku nyumba yamulirwaana.
entries.english: We will never overhaul a car, build a fountain in our backyard, travel to Peru to examine ancient ruins, or remodel our neighbour's house.

entries.luganda: Okwebaza kudda eri waya entono eziyita wansi mu semayanja okutuuka e Europe n’esetiliyiti, Ne Greenland eyungidwa bulungi ne 93% kumuwendo gw’abantu basobola okutuuka kumutimbagano.
entries.english: Thanks to undersea fiber optic cable links to Europe and broadband satellite, Greenland is well connected with 93% of the population having internet access.

entries.luganda: Wooteli yo oba abagenyi (bwoba osula mu wooteri oba amaka go ab’obwananyini) kiyinzika okuba n’omutimbagano ogutali gwa kusasulirwa ku komputa yo eyewaka, era ebifo byona bibera ne yintaneti etali yakusasulirwa ne mubifo ebyolukale.
entries.english: Your hotel or hosts (if staying in a guesthouse or private home) will likely have wifi or an internet connected PC, and all settlements have an internet cafe or some location with public wifi.

entries.luganda: Nga bwe twogedde wagulu,newankubadde ekigambo “Eskimo” kisigala kikirizibwa mu Amerika , kitwalibwa nga kinyooma naddala abantu abatali bamerika banji, naddala mu Canada.
entries.english: As mentioned above, though the word "Eskimo" remains acceptable in the United States, it is considered pejorative by many non-U.S. Arctic peoples, especially in Canada.

entries.luganda: Wadde nga oyinza okuwulira ebigambo ebikozesebwa abatuuze b’eGreenlandic, abagwiira balina okwewala okulukozesa.
entries.english: While you may hear the word used by Greenlandic Natives, its use should be avoided by foreigners.

entries.luganda: Ababeeranga mu Greenland beyita Inuit mu Canada ne (plural Kalaaallit), e Greenlander, mu Greenland.
entries.english: The native inhabitants of Greenland call themselves Inuit in Canada and Kalaalleq (plural Kalaallit), a Greenlander, in Greenland.

entries.luganda: Obumenyi bwa mateeka, n’obulwadde bijja kusindikiriza abagwira awamu, kimanyikidwa mu Greenland. Ne mu bibuga, era tewali “bifo ebikakali.”
entries.english: Crime, and ill-will toward foreigners in general, is virtually unknown in Greenland. Even in the towns, there are no "rough areas."

entries.luganda: Embeera y'obudde ey'obunyogovu yegenda okukosa ennyo abanabeera nga tebeetegese.
entries.english: Cold weather is perhaps the only real danger the unprepared will face.

entries.luganda: Bwogenda mu Greenland mu biseera by’obunyogovu (nga okitadde mu nkola nti gye weyongera mu mambuka obunyogovu nga nabwo bweyongera), kyamugaso okuleeta engoye ezibuguma.
entries.english: If you visit Greenland during cold seasons (considering that the further north you go, the colder it will be), it is essential to bring warm enough clothing.

entries.luganda: Ennaku ezisuse obungi mu summer zisobola okuleta ebizibu okufuna otulo otumala nensonga endaala ezekuusa kubyobulamu.
entries.english: The very long days in the summer can lead to problems getting sufficient sleep and associated health issues.

entries.luganda: Mu musana, wegenderezanga nyo ensiri zabanordiki. Newankubadde tezirina bulwadde bwezisaasanya, zinyizza era zimalako emirembe.
entries.english: During the summer, also watch out for the Nordic mosquitoes. Although they do not transmit any diseases, they can be irritating.

entries.luganda: Wadde nga ebyenfuna bya San Francisco byesimbye ku bulambuzi, waliwo ebyenfuna ebilala nkumu.
entries.english: While San Francisco's economy is linked to it being a world-class tourist attraction, its economy is diversified.

entries.luganda: Ebitundu ebisinga eby'emirimu bya mirimu egy'ekikugu, gavumenti, eby'ensimbi, obutunzi, n'obulambuzi.
entries.english: The largest employment sectors are professional services, government, finance, trade, and tourism.

entries.luganda: Okulagibwa kwa buli kaseera mu kuyimba, firimu, okuwandiika n'obuwangwa obumanyifu buyambye okufuula ekibuga n'okulamba kw'ettaka kwaakyo okumanyibwa munsi yonna.
entries.english: Its frequent portrayal in music, films, literature and popular culture has helped make the city and its landmarks known throughout the world.

entries.luganda: San Francisco ekoze ebikozesebwa eby'abalambuzi ne woteri ez'enjawulo, ebifo ebitunda emere, n'ebifo ebikunganilwamu ebyo’mulembe
entries.english: San Francisco has developed a large tourist infrastructure with numerous hotels, restaurants, and top-notch convention facilities.

entries.luganda: San Francisco kye kimu ku biffo ebisinga obulungi wosobola okusanga emmere engwiira: Eya aba Korea, Thai, eya abayindi ne aba Japaani.
entries.english: San Francisco is also one of the best places in the nation for other Asian cuisine: Korean, Thai, Indian, and Japanese.

entries.luganda: Okutambulira ewazanyirwa abaana mu nsi yonna Walt kiraga ekyobulamazi ekyamaanyi eri amaka Gabamerika bangi.
entries.english: Traveling to Walt Disney World represents a major pilgrimage for many American families.

entries.luganda: Okukyala okwabulijjo kulimu, okulinya enyonyi paka ku kisaawe ky'enyonyi ekya Orlando, n'olinya emotoka paka ku woteeri mu Disney, noomala sabbiiti nga tovudde ku ttaka lya Disney n'omala n'oddayo eeka.
entries.english: The "typical" visit involves flying into Orlando International Airport, busing to an on-site Disney hotel, spending about a week without leaving Disney property, and returning home.

entries.luganda: Waliyo engeri nyingi ezitabalika ez’enjawulo ezisoboka, naye kino kisigala abantu abasinga kye bategeeza bwe boogera ku “ kugenda mu Disney World”
entries.english: There are infinite variations possible, but this remains what most people mean when they talk of "going to Disney World".

entries.luganda: Tikiti nnyingi ezitundidwa ku mitimbagano nga ebay oba Craigslist nkozeseko emirundi mingi mu pakingi ez'enjawulo emisana.
entries.english: Many tickets sold online through auction websites such as eBay or Craigslist are partially used multi-day park-hopper tickets.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde nga kino kikolebwa buli wamu, Disney tekikiliza kukyusamu tiketi n’ogiwa omulala.
entries.english: While this is a very common activity, it is prohibited by Disney: the tickets are non-transferable.

entries.luganda: Okukampinga wansi wa Canyon enene kwetagisa okufuna olukusa
entries.english: Any camping below the rim in Grand Canyon requires a backcountry permit.

entries.luganda: Pamiti ntono era nga ziliwo kukuuma ba kanyoni era ngazatekebwawo mu lunaku olusooka mu mwezi, emyezi ena oluvanyuma lwomwezi okutandika.
entries.english: Permits are limited to protect the canyon, and become available on the 1st day of the month, four months prior to the start month.

entries.luganda: Nolweekyo okukirizibwa okubeera mukifo omuli abantu abesudde mu naku ezisooka mugw’okutaano zijja kubeelawo nga 1 ogw’oluberyeberye.
entries.english: Thus, a backcountry permit for any start date in May becomes available on 1 Jan.

entries.luganda: Amabanga mu bifo ebimanyikidwa , nga Bright Angel Campground ekiriraanye phantom Ranch, kya bulijjo bijjula olw'okusabwa okufunibwa ku naku ezisooka nga biguddwawo.
entries.english: Space for the most popular areas, such as the Bright Angel Campground adjacent to Phantom Ranch, generally fill up by the requests received on first date they are opened to reservations.

entries.luganda: Waliwo pamiti ntononnyo zebatelekedde abajja obuzi nga asooka okujja yatwaala.
entries.english: There are a limited number permits reserved for walk-in requests available on a first come, first served basis.

entries.luganda: Okuyingira amaserengeta ga Afrika nga okozesa motoka kya ssanyu jeleele kubanga ogenda olaba obulungi bw’ensi n'okugenda mu bifo ebyenjawulo abalambuze byebatatera kugenda mu.
entries.english: Entering Southern Africa by car is an amazing way to see all the region's beauty as well as to get to places off the normal tourist routes.

entries.luganda: Kinno kisobola okukolebwa mu motoka munteekateeka eyobwegendereza naye eya 4x4 yekubirizibwa era bisangibwa mubiffo ebyenjawulo naye nga bituukibwaako nga olina beesi eyawagulu eya 4x4.
entries.english: This can be done in a normal car with careful planning but a 4x4 is highly advised and many locations are only accessible with a high wheel base 4x4.

entries.luganda: Olina okukisigaza mu bilowozo nti newankubadde nga amaselengeta ga Afirika matebenkevu, ensi ezziriranye si bwezili.
entries.english: Keep in mind while planning that although Southern Africa is stable not all neighboring countries are.

entries.luganda: Ebyetaagisa okufuna visa nemiwendo gisinzira kuva ku buli nsi era bikwatibwakonyo ensi gyovamu.
entries.english: Visa requirements and costs vary from nation to nation and are affected by the country you come from.

entries.luganda: Buli nsi erina amateeka gaayo agenjawulo aagalaga ebyeetagisa ebirina okubeera mu motoka ebyokwekuumisa.
entries.english: Each country also has unique laws requiring what emergency items need to be in the car.

entries.luganda: Amazzi agayiika okuva mu Nalubaale bwebukiika konnobw’ekitundu kya Zimbabwe, okusala ensalo okuva mu Livingstone, Zambia okuliraana Botswana.
entries.english: Victoria Falls is a town in the western portion of Zimbabwe, across the border from Livingstone, Zambia, and near Botswana.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga kili okumpi dala nebiliyililo, era byebisinga okusikiriza, naye ono omulambuzi ow’olulango jalaga afunayo abanoonya okuvumbula n’abalengera emikisa mingi okubeerayo akabanga akawera.
entries.english: The town lies immediately next to the falls, and they are the major attraction, but this popular tourist destination offers both adventure seekers and sightseers plenty of opportunities for a longer stay.

entries.luganda: Mu biseera by'enkuba (ogwekumi nogumu okutuuka mu gwokusatu), obungi bw'amazzi bugya kubeera waggulu n’ebiliyiliro bigya kuba byewunyisa nyo.
entries.english: In the rainy season (November to March), the water volume will be higher and the Falls will be more dramatic.

entries.luganda: Bwosala olutindo oba bwotambula mu makubo agayita okumpi n'ebiliyiliro, ojja kutoba.
entries.english: You are guaranteed to get wet if you cross the bridge or walk along the trails winding near the Falls.

entries.luganda: Kuludda olulala, kyekyo kyenyinyi kubanga obungi bwamazzi buli waggulu nyo nti okulengera kwo okw'ebilibiliyiro byenyini kuja kusikirizibwa - namazzi gonna!
entries.english: On the other hand, it is precisely because the volume of water is so high that your viewing of the actual Falls will be obscured—by all the water!

entries.luganda: Amasiro ga Tutankhamun (KV62). KV62 gayinza okuba amasiro agasinga etutumu mu ekiwonvu, Howard Carter mu 1922 nga avumbudde amasiro ga kabaka omuto nga galinga agatataataaganyizibwanga.
entries.english: Tomb of Tutankhamun (KV62). KV62 may be the most famous of the tombs in the Valley, the scene of Howard Carter's 1922 discovery of the almost intact royal burial of the young king.

entries.luganda: Okusinziira ku masiro g'obwakabaka amalala amangi, entaana ya Tutankhamun tesikiriza nnyo kulambula, olwokuba nti ntono ate teyatimbibwa nnyo.
entries.english: Compared to most of the other royal tombs, however, the tomb of Tutankhamun is barely worth visiting, being much smaller and with limited decoration.

entries.luganda: Omuntu yenna ayagala okukakasa nti yafadda ajja kulabila kumagumba nga wasigade mutwe nabibegabega mu sanduuko.
entries.english: Anyone interested in seeing evidence of the damage to the mummy done during attempts to remove it from the coffin will be disappointed as only the head and shoulders are visible.

entries.luganda: Ebyoobugagga ebisuffu ebyentaana tebikyaalimu, naye byajjibwaamu ne bitwaalibwa e Cairo mu terekkero lyebikadde
entries.english: The fabulous riches of the tomb are no longer in it, but have been removed to the Egyptian Museum in Cairo.

entries.luganda: Abagenyi abalina obudde obutono, kyandibadde kirungi okutwaliriza obudde bwabwe mu biffo ebirala.
entries.english: Visitors with limited time would be best to spend their time elsewhere.

entries.luganda: Phnom Krom, kilomita kumi n’abbiri mu maselengetta g'ebungwanjuba ga Siem Reap. Eno kanisa yazimbibwa mu kyasa ky’omwendda, mu bisera bya sekabaka Yasovarman
entries.english: Phnom Krom, 12 km southwest of Siem Reap. This hilltop temple was built at the end of the 9th century, during the reign of King Yasovarman.

entries.luganda: Embeera embi ey’esinzizo nokulengele ebusuka Nyanja kikola amakulu okulinya olusozi.
entries.english: The gloomy atmosphere of the temple and the view over the Tonle Sap lake make the climb to the hill worthwhile.

entries.luganda: Bw'oba ogenda okukyalayo, oyinza okufunayo eryaato erikutambuza ku nyanja.
entries.english: A visit to the site can be conveniently combined with a boat trip to the lake.

entries.luganda: Okuyisa Angkor kyetagisa okuyingira esinzizo nolweekyo teweelabila okuleeta ebitambuliso byo nga ogenda e Tonle Sap.
entries.english: The Angkor Pass is needed to enter the temple so do not forget to bring your passport along when heading to Tonle Sap.

entries.luganda: Yerusalemu ky'ekibuga ekikulu ekya Israel atte ekikyasinze obunene, lwakuba ensi ezisinga ne ekibiina ky’amawaanga amagatte tezikiriza nti kye ekibuga ekikulu ekya Israel.
entries.english: Jerusalem is the capital and largest city of Israel, though most other countries and the United Nations do not recognize it as Israel's capital.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga ekikadde munsozi za Judea kilina ebyedda ebisensera okumala emyaaka lukumi nomusobyo.
entries.english: The ancient city in the Judean Hills has a fascinating history spanning thousands of years.

entries.luganda: Ekibugga kitukuvu eri amaddini g’abayuda, abakulisitayo n’abayisiramu era kikola nga ekyeddini, n’ebyobuwanga
entries.english: The city is holy to the three monotheistic religions - Judaism, Christianity and Islam, and serves as a spiritual, religious, and cultural center.

entries.luganda: Okusinzira ku ddini y’ekibuga, atte okusingira ddala ebitundu bingi ebiri mu kibuga ekikadde, Yelusalemu kye kimu ku bibuga ebyetanirwa abalambuzi mu Yisirayiri.
entries.english: Due to the religious significance of the city, and in particular the many sites of the Old City area, Jerusalem is one of the main tourist destinations in Israel.

entries.luganda: Jerusalemi erina ebyafaayo bingi wamu nebifo byebyobuwangwa, ngamulimu ebifo ewagulwa ebintu ebikubyeko obugule, ewalirwa emmere nebirala.
entries.english: Jerusalem has many historic, archeological and cultural sites, along with vibrant and crowded shopping centers, cafés, and restaurants.

entries.luganda: Ecuador yetaaga abatuuze be Cuba okufuna okuyitibwa nga tebanayingira Ecuador wadenga ebisaawe biri yintanasonolo oba byakumwanjo.
entries.english: Ecuador requires that Cuban citizens receive an invitation letter prior to entering Ecuador through international airports or frontier admission points.

entries.luganda: Ebaluwa eno etekedwa okutongozebwa aba minisitule ya Ecuador mu nsonga zebunaayira, era bakkirizaganye nebyetago ebimu.
entries.english: This letter must be legalized by the Ecuadorian Ministry of Foreign Affairs, and comply with certain requirements.

entries.luganda: Bino ebyetaagisibwa byakolebwa nga biwa entegeka eyentambula mu nsi zombie.
entries.english: These requirements are designed to provide an organized migratory flow between both countries.

entries.luganda: Abatuuze ba Cuba abalina Kaadi za US eza kilagala oba ezobutuuze bandikyalidde konsoleti wa Ecuador, okufuna eky'enjawulo eri eky'etaago kino.
entries.english: Cuban citizens who are US green card holders should visit an Ecuadorian Consulate to obtain an exemption to this requirement.

entries.luganda: Passipota yo elina okubeera nga ekyalina yo emyezi 6 egissukka mu biseera byogenda okutambulira mu. Tikiti ekutwala nekuzza yetagisibwa okukakasa obuwaanvu bw’okukyala kwo.
entries.english: Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates. A round/onward trip ticket is needed to prove the length of your stay.

entries.luganda: Ebyokulambula bya beeyi ntono eri abo abali abangi mu bubinja, noolwekyo bwewabanga wekka oba nemukwano gwo omu, gezaako okusisinkana abantu abalala mukole akabinja kabantu bana ku mukaaga okusobola okufuna omuwendo buli omu omurungi.
entries.english: Tours are cheaper for larger groups, so if you're by yourself or with just one friend, try to meet other people and form a group of four to six for a better per-person rate.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde, kino tewandi kitaddeko mutima, kubanga bulijjo abalambuuzi bategekebwa okwetoloola okujjuza emotoka.
entries.english: However, this shouldn't really be off your concern, because often tourists are shuffled around to fill the cars.

entries.luganda: Kirabika okubeera enyo mukubuzabuza abantu basobole okukiriza nti balina okusasula ekisingako.
entries.english: It seems actually to be more a way of tricking people into believing they have to pay more.

entries.luganda: Mubitiibwa byakyo ebiyitirivu mu mambukka ga Machu Picchu wewayimiridde olusozi gagadde, awatera okukubibwa ebifaananyi ebyenjawulo.
entries.english: Towering above the north end of Machu Picchu is this steep mountain, often the backdrop to many photos of the ruins.

entries.luganda: Kilabikanga kizibunnyo okuva wansi, era kyewunzise era kizibu okwambuka, naye kikola amakulu omuntu ali fiiti aba asobola okwambukawo mudakiika 45.
entries.english: It looks a bit daunting from below, and it is a steep and difficult ascent, but most reasonably fit persons should be able to make it in about 45 minutes.

entries.luganda: Amadaala g'amayinja gatekebwa kumpi Ku buli kkubo, ne mu bitundu eby'ebiko enyo Keble za stilu ziwa wokwatira eziwanilira.
entries.english: Stone steps are laid along most of the path, and in the steeper sections steel cables provide a supporting handrail.

entries.luganda: Ebyo nga by’ogeddwa, suubira okugweebwaako omukka, era weegendereze mu bisirikko, naddala ewabisi, kubanga wayinza okuba awoobulabe mu bwangu
entries.english: That said, expect to be out of breath, and take care in the steeper portions, especially when wet, as it can become dangerous quickly.

entries.luganda: Waliyo empuku entono nyo kumpi n’entikko gyolina okuyitamu, eri wansi ko atte nga nfunda.
entries.english: There's a tiny cave near the top that must be passed through, it is quite low and a rather tight squeeze.

entries.luganda: Okulaba ebifo n’ebyobutonde bya Galapagos ky’ekisinga okukolebwa eryaato, era nga Charles Darwin bweyakola mu 1835.
entries.english: Seeing the sites and wildlife of the Galapagos is best done by boat, just as Charles Darwin did it in 1835.

entries.luganda: Emeeri ezisoba mu 60 zeziseeyeya ku mazzi ga Galapagos – mu sayizi ngazitise abasaabaze wakati wa 8 ku 100.
entries.english: Over 60 cruise ships ply the Galapagos waters - ranging in size from 8 to 100 passengers.

entries.luganda: Abantu abasing basasulila ebifo byabwe mangu (Kubanga amaato gajula nyo mu sizoni eno).
entries.english: Most people book their place well in advance (as the boats are usually full during the high season).

entries.luganda: Beera n’obukakafu nti oyo akukwaatira ekifo mu Galapagos omukugu era alina obumanyifu obulungi ku bika by'emmeeri ebiwerako.
entries.english: Be sure the agent through whom you book is a Galapagos specialist with a good knowledge of a wide variety of ships.

entries.luganda: Kino kijja kulaga nti ebikusanyusa n’ebikunyigiliza bikwatagana mungeri yemmeeri ebagwanira ngabo.
entries.english: This will ensure that your particular interests and/or constraints are matched with the ship most suitable to them.

entries.luganda: Nga aba sipanisi tebanatuuka mu kyaasa ekye 16, Chile yomumambuuka yali wansi wo bufuzi bwa Inca ate nga banansi ba Araucaniya (Mapuche) baali babeera mu masekati ne mu mamaserengeta ga Chile.
entries.english: Before the Spanish arrived in the 16th century, northern Chile was under Inca rule while the indigenous Araucanians (Mapuche) inhabited central and southern Chile.

entries.luganda: Ba Mapuche bebamu kunnimi ezamefuga ezaasembayo mu America, tebabulila ddala mu lu Spanish paka nga Chile efunye obwetwaze.
entries.english: The Mapuche were also one of the last independent American indigenous groups, that were not fully absorbed into Spanish-speaking rule until after Chile's independence.

entries.luganda: Waddenga Chile yalangilira amefuga mu 1810 (mumasekati entalo z’aba Nepali ezaleka Spain nga telinakiffo mu bufuzi okumala emyaaka egiwera), obuwanguzi obwenkomeledde tebabufuna paka mu 1818.
entries.english: Although Chile declared independence in 1810 (amid the Napoleonic wars that left Spain without a functioning central government for a couple of years), decisive victory over the Spanish was not achieved until 1818.

entries.luganda: Abapabulikano ba Dominican (Spanish: República Dominicana) nsi ya Caribbean eri mubuvanjuba nga ekitundu kyekizinga kya Hispaniola kyegabana ne Haiti
entries.english: The Dominican Republic (Spanish: República Dominicana) is a Caribbean country that occupies the eastern half of the island of Hispaniola, which it shares with Haiti

entries.luganda: Nga ojeeko embalama z’omusenyu omweeru ne’amaserengeta g’esozzi, ensi eyo gemakka g’ekibuga ky’abungereza mu America, kati ekitundu kya Santo Domingo.
entries.english: Besides white sand beaches and mountain landscapes, the country is home to the oldest European city in the Americas, now part of Santo Domingo.

entries.luganda: Akazinga kasooka kusengwako aba Taínos ne Caribes. Aba Caribes bali boogera olulimi lwa Arawakan era ngalwali lwabantu abatuuka mu 10,000 BCE.
entries.english: The island was first inhabited by the Taínos and Caribes. The Caribes were an Arawakan-speaking people who had arrived around 10,000 BCE.

entries.luganda: Mu myaka mitono nyo nga abalambuzzi okuva e bungereza batuuse, abantu b’e Tainos bakendelela ddala olwa bawambi okuva e spayini.
entries.english: Within a few short years following the arrival of European explorers, the population of Tainos had significantly been reduced by the Spanish conquerors

entries.luganda: Okusinzila Ku Fray Bartolomeo de las Casas (Tratado de las India's) wakati wa1492 be 1498 abawanguzi ba Spanish batta kumpi 100,000 Tainos.
entries.english: Based on Fray Bartolomé de las Casas (Tratado de las Indias) between 1492 and 1498 the Spanish conquerors killed around 100,000 Taínos.

entries.luganda: “Jardin de la Unión. Ekibangirize kino kyazimbibwa mu kyaasa kya 17th, ngamukino Templo de San Diego kyekizimbe kinamunigina ekikyayimiride.
entries.english: Jardín de la Unión. This space was built as the atrium for a 17th-century convent, of which the Templo de San Diego is the sole surviving building.

entries.luganda: Kati ekola nga plaza eyawakati, era bulijjo erina ebintu bingi ebigenda mumaaso emisana n'ekiro.
entries.english: It now functions as the central plaza, and always has a lot of things going on, day and night.

entries.luganda: Waliwo ebiriiro by'emmere okwetoloola ennimiro era mu tuuntu n’akawungeezi waberayo ebivvulu ebyobwerere mu masekati ga gazebo.
entries.english: There are a number of restaurants surrounding the garden, and in the afternoons and evening there free concerts are often given from the central gazebo.

entries.luganda: Callejon del Beso (Alley of the Kiss). Obubalaza bubiri bwayawulwa ne centimeters 69 zoka gem aka g’omuzira womukwano omkade omu.
entries.english: Callejon del Beso (Alley of the Kiss). Two balconies separated by only 69 centimeters is home of an old love legend.

entries.luganda: Ku busente obutono abaana abamu basobola okubuulir aolugero lwonna
entries.english: For a few pennies some children will tell you the story.

entries.luganda: Ekizinga kya Bowen kimanyidwa nyoo okulambulwa munaku oba ku wiikendi okusiyagguka, okuwayo kayaking, okuwalampa ensozi, amaduuka, ebiriiro by’emmere nebirala ntoko.
entries.english: Bowen Island is a popular day trip or weekend excursion offering kayaking, hiking, shops, restaurants, and more.

entries.luganda: Akaalo kano kasangibwa mu Howe Sound nga wa kava e Vancouver, era nga kangu okumutuuka ko nga okozesa takisi z'okumazzi eziva e Granville Island mukibuga kya Vancouver.
entries.english: This authentic community is located in Howe Sound just off Vancouver, and is easily accessed via scheduled water taxis departing Granville Island in downtown Vancouver.

entries.luganda: Abo abanyumirwa emizanyo jje bweru ,okugendako ku nyanja ne mulukuubo lwomubire kyetaagisa.
entries.english: For those who enjoy outdoor activities, a trek up the Sea to Sky corridor is essential.

entries.luganda: Whistler (Essaawa 1.5 nga ovuga okuva e Vancouver) kya beeyi naye lwakuba kimanyikiddwa kulwa emizanyo gya Olimpikisi egyobutiti egya 2010.
entries.english: Whistler (1.5 hour drive from Vancouver) is expensive but well-known because of the 2010 Winter Olympics.

entries.luganda: Mu biseera by'obutiti, nyumirwa okwevugira ku muzira okusinga mu mambuka g’America ate ne mubiseera by'ebbugumu gezaako kukuvuga eggaali ku nsozi okukakasiddwa.
entries.english: In the winter, enjoy some of the best skiing in North America, and in the summer try some authentic mountain biking.

entries.luganda: Kalata ekuwa olukusa erina okubeera nga yaterekeddwa . Olina okubba nga olina kalata eno okusobola okusula e Sirena.
entries.english: Permits must be reserved in advance. You must have a permit to stay overnight at Sirena.

entries.luganda: Sirena kyekifo akuuma paaka eno wasula era n’abagenyi abalala basobola okusula wo nebalya nemmere eyokya. Ebifo bya La Leona, San Pedrillo, nne Los Patos bisuza busuza bantu tebibeera nabyankatika.
entries.english: Sirena is the only ranger station that offers dormitory lodging and hot meals in addition to camping. La Leona, San Pedrillo, and Los Patos offer only camping with no food service.

entries.luganda: Kisoboka okufuna pamiti za paaka okuviira ddala mu bakuuma ebisolo mu Puerto Jiménez, naye tebakkiriza kusasula na kaadi z’esimbi.
entries.english: It is possible to secure park permits directly from the Ranger Station in Puerto Jiménez, but they do not accept credit cards

entries.luganda: Okukola ku paaka (MINAE) tekuwa bisanyizo bya paaka bisusa mwezi gummu mumaaso okutebereza lwotuuka.
entries.english: The Park Service (MINAE) does not issue park permits more than one month in advance of anticipated arrival.

entries.luganda: CafeNet El Sol egaba saviisi yokubookinga ekifo ku muwendo US$30, oba $10 ezikuyisawo olunaku lumu; ebisingawo bisange ku peegi ya Corcovado.
entries.english: CafeNet El Sol offers a reservation service for a fee of US$30, or $10 for one-day passes; details on their Corcovado page.

entries.luganda: Ekizinga bya Cook byansi etalina kakwate ku New Zealand ebisangibwa mu Polynesia mumasekati g’amaserengeta ga ssemayanja Pacific.
entries.english: The Cook Islands are an island country in free association with New Zealand, located in Polynesia, in the middle of the South Pacific Ocean.

entries.luganda: Eno archipelago n’obuzinga 15 nga yeesuulidde obukadde bwa km 2.2 obwa ssemayanja.
entries.english: It is an archipelago with 15 islands spread out over 2.2 million km2 of ocean.

entries.luganda: Nga ebizinga bilina obudde bwebumu nga Hawaii, olusi biyitibwa nti bili wansi was Hawaii
entries.english: With the same time zone as Hawaii, the islands are sometimes thought of as "Hawaii down under".

entries.luganda: Newankubadde nga watonotono, kijukiza abagenyi abakadde mu ngeri Hawaii gyeyalabikamu nga tenafuna woteri enene nenkulakulana enddala
entries.english: Though smaller, it reminds some elderly visitors of Hawaii before statehood without all the large tourist hotels and other development.

entries.luganda: Akazinga kabafumbi tekalina kibuga nakimu naye kalimu obuzinga obwenjawulo 15. Ezisinga ettutumu mwemuli Rarotonga ne Aitutaki.
entries.english: The Cook Islands do not have any cities but are composed of 15 different islands. The main ones are Rarotonga and Aitutaki.

entries.luganda: Mu mawanga agakuzeemu leero, enkola eyokusula n’okuwa eky’enkya kiyitimuse nnyo nga enkola kati.
entries.english: In developed countries today, providing deluxe bed and breakfasts has been raised to a sort of art-form.

entries.luganda: Kuntiko y’enkomerero, B&Bs eteekwa okuwakanya kubintu bibiri ebikulu: okusula n’ekyenkya.
entries.english: At the top end, B&Bs obviously compete mainly on two main things: bedding and breakfast.

entries.luganda: Era, mu bulungi ebintu ebitondedwawo ng'ebyo omuntu abeera alina okunoonya ebyokwebikka ebyomulembe, okugeza nga bulangiti etungiddwa emikono oba ekitanda ekyenkola enkadde.
entries.english: Accordingly, at the finest such establishments one is apt to find the most luxurious bedding, maybe a handmade quilt or an antique bed.

entries.luganda: Ekyenkya kiyinza okubeeramu ebiwoomera embeera y'obudde mu kitundu oba eyatukyazizza byasinga okukenkukamu.
entries.english: Breakfast may include seasonal delights of the region or the host's speciality dish.

entries.luganda: Enzimba eyinza okuba nga ya kizimbe kikadde nga kya byaffaayo nga mulimu ebifaananyi ebikadde, wansi awa siige, n’ekidiba ekiwugirwamu.
entries.english: The setting might be an historic old building with antique furnishings, manicured grounds and a swimming pool.

entries.luganda: Okuyingila mu motoka yo n’ogendda ku lugenddo obwomu kilina obulungi n’obwangu.
entries.english: Getting into your own car and heading off on a long road trip has an intrinsic appeal in its simplicity.

entries.luganda: Obutafaananako motoka neene, wamanyideda okufuga emotoka yo era omanyi n’obusobozi bwayo.
entries.english: Unlike larger vehicles, you are probably already familiar with driving your car and know its limitations.

entries.luganda: Okuteka weema Ku kifo eky'obwananyini oba mu kibuga ekyobunene bwonna kisobola okusikiriza okulabisa okutetagisa.
entries.english: Setting up a tent on private property or in a town of any size can easily attract unwanted attention.

entries.luganda: Mubufunze, okukozesa emotokayo ngeri y’amaanyi okutambulamu naye singer ngayo ey’”okwewumuzaamu”.
entries.english: In short, using your car is a great way to take a road trip but seldom in itself a way to "camp".

entries.luganda: Okwebaka mu motoka kusoboka bwoba nga olina emotoka enene, SUV, sedan oba sitenseni wagon n’emito ejjebaka.
entries.english: Car camping is possible if you have a large minivan, SUV, Sedan or Station Wagon with seats that lay down.

entries.luganda: Wooteri ezisinga zirina obusika bwe zekumidde okuva ku ggaali z'omukka ezenjawulo n'obukuubo bwa gasemayanja: nga sematalo owokubiri tanabaawo, mu byasa kya 19 oba ekya 20 nga kitandika.
entries.english: Some hotels have a heritage from the golden age of steam railways and ocean liners; before the Second World War, in the 19th or early 20th centuries.

entries.luganda: Zino wooteri abaggagga n’abettutumu mwebasula bafumba bulungi atte n’obulamu bw’ekiro bunyuma.
entries.english: These hotels were where the rich and the famous of the day would stay, and often had fine dining and nightlife.

entries.luganda: Enzimba eyedda, obutabeera nabizimbe byamulembe, nno kukadiwa nemirembe byebimu kubyobalabilako.
entries.english: The old fashioned fittings, the lack of the latest amenities, and a certain graceful agedness are also part of their character.

entries.luganda: Newankubadde zitera kuba z’abwannanyini, oluusi z’akyaazanga Abakulembeze b’amawanga nabakungu abalala
entries.english: While they are usually privately owned, they sometimes accommodate visiting heads of state and other dignitaries.

entries.luganda: Omutambuuze nga alina omudidi gwesente ayinza okusalawo lawundi ya fulayiti yensi, emenyedwa mu nokubeera mu woteri zino enyingi.
entries.english: A traveller with piles of money might consider a round the world flight, broken up with stays in many of these hotels.

entries.luganda: hospitality exchange network kyekitongole ekikwataganya abatambuze ne bannakyaalo mu bibuga byebaba bagendamu
entries.english: A hospitality exchange network is the organization that connects travelers with locals in the cities they are going to visit.

entries.luganda: Okweyunga ku mukutu nga guno kyetaagisa kujjuza foomu ku mutimbagano. Newankubadde enyunga ezimu ziwa oba zeetaaga okwongera okukakasa.
entries.english: Joining such a network usually just requires filling out an online form; although some networks offer or require additional verification.

entries.luganda: Olukalala lw’abayise lulagibwa oba nga lukubisidwa oba kumutimbagano, oluusi nga kuliko abatambula webatuukira n’ewebateseza.
entries.english: A listing of available hosts is then provided either in print and/or online, sometimes with references and reviews by other travelers.

entries.luganda: Couchsurfing yasangibwa mu gatonnya 2004 oluvanyuma lw’omutetenkanya wa komputa Casey Fenton okusanga enyonyi eyalayisi ookugenda mu Iceland naye nga talina kifo wakusigala.
entries.english: Couchsurfing was founded in January 2004 after computer programmer Casey Fenton found a cheap flight to Iceland but did not have a place to stay.

entries.luganda: Yawandiikira abayizi bayunivasite ey'omukitundu nafuna ennamba y’abantu bangi abaali bamuwa awokusula aw’obwerere.
entries.english: He emailed students at the local university and received an overwhelming number of offers for free accommodation.

entries.luganda: Ebisulo bisinga kufaayo nyo ku bavubuka- omugenyi wabwe ali mumyaka nga abbiri- naye era osobola okusangayo abatambuze abakulumu.
entries.english: Hostels cater primarily to young people – a typical guest is in their twenties – but you can often find older travellers there too.

entries.luganda: Famire ezirina abaana tezirabika, naye ebisulo ebimu bibakkiriza mu bisenge mwe basula bokka.
entries.english: Families with children are a rare sight, but some hostels do allow them in private rooms.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga kya Beijing e China we wagenda okubeera emizannyo gya Olympic Winter Games mu 2022, ekigenda okukifuula ekibuga ekisose okutekawo emizannyo gyombi egya ebiseera eby’omusana n’obutiti.
entries.english: The city of Beijing in China will be the host city of the Olympic Winter Games in 2022, which will make it the first city to have hosted both the summer and winter Olympics.

entries.luganda: Ekibuga Beijing kigenda kukyaza emizannyo egigulawo n’egy'akamalirizo awamu n’egy'emizannyo gy’omunda.
entries.english: Beijing will host the opening and closing ceremonies and the indoor ice events.

entries.luganda: Emizannyo emirala egy'okwebonga gyakubeera mu kitundu ewabongerwa mu Zhangjiakou, kumpi 220km (mayilo 140) okuva mu Beijing.
entries.english: Other skiing events will be at the Taizicheng ski area in Zhangjiakou, about 220 km (140 miles) from Beijing.

entries.luganda: Amasinzizo agasinga galina ebikujuko buli mwaaka okutandikila mu gw’ekumi nogumu nga gugwaako paka mu Makati ga’ogwokutaano ebyawukana okusinziila ku kalenda y’esinzizo eyabulimwaaka.
entries.english: Most of the temples do have an Annual festival starting from November end to Mid of May, which varies according to each temple's annual calendar.

entries.luganda: Ebikujuko ebisinga mu masinzizo bikuzibwa nga annivasale yesinzizo oba amazaalibwa ga bukatonda obutono oba ekikujuko ekirala ekyamaanyi ekyekuusa ku sinzizo.
entries.english: Most of the temple festivals is celebrated as part of temple's anniversary or presiding deity's birthday or any other major event associated with the temple.

entries.luganda: Omwoleso ogwa yekaalu ya Kerala gusanyusa okulaba, ne bintu bingi ebiba bigobererwa ne njovu ezitimbiddwa, abakubi b'ebivuga bya yekaalu n'ebyokusanyusa ntoko.
entries.english: Kerala's temple festivals are very interesting to see, with regular procession of decorated elephants, temple orchestra and other festivities.

entries.luganda: Olukujjuko lw’ensi (ekimanyikiddwa nga okubwatuka kwensi, obaomwoleso omwangu) mwoleso munene ogwensi yonna ogw’ebisiige ne sayansi.
entries.english: A World's Fair (commonly called World Exposition, or simply Expo) is large international festival of arts and sciences.

entries.luganda: Ensi ezetabyemu ziraga ebisiige nebyenjigiriza mu biffo byegwanga awalabirwa ensonga zensi yona oba ebyobuwangwa n’ebyafaayo byensi zabwe.
entries.english: Participating countries present artistic and educational displays in national pavilions to showcase world issues or their country's culture and history.

entries.luganda: Emyooleso egya etabbi elyekuusa ku Bimuli mu nsi yonna gyibeera mikolo mwebayolesezza ebimuli, e’nnimiro z’ebimuli n’ebintu ebirala ebikwata ku birime.
entries.english: International Horticultural Expositions are specialised events which showcase floral displays, botanical gardens and anything else to do with plants.

entries.luganda: Lwakuba mubiwaandiiko zibelawo buli mwaka (kasita zibeera munsi ezenjawulo), mu byaddala sikituufu.
entries.english: Although in theory they can take place annually (so long as they are in different countries), in practice they are not.

entries.luganda: Ebintu bino bitera okumala wakati we myeezi esaatu ku mukaaga, era zibeera mu bifo ebissussa eeka ataano (50)
entries.english: These events normally last anywhere between three and six months, and are held on sites no smaller than 50 hectares.

entries.luganda: Waliiwo engeri nnyingi eza firimu ezikozesebwa okumala emyaka. Okupimamu ekifananyi kya 35mm (36 ku 24 mm ezekifananyi) yesiinga okukozesebwa.
entries.english: There are many different film formats that have been used over the years. Standard 35 mm film (36 by 24 mm negative) is much the commonest.

entries.luganda: Kisoboka okuzibwaawo mpola mpola mubwangu singa tebigwaawo, ate nosobola okusalawo kumbeera eriwo eya DSLR.
entries.english: It can usually be replenished fairly easily if you run out, and gives resolution roughly comparable to a current DSLR.

entries.luganda: Kamera ezimu ezitali zamayi nyo zikozesa enkola ya 6 ku 6 cm okusimba esila eya 56 ku 56 mm negatiivu.
entries.english: Some medium-format film cameras use a 6 by 6 cm format, more precisely a 56 by 56 mm negative.

entries.luganda: Kino kiwa endabika esinga eya 35mm negative emirundi (3136 mm2 ebisanya 864).
entries.english: This gives resolution almost four times that of a 35 mm negative (3136 mm2 versus 864).

entries.luganda: Ebisolo by’omunsiko bye bikyasiinze okutawanya omukubi w’ebifaananyi, yetaaga okugatta omukisa, obugumikiriza, obumanyifu n’ebyuuma ebirungi.
entries.english: Wildlife is among the most challenging motifs for a photographer, and needs a combination of good luck, patience, experience and good equipment.

entries.luganda: Ebifaananyi byensolo zomukibira tebatera bilabamu makulu, naye era ngebyebifaananyi ebirala, ekifaananyi kyogerera ddala ngebigambo lukumi.
entries.english: Wildlife photography is often taken for granted, but like photography in general, a picture is worth a thousand words.

entries.luganda: Okukuba ebifaananyi mu ddundiro ly’ebisolo kyetaagisa ebirawuli ebirengerera ewala, wadde nga ebintu nga ebinyonyi ebiri awamu oba obutonde obutono byetaaga birawuli birala.
entries.english: Wildlife photography often requires a long telephoto lens, though things like a flock of birds or a tiny creature need other lenses.

entries.luganda: Ebisolo ebisinga eby'abazungu bizibu okufuna, era ne mw’ebikumirwa ebisera ebisinga balina amateeka olw’okubikuba bifaananyi olw’ensonga za ssente
entries.english: Many exotic animals are hard to find, and parks sometimes have rules about taking photographs for commercial purposes.

entries.luganda: Ebisolo by'omunsiko biyinza okuba nga bya nsonyi oba nga bikambwe. Obudde buyinza okuba nga bunyogovu, bwookya oba ekirala nga bukaawu.
entries.english: Wild animals might either be shy or aggressive. The environment might be cold, hot, or otherwise hostile.

entries.luganda: Ensi erina kumpi enimi ez’enjawulo 5,000, nga ogaseeko aboogezi baazo abawereradala obukadde 50 nomusobyo.
entries.english: The world has over 5,000 different languages, including more than twenty with 50 million or more speakers.

entries.luganda: Ebigambo ebiwandiike bulijjo byangu okuteegera okusinga ebigambo ebyogerwa. Neera.Kino kituufu nnyo ku ndagiriro, ezimu ngebiseera ebisinga nzibu okwatuukiriza namagezi.
entries.english: Written words are often easier to understand than spoken words, also. This is especially true of addresses, which are often difficult to pronounce intelligibly.

entries.luganda: Amawanga amalala mangi gakugu nyo mu lungereza, era nga mu bungi nekukyosuubira osangayo okumanya kutono – naddala mumasekati gabavubuka abato.
entries.english: Many entire nations are completely fluent in English, and in even more you can expect a limited knowledge - especially among younger people.

entries.luganda: Tebeereza, woba osobodde, nga omu Mancunia, omu Jamaica, n’omu Sydney nga batudde ku kameeza balya kyagulo ewaliibwa awamu mu kibuga kya Toronto.
entries.english: Imagine, if you will, a Mancunian, Bostonian, Jamaican and Sydneysider sitting around a table having dinner at a restaurant in Toronto.

entries.luganda: Benyumiza emboozi okuva mu bubuga bwomubyalo byabwe, nga boogera mu njatula ezesudde ate ezobuzaaliiranwa.
entries.english: They're regaling each other with stories from their hometowns, told in their distinct accents and local argot.

entries.luganda: Okugula emmere mu zi semaduuka yengeri yokka enyangu ate eyalayisi okuliisibwa. Woba tosobola kufumba, orina kulya mmere ebayafumbidwa edda.
entries.english: Buying food in supermarkets is usually the cheapest way to get fed. Without cooking opportunities, choices are however limited to ready-made food.

entries.luganda: Okweeyongera amaduuka amanene goongera okufuna ebifo bye mmere enfumbe. Ezimu zilina n’akuuma akubugumya oba enegeri yookubugumya emmere.
entries.english: Increasingly supermarkets are getting a more varied section of ready-made food. Some even provide a microwave oven or other means to heat food.

entries.luganda: Mu nsi ezimu, ne mu maduka amalala, mubelamu ekifo kyokulilamu emere, nga kilina emiwendo gyemele emisamu samu.
entries.english: In some countries or types of stores there is at least one on-site restaurant, often a rather informal one with affordable prices.

entries.luganda: Kola era otambulenga ne koppi yenkola nebikwata Ku ndagiliro ya yinsuwa yo.
entries.english: Make and carry copies of your policy and your insurer's contact details with you.

entries.luganda: Betaaga okulaga ymayiro yaba yinsuwa nenamba yebweru okufuna amagezi n’okukirizibwa okusaayo okubanja kwabwe.
entries.english: They need to show the insurer's e-mail address and international phone numbers for advice/authorizations and making claims.

entries.luganda: Funayo kopi endala mu muguggu gwo ne kumutimbagano (wewereze e-mail nga otadeko ekikwate, oba obitereke “mubire”).
entries.english: Have another copy in your luggage and online (e-mail to yourself with attachment, or stored in the "cloud").

entries.luganda: Bwoba otambula ne laaputoopu oba tabuleeti, tereka ebintu ebijiriko ku memole oba disiki (Woosobola okubijja nga tolina mutimbagano)
entries.english: If traveling with a laptop or tablet, store a copy in its memory or disc (accessible without the internet).

entries.luganda: Nabo bawe amateeka eri famire ne mikwano engerigyeba genda abanaba bagala okudda bagya kuyamba.
entries.english: Also give policy/contact copies to traveling companions and relatives or friends back home willing to help.

entries.luganda: Enfulege (zimanyikiddwa nga Enfulege) tezirina buzibu naye zijja kwelwanako singa zesanga mu mbeera etiisatiisa.
entries.english: Moose (also known as elk) aren't inherently aggressive, but will defend themselves if they perceive a threat.

entries.luganda: Abantu bwe batakiraba nga kya bulabe, bayinza okukisemberera ennyo n’ebateeka obulamu bwabwe mu katyabaga.
entries.english: When people don't see moose as potentially dangerous, they may approach too closely and put themselves at risk.

entries.luganda: Nywa eby’okunywa ebitamiiza nga webalirira. Omwenge buli omu gumuyisa bubwe era okumanya wokoma kikulu nyoo.
entries.english: Drink alcoholic drinks with moderation. Alcohol affects everyone differently, and knowing your limit is very important.

entries.luganda: Emikolo egisoboka egy’ebyobulamu egimala akabanga ogiva mu kunywa enyo gisobola okubaamu okukosa ekibumba n’okuziba amaaso n’okuffa.Obuzibu obusobola okuvaamu bweyongera nga okozeseza omwenge ogukoledw mu bukyaamu.
